strengths.463:
- Sochan, a 19-year-old freshman forward, has risen in draft rankings due to his rare physical attributes, length, build, agility, and verticality.  
- He demonstrates versatility by being able to cover multiple positions and serves as a strong defensive asset, particularly with long arms for rim protection and high steal rates (1.3 per game).  
- Improved offensively in his final eight games, averaging 12.25 points with two instances of 17 points, and showing consistency and self-creation despite limited playing time.  
- A strong rebounder for his position, grabbing 9.1 rebounds per 36 minutes, with high energy on the glass and in transition.  
- Shows promise as a future high-level scorer with potential development in three-point shooting and a strong feel for the game, including nearly two assists per game.
strengths.464:
- Tyler is a highly touted prep big man with a physical presence resembling an NBA player at 6'10" and 260 pounds.  
- He demonstrates excellent coordination, fluidity, and strong footwork for his size.  
- Features a massive 7'5" wingspan and big hands, enabling him to play above his height.  
- Shows a raw but promising post game with multiple scoring moves, counters, and consistent range up to 18 feet.  
- Possesses strong rebounding ability and can operate effectively in both the high and low post.
strengths.465:
- Jerian Grant is a 6'5", 205-pound point guard with elite athleticism, strength, and lateral speed, making him a strong NBA translation candidate.  
- He made 73.1% of his shots at the rim as a senior and posted a 6.7 assist per game average with only 2.2 turnovers (3.04 assist/to ratio), showcasing excellent ball handling and team play.  
- A reliable shooter from the dribble and spot-up, with a 78% free throw percentage and 82% foul shot clip, and strong shot form and composure.  
- Defensively, he ranks 6th in the ACC in steals per game, excels in passing lanes, and uses his length to challenge smaller guards and disrupt offensive plays.  
- A clutch performer with big-game experience, including a game-winning step-back three in the NCAA tournament, and demonstrates strong versatility, isolation ability, and defensive fundamentals.
strengths.467:
- Strong outside shooting ability with good range, high release, and balanced stance  
- Excels coming off screens, reads defensive action well, and consistently gets open  
- Comfortable with pull-up shots in both directions, uses shotfakes to create space  
- Poise, confidence, and effectiveness in clutch situations; strong on break and transition plays  
- Athletic build with wide shoulders and chiselled frame, providing advantages on the glass and rebounding
strengths.468:
- Averaged 15 points and 9 rebounds per game as a senior, with over 2 blocks per game  
- Possesses an ideal NBA center frame with a 7-foot-6 wingspan and 7-plus-foot stature  
- Demonstrates strong agility for his size and solid leaping ability, with effective post moves and drop steps  
- Shows excellent shooting form, particularly from mid-range and free throws (68% as a senior, with room for improvement)  
- Consistently performs at a high level, scoring in double digits in all but 4 games, and outperformed Hassan Whiteside in a key matchup with 21 points and 12 rebounds
strengths.469:
- Excellent ball handler with a clean release and polished shot, known for strong ball skills and creativity  
- One of the draft’s top shooters, hitting 40% from three-point range and 83% from the free-throw line  
- Highly effective off the dribble, using pump fakes, change of momentum, and crossovers to create open shots  
- Strong spot-up shooter with great synergy off Ky Bowman, excelling in catch-and-shoot situations and step-back moves  
- Aggressive on the break, skilled at attacking the rim, drawing fouls, and making thunderous dunks when open
strengths.470:
- Physically gifted swingman with 6'8", 215 lbs frame, well-suited for wing play at the professional level  
- Efficient perimeter shooter with 35% 3FG% and 7.3 attempts per game; high and compact release, confident range and touch  
- Moves well without the ball, dangerous on screens and handoffs; uses stepback and jab step moves to create space  
- Effective mid-range shooter, excels in pull-ups and runners; shows solid court vision and patience in decision-making  
- Strong pick-and-roll player who prefers to shoot, with flashes of post ability and 80% free throw shooting as a freshman
strengths.471:
- Two-time Big 12 Defensive Player of the Year with a fearless, tough style of play and a 6’4.5” wingspan despite being 6’1.5” in shoes.  
- Ranked 17th all-time in NCAA steals with 330 career steals, known for strong hands, quick reflexes, and high ball-stripping ability.  
- Averaged 4.3 rebounds per game and excels at ball-handling off misses due to Bob Huggins’ fast-paced system.  
- Effective combo guard with 6.6 assists per game (3rd in the Big 12), low turnover rate (2.53 A/TO), and strong ability to penetrate and kick out to open shooters.  
- Consistent 3-point shooter (39% over last two seasons, 86% from the free-throw line) and skilled at finishing around the basket with floaters, flip shots, and body control.
strengths.472:
- Solid athlete with great quickness and length  
- Extremely active on both ends of the court, excelling in transition and defensive play  
- Effective offensively with strong body control, rim finish, and good instincts for cutting  
- Confident mid-range shooter with a developed pull-up jumpshot and 78.3% free throw accuracy  
- Unselfish, efficient passer, and impactful on offensive rebounds and second-chance opportunities
strengths.473:
- 6’10” with a 7’3” wingspan and quick-twitch athleticism, jumps off the film  
- Defensive tone-setter with elite rim protection, over 2 blocks per 36 minutes, and strong instincts as a help-side shot blocker  
- Highly mobile for his size, capable of sliding, rotating, and recovering in space; defends guards, in drop, and hedges screens  
- Offensive role player who sets firm screens, dives hard, and finishes quickly on pocket passes and lobs; vertical lob threat without dribbling  
- Exceptional transition player—first big in transition, explosive speed, and dangerous finisher who catches defenses off guard
strengths.474:
- Joan Tomas is a promising European prospect born in 1992.  
- He may play continuously in the ACB League with DKV Joventut Badalona next season.  
- Despite his youth, he demonstrates great court maturity.  
- He is aggressive, with an excellent first step and strong rim attacks, especially to his right.  
- His length makes him a strong rebounder and defender.
strengths.475:
- Averages 28 points per game on 17 shots per game with efficient shooting, including a.415 three-point percentage and 1.52 points per possession from spot-up shooting.  
- Excels at reading defenses, using screens, and creating open shots through quick cuts, speed, and strong body control as a shooting guard.  
- Possesses a consistent, high-quality jump shot with excellent form, good elevation, and a range beyond the NBA average.  
- Creates scoring opportunities through off-the-dribble moves, step-backs, pump fakes, and backdoor cuts, with a notable increase in scoring from 8.8 to 25 ppg.  
- Strong all-around game: reliable free-throw shooter (.89 ft%), effective passer, good defensive presence, and consistent offensive impact in every game.
strengths.476:
- Finished college career second all-time at Virginia in 3-point percentage (40.7%) and 3-pointers (263).  
- Excels as a catch-and-shoot shooter, using screens and misdirection to create open looks.  
- Effective at creating space with ball fakes, step-backs, crossovers, and hesitations; averages 3.2 assists per 40 minutes as a senior.  
- Tough, durable defender with strong awareness, anticipation, and ability to avoid being picked off; played through a hand injury.  
- Intelligent, unselfish, and efficient player with strong intangibles including leadership, awareness, and free-throw shooting.
strengths.477:
- Wieskamp is a high-level three-point shooter with a 46.2% success rate on 158 attempts and a 60.2% effective field goal percentage, outperforming Luka Garza.  
- He possesses a beautiful stroke, quick release, and versatility, hitting shots off the dribble or via assists, with strong performance off screens and through contact.  
- Wieskamp has deep range, is a multi-dimensional offensive threat (capable of attacking the rim and converting 60% of his close-range attempts), and is a solid rebounder (averaging over five defensive rebounds and one offensive rebound per game).  
- He has NBA-level athleticism, evidenced by a 42-inch vertical leap (tied for fifth at the NBA Combine), 10.7-second lane agility (fourth), and a 3.04 three-quarter sprint (tied for third among forwards).  
- With a 6'7", 205-pound frame and 6'11" wingspan, Wieskamp is a sturdy defender who guards multiple positions and rarely allows uncontested shots.
strengths.478:
- Long, athletic post player with a 7'1.75 wingspan and strong rebounding ability (12.7 rebounds per game, 3rd in the country)  
- Explosive leaper with a strong, developed hook shot and solid consistency in shooting  
- Effective mid-range shooter with a 36% three-point percentage over two seasons (low volume: 36-of-96)  
- Strong finisher at the rim, draws fouls frequently, and shoots over 70% from the free-throw line  
- Shows aggressive motor, steady offensive improvement (from 7 ppg as a freshman to 17.1 ppg as a senior), and solid pick-and-pop potential
strengths.479:
- Embiid has a prototypical NBA center frame: 7' tall, 7.5' reach, 250 lbs, with strong potential for additional muscle.  
- Posted outstanding per-40-minute stats at Kansas: 19.4 points, 14 rebounds, 4.5 blocks, with 76.3% field goal success at the rim.  
- Highly agile and mobile for a center, with quickness, speed, vertical explosiveness, and excellent body control and lateral movement.  
- Offensive game is developing with tools like drop-steps, jump hooks, turn-around jumpers, and the "Dream Shake," plus solid free-throw shooting (69%) and foul draw (8.8 per 40 minutes).  
- Aggressive, active defender with strong length, disruptive shot alteration, and confidence in setting screens and handling double teams.
strengths.480:
- Standing 6’11”, Lauvergne has exceptional mobility and speed for European standards.  
- His speed enables effective play in secondary transition, often finishing with dunks.  
- He is active and mobile in half-court offense, demonstrating strong offensive rebound instincts.  
- His game is primarily driven by motor skills and mobility, with room for technical improvement.  
- On defense, his mobility allows him to contest shots and guard smaller opponents effectively in one-on-one situations.
strengths.481:
- Active and energetic big man with exceptional length and skill around the basket  
- High rebounding ability, consistently grabs offensive and defensive rebounds with strong positioning  
- Explosive leaper with a quick second jumper and strong post presence; scores efficiently at the rim (62.1 FG%)  
- Mobile for a big man with good footwork, effective in the post, and capable of creating opportunities in transition  
- Strong defensive presence with shot-blocking ability, excellent help defense, and quick recovery and rotation
strengths.482:
- 6’10” power forward with a 7’4” wingspan and 9’3.5” standing reach, making him a dominant shot blocker and defensive presence  
- Averages 3.2 blocks per game with exceptional reactive leaping and the ability to block with either hand  
- Exceptional defensive range, covers large areas of the court, and effectively denies post entry feeds and pick-and-roll opportunities  
- Strong rebounder (10.1 rpg), especially on the offensive glass (3.2 off), and a key contributor in transition with alley-oop and dunk opportunities  
- Athletic, explosive, and aggressive player with improving scoring versatility, soft hands, and a developing low-post game and jumper (up to 18 ft)
strengths.483:
- Jenkins is a pure jump shooter with elite range, capable of hitting shots from deep and consistently scoring from the perimeter.  
- He possesses a fundamental, textbook shooting motion with a quick release, maintaining proper foot and shoulder alignment on every shot.  
- His game includes effective head fakes, hesitation moves, and shiftiness—both with and without the ball—to draw defenders off balance and create scoring opportunities.  
- A highly efficient scorer, he shoots 43% from three-point range with 6 attempts per game and shows excellent touch around the rim and strong defensive effort.  
- Known for his solid decision-making, low turnover rate, and work ethic, Jenkins is a fundamentally sound, all-around wing player who excels in high-pressure situations.
strengths.484:
- Possesses elite speed, athleticism, and explosiveness with exceptional first-step ability and body control.  
- Excels in offensive playmaking, making quick, creative passes and finding teammates even under pressure.  
- A fearless finisher around the rim with both hands, capable of scoring in transition and in traffic.  
- Combines elite defensive skills with strong rebounding and steals, using long arms and quick hands to disrupt opponents.  
- Shows leadership, vision, and potential to become a dominant two-way player and a major NBA draw.
strengths.485:
- Averaged 18.6 points, 10.8 rebounds, and 2.1 blocks per game in 30 minutes as a senior, showcasing consistency and maturity as Auburn’s interior anchor.  
- Elite post scorer using hooks, floaters, and short jumpers with advanced footwork and soft hands, capable of finishing through contact or creating space with fakes and pivots.  
- Led the SEC in rebounding (total, offensive, and defensive), averaging 3.7 offensive rebounds and nearly 4 per game, with strong hands and relentless glass pressure.  
- Disciplined defensive presence, posting over 2.0 blocks per game across college seasons, excelling in shot blocking and drop coverage without fouling.  
- Mature, poise-driven leader with improved face-up ability, passing awareness, and a clear understanding of his role, making him one of the SEC’s top two-way players.
strengths.486:
- Averaged 21 points per game as a sophomore after scoring only 7 as a freshman, showing significant improvement and breakout potential.  
- Strong all-around skillset with effective driving, mid-range shooting (44% from 2-point range), and post moves, including a reliable turnaround jumper.  
- Excels in transition, creating fouls and using body control to generate driving lanes; consistently effective in pick-and-roll and dribble handoff situations.  
- Elite rebounder (8.2 rpg) and defensive presence with 1.6 steals per game, strong lateral movement, and ability to disrupt offenses and block shots (nearly one per game).  
- High basketball IQ, aggressive mindset, and leadership; shows potential to thrive as a 3-and-D guard or primary shot creator in the NBA.
strengths.487:
- Athletic, tough, and versatile wing with the ability to score in multiple ways, including from beyond the 3-point arc  
- Over half of his field goal attempts in freshman season came from three-point range, with a 35% conversion rate  
- Effective even when not featured on offense; excels in going after offensive rebounds and playing tough on the boards  
- Possesses a smooth, consistent shooting stroke and is a quality free throw shooter (77%), with potential to improve to 80%+  
- Strong around the hoop with soft touch and crafty layups; has good size and skill set as a modern NBA wing, and is one of the younger prospects in this year’s draft
strengths.488:
- 6-foot-9, 257-pound power forward with a solid build and agility for an interior player  
- Versatile scorer who can drive, shoot in the post, or hit midrange shots with a difficult fade-away  
- Strong shooting percentages: 69% at the rim and 40% from two-point range  
- Effective post player with strong hands, ability to draw fouls (7.3 FTA per 40 minutes), and resistance to contact  
- Solid rebounder, good screen setter, coachable, and shows consistent physical and mental growth throughout his LSU career
strengths.489:
- Demonstrates relentless hustle and heart, earning respect through consistent effort and being the first to the floor.  
- Possesses a strong, low-center-of-gravity build (67", 245 lbs) that enables effective rebounding and physicality.  
- Excels in rebounding, especially on the offensive glass, with strong anticipation and ability to reach high-percentage spots.  
- Shows excellent court awareness, using his wide frame to find gaps, slip to the basket when double-teamed, and create easy scoring opportunities.  
- Provides solid post defense, runs the floor efficiently, and capitalizes on fast breaks with lob finishes or tip-dunks.
strengths.490:
- One of the best three-point shooters in NCAA history, hitting over 50% as a senior  
- Exceptional range and impeccable jumpshot mechanics, allowing him to shoot over most defenders  
- High basketball IQ with strong ability to find open teammates and create scoring opportunities  
- Effective use of pump fakes and ball movement to create space and draw defenders  
- Efficient, low-turnover scorer with excellent shot selection and surprising leaping ability
strengths.491:
- Averaged 15 points in his 3rd season at Wisconsin, showcasing a solid offensive performance.  
- At 6’10”, Leuer creates matchup problems with his size, outside shooting, and ability to drive to the basket.  
- Possesses excellent footwork and a variety of post moves, making him unpredictable when back to the basket.  
- Shows strong post touch and comfort shooting in any direction, with a 39% shooting percentage from 21 feet or beyond.  
- Efficient offensively (52% field goal percentage, one turnover per game) and defensively smart, with only 2.1 fouls per game.
strengths.492:
- Versatile frontcourt player capable of playing all positions with strong size, length, and a 7-3 wingspan  
- Smooth athlete with excellent jumping ability, light on his feet, and strong court vision  
- Effective ball handler for a big, capable of starting fast breaks and executing coast-to-coast plays  
- Strong perimeter shooter with a reliable stroke, solid mid-range game, and excellent pick-and-pop skills  
- Defensive standout with active hands, strong shot blocking, lateral quickness, and ability to switch and cover ground effectively
strengths.493:
- Has the size and athleticism to contribute effectively both inside and outside the game.  
- Improved outside shooting, able to comfortably hit NBA three-pointers from deep.  
- Demonstrates strong athleticism with finishes around and above the rim.  
- Uses strength to compete with bigger players inside and scores effectively with back-to-the-basket moves.  
- Capable of converting on various plays, including running hooks, spin moves, and a mini fadeaway.
strengths.494:
- Long, agile bigman with a 6'11" frame and exceptional wingspan, known for fluid movement and strong rebounding instincts  
- Extremely aggressive and active rebounder, consistently grabbing and tipping out-of-area rebounds to teammates  
- Solid shot blocker with length to contest entry passes and strong defensive presence; competes hard on both ends  
- Efficient shooter (70.8% field goal percentage in Euroleague), with soft touch, good mechanics, and a developing hook shot  
- High basketball IQ, strong court vision, and competitive drive; shows genuine love for the game and significant growth potential
strengths.495:
- 6’10” with a 9’0.5” standing reach, combining size, length, and fluidity ideal for a versatile wing or forward role  
- Exceptional foot speed, quickness, and agility allow him to cut, drive, and change directions effectively, with strong first-step and rip-through moves  
- Strong scoring ability both inside and outside, with a reliable jumper, ability to pull up, and effective fadeaways and step-backs  
- Solid ball-handling, shot creation, and court vision; can create his own shot with hesitations, crossovers, and step-backs  
- High defensive potential as a switchable, agile defender with good lateral movement, rim protection, and rebounding ability; a late bloomer with immense long-term upside
strengths.496:
- Physical presence and athleticism at 6'7", 220 pounds with exceptional strength, explosiveness, and body control  
- Strong defensive capabilities, excelling in disrupting opposing wings and forwards, capable of defending positions 1–4  
- Versatile offensive game with effective dribbling, post scoring, shooting (including from 3-point range), and playmaking  
- Elite transition player due to speed, hustle, and ability to finish in traffic, leading to easy dunks  
- High-level skill development, improved instincts, and confidence, making him a well-rounded, NBA-ready prospect
strengths.497:
- 6'7", 220 lbs power forward with a 7'2" wingspan, enabling him to play above his height with exceptional leaping and mobility  
- Scores primarily near the basket, converting plays with strong second-jump ability and a high dunk rate (14.2 PPG on 63% FG)  
- Active offensive presence with 10.2 RPG, strong ball-nose, and effective offensive rebounding (3 OPRG over two seasons)  
- Excels in transition, initiates fast breaks, and demonstrates above-average ball-handling and change-of-direction skills  
- Versatile defender with strong ball denial (1 BPG, 1.4 SPG), 28 blocks this season, and hustle that supports a role as a defensive "junkyard dog"
strengths.498:
- Quick point guard with exceptional dribbling skills, including crossover, in-and-out, and hesitation moves  
- Excellent leaper and directional change ability, especially on breaks and in transition  
- Strong mid-range game with pull-ups, floater, and consistent shot form, including NBA-range shooting  
- Effective pick-and-roll player who excels at attacking switches and finishing around the basket  
- Intelligent, competitive, and aggressive on defense with strong lateral quickness and pressure on ball handlers
strengths.499:
- Strong scoring ability with a high desire to win and a competitive drive to score at all times.  
- Excels on defense by anticipating passes, jumping into fast breaks, and crashing the boards aggressively.  
- Dominates in the paint with big-body finishes, effective cuts, and back cuts to create easy layups.  
- Masterful at mid-range shooting, reminiscent of Rip Hamilton, and skilled at reading screens to execute jump shots.  
- Uses strong upper body to "jam" defenders and execute floaters or post moves, with good post skills for a guard.
strengths.500:
- Undersized big man with explosive athleticism, unbridled energy, and strong instincts as a shot blocker with excellent timing and space coverage.  
- Excels in rebounding, quickly gets off the floor, and is a threat to score on offensive rebounds with strong finishes from two feet.  
- Improved significantly in body, skills, and work ethic at Oregon, evolving from a rotation player to a top-tier Pac-12 big man.  
- Strong defensive presence: covers extensive space, defends perimeter effectively, blocks shots from the weak side, and shows high steal percentage with disciplined foul management.  
- Confident, self-aware, and team-oriented, with solid hands, passing ability, and a high-energy, consistent performance that supports teammates and wins on the floor.
strengths.501:
- Elite athleticism with a 42.5-inch vertical jump and exceptional speed in lane agility, shuttle runs, and 3/4 court runs  
- Efficient 3-point shooter (35.3% over three seasons) with good shooting touch from multiple spots on the floor  
- Improved scoring ability as a junior (13.5 points per game) and solid shot selection with strong team awareness  
- Unselfish play: 5.8 assists per game, near 3/1 assist-to-turnover ratio, and effective passing to teammates  
- Strong defensive work ethic, good body control in the air, reliable free throw shooting (83.5% last season), and high energy on the court
strengths.502:
- Displays strong point guard ability with solid vision, handle, and lateral quickness  
- Can post up smaller players and score efficiently using quickness and dribble moves  
- Versatile scorer from all three levels, with effective midrange and spot-up shooting  
- Strong defensive potential due to size, length, and ability to guard multiple positions  
- Excellent body control, rebounding, and ability to create opportunities around the basket
strengths.503:
- Excels as a scorer with a strong first step and excellent elevation on his jumper.  
- Highly effective in isolation, capable of shooting with hands in his face with minimal space.  
- Efficient midrange and three-point shooting, both off the dribble and from spot-up positions.  
- Demonstrates relentless drive and confidence when advancing to the basket, regardless of lane availability.  
- Strong ball handling, defensive anticipation, and mobility, with potential to be a reliable on-ball defender.
strengths.504:
- Confident wing player with a strong frame and excellent scoring ability  
- Versatile scorer who can score in multiple ways, including post play using strength and size (6’8.5" in shoes, 6’9.5" wingspan)  
- Effective spot-up shooter with high, quick release, strong shooting mechanics, and range extending beyond the NBA three-point line (25–28 feet)  
- Volume shooter, averaging 2.5 threes per game during sophomore season (38.5%) and capable of shooting without much space  
- Strong rebounder (7.7 rebounds per game), solid passer, good open-court speed, and effective in fast-break finishes
strengths.505:
- Slender, 6’5” prospect with 185 lbs, strong athletic ability and excellent jumping skills  
- Elite three-point shooter with a near 38% 3FG% and consistent ability to hit from deep, including in transition and off screens  
- Clean, consistent shooting mechanics with feathery touch and a hairpin trigger release  
- Exceptional off-ball movement, quick footwork, and ability to drive to the rim when guarded tightly  
- Strong defensive presence with 17 blocks as a sophomore, good shot contesting, and reliable free throw shooting (88%)
strengths.507:
- Stands 6’5 with a 7’0 wingspan, using his length effectively on both offense and defense  
- Possesses solid leaping ability, fluidity, and a quick, consistent release with an unconventional shot motion  
- Effective mid-range shooter with good footwork, ability to create space, and success in contested jumpers  
- Can score in bunches, finish at the rim on drives, and make shots off the dribble with strong movement and vision  
- Strong defensive potential, capable of guarding multiple perimeter spots, excelling in shot-blocking and on-ball pressure
strengths.508:
- Elite shot blocker, averaging over 3 blocks per game at the college level  
- Extremely athletic with great timing and length (7'2 wingspan) to compensate for below-average height (6'7.5)  
- Improving offensive player with a strong face-up game and added range on his jumper  
- Aggressive on both ends of the floor, becoming one of the better rebounders in college basketball  
- Smart, self-aware player with high basketball IQ, active hands, and a strong work ethic to develop his game
strengths.509:
- Tall, versatile guard excels in attacking the basket on either side of the court  
- Strong positional rebounder, averaging over 6 rebounds per game as a small-ball four  
- Shifty ball-handler who finishes efficiently through contact, shooting 78% from the free-throw line (3.5 FTA per game)  
- Defends 1–3 and has experience guarding wing players, with potential to adapt with size and athleticism  
- Reliable secondary ball-handler with near 3 assists per game, excellent ball security (under 2 turnovers per game), and strong playmaking ability
strengths.510:
- 6’7”, 215-pound forward with effortless scoring and a high IQ, playing a controlled, cerebral game  
- Strong catch-and-shoot ability with a quick, high release and consistent footwork when catching the ball  
- Excels at navigating to the rim and finishing effectively, with above-average ball-handling skills  
- Versatile scorer who uses height and length to shoot over smaller defenders and averages 7.6 rebounds per game  
- Sneaky athlete with surprising athleticism, strong defensive awareness, and high character
strengths.511:
- Exceptional long-range shooter with unlimited range and comfort spotting up or shooting off the dribble  
- Strong ball handler capable of creating for himself and teammates, with solid combo play  
- Reliable finisher around the rim, able to score with either hand  
- Versatile offensive tool with a variety of moves and the ability to operate off screens or pull up  
- Solid perimeter defender with a 6'6.75 wingspan, confidence in taking big shots, and youth advantage from turning 20 just before the 2019 NBA Draft
strengths.512:
- Physically gifted 6'7" wing with 7'2" wingspan, excellent length, athleticism, and motor; strong leaper and above-the-rim finisher.  
- Versatile defensive player with active hands, high competitiveness, and ability to guard PG-PF; excels in perimeter defense, ball disruption, and shot contesting.  
- Effective offensive contributor with a natural, aggressive scoring style, strong court vision, and ability to create opportunities; scores in the paint, draws fouls, and hits threes.  
- High-level athlete (41" vertical), strong rebounder (7.4 RPG, 2.7 offensive), and reliable free throw shooter (90% FT%) with excellent offensive rebounding and tip-out ability.  
- Unselfish, vocal, and intense teammate who consistently hustles, sacrifices for possessions, and contributes in multiple ways beyond traditional stats.
strengths.513:
- Broad shoulders and burly build have potential to develop into a vintage power forward.  
- Significantly improved conditioning since arriving on campus, though further development is needed.  
- Demonstrates strong work ethic and hustle, especially on fast breaks and rebounding.  
- Excels in the paint with excellent hand control, board dominance, and a soft touch on shots.  
- Shows growing instincts in passing out of double teams and adaptability in a flex offense system.
strengths.514:
- Top NCAA scorer with strong offensive versatility, excelling as a jump shooter from all three levels  
- Effective in both pick-and-roll and spot-up shooting scenarios  
- Improves efficiency near the rim and demonstrates aggressive rebounding and intensity  
- Developed into a strong playmaker at point guard, with a 1.58:1 assist-to-turnover ratio and smart decision-making  
- Consistent free-throw shooter (90–92.5%) and a reliable finisher when cutting to the basket
strengths.515:
- Elite scoring guard with 19.9–21.4 PPG in Nike EYBL, strong transition and half-court scoring, and ability to score in bunches when needed  
- Excellent ball-handling, speed, and first step; creates space with creative moves and aggressive rim attacks  
- Improved three-point shooting (12-25 in final games) with potential to shoot much better; complements rim attacks with mid-range pull-ups  
- High free-throw percentage (80% as a freshman) and draws fouls frequently, contributing significantly in the paint  
- Versatile defender with strong pressure, 1.5 steals per game at ASU, and ability to disrupt offense through steals and transition dunks
strengths.516:
- Natural talent with great size and high basketball IQ  
- Elite passer with exceptional court vision and ability to see angles and passes  
- All-around player who can do a little bit of everything and consistently fills up the stat sheet  
- Triple-double threat who excels in pick and roll actions, finding open teammates or cutters  
- Smart, controlled playmaker who is ambidextrous, efficient at the rim, and effective defensively
strengths.517:
- Elite defender with 1.6 steals per game, aided by a freakish 6’10 wingspan and exceptional length  
- Versatile guard who can play point guard, shooting guard, or wing, excelling as a passer, facilitator, and three-point shooter  
- Strong all-around player with excellent ball-handling, vision, and composure, capable of taking over the PG role when needed  
- High-level athlete with quickness, agility, and body control, making plays above the rim and thriving in transition  
- Improved free throw percentage from 59% to 78% since 2018, showing strong development and potential to become an elite NBA player
strengths.518:
- Hustle was a consistent and reliable contributor on the team.  
- One of few veterans on a Final Four team.  
- Dramatically improved as a senior after receiving regular playing time from Coach John Calipari.  
- Maintained a solid rebound rate despite limited minutes.  
- Played within his skill set, avoiding unnecessary risks or overreaching.
strengths.519:
- Scored 18.7 points per game with a.51 field goal percentage and improved to 40% from long range on 5.1 attempts per game.  
- Established himself as a reliable spot-up shooter and effective spacer, a key asset in modern NBA offenses.  
- Achieved a career-high 4.7 charity stripe attempts per game with a 75% success rate, showcasing strong finishing ability at the rim.  
- Contributed 2.9 assists per game and demonstrated high court IQ, making him a willing and effective passer.  
- A strong defensive guard with 1.6 steals per game, elite defensive skills, and a coachable, mature demeanor that facilitates a smooth NBA transition.
strengths.520:
- Versatile defender who played effectively on both the block and on the wing  
- Strong rebounder who battled with bigger players on both offensively and defensively  
- Tough in the post and efficient at cutting to the basket  
- Defended the pick-and-roll well and was a reliable on-ball defender, especially against Andrew Wiggins in the tournament  
- Good shot blocker with strong help-side defense and rarely got in foul trouble despite aggressive play
strengths.521:
- Versatile wing with elite offensive and defensive capabilities, playing multiple positions including shooting guard and small forward  
- High basketball IQ and advanced court vision enable him to distribute the ball effectively and create scoring opportunities  
- Averages 3.1 assists per game with strong ball-handling skills, excellent first step, and ability to control defenders on the perimeter  
- Elite offensive rebounder and shot blocker for a wing (1.5 blocks and 1.5 steals per game), with aggressive defensive instincts and disruption in passing lanes  
- Combines explosiveness, finesse, and work ethic with a competitive drive, clutch performance, and leadership presence on both ends of the floor
strengths.522:
- Excellent size and wingspan for a wing at 6'8" with a 6'11" wingspan  
- Above-average athlete with strong finishing ability and extensive floor coverage on drives  
- Good motor and consistent floor presence; reliable playmaker and unselfish passer  
- Strong offensive versatility with range extending to the 3-point line and effective pull-up and spot-up shooting  
- Mature, focused, and a late-blooming player who rose in evaluation during senior year
strengths.523:
- 6'4" sophomore with a 7-foot wingspan and elite athleticism, among the best in NBA Combine testing  
- Solid scorer with a 38/100 three-point shooting percentage and strong pull-up game  
- Aggressive, fearless style that leads to frequent rim attacks and free throw opportunities  
- 82% free throw shooting and strong rebounding (6.3 per game), with excellent hands and loose ball recovery  
- Versatile player capable of post play, catch-and-shoot, and ball handling, with upside due to youth (turns 20 in September)
strengths.524:
- Listed at 6'6" with a 6'10" wingspan, offering excellent size and length for a shooting guard.  
- Possesses strong athleticism, speed, and a good first step, making him effective with or without the ball.  
- Developed into a reliable primary ball handler, showing composure in traffic and strong decision-making.  
- Excels in pick and roll situations, reads defenses well, and consistently finds open teammates or finishes in isolation.  
- Strong defensive potential with NBA-level size, agility, and energy, averaging 2.1 steals per 40 minutes and capable of becoming a strong "3 and D" player.
strengths.525:
- Athletic combo guard with a scorer’s mentality and strong physical attributes, standing 6’3” in shoes and 6’5.25” with a 42” vertical leap  
- Quick off the dribble, excellent lateral quickness, and strong body control to finish at the rim with or after contact  
- Dynamic offensive arsenal including a developing step-back jumper and solid three-point shooting (36.2% last season)  
- Confident (sometimes overconfident) shooter who takes advantage of defensive gaps and demonstrates NBA-range shooting  
- Tough, competitive defensively with good closing ability, though occasionally gambles; high potential as a freshman with room for development
strengths.526:
- Lean 6’5 wing with solid physical ability, quickness, and frame for muscle development  
- Sweet shooting stroke (38% 3FG) with confidence and quick release, effective as a floor spacer when defenses break down  
- Shows ability to create and finish mid-range and deep shots, especially during pick-and-roll actions  
- Fundamentally sound with good ball-handling, patience, basketball IQ, and efficient movement off the bounce  
- Strong defensive potential with length (6’9 wingspan), active footwork, and ability to contain dribble penetration
strengths.527:
- Tokoto has elite athleticism with a 6’10" wingspan and 40-inch max reach, allowing him to make an immediate impact in the NBA.  
- He developed into UNC’s best passer under Roy Williams, excelling in passing and creating opportunities, especially in transition.  
- His length and defensive awareness make him a strong defensive presence, particularly in covering the opponent’s best perimeter player.  
- He struggles with screens due to his slight frame, both on and off the ball, limiting his defensive versatility.  
- Tokoto excels in transition play, using his speed, length, and athleticism to finish at the rim or make effective plays like outlets and cuts.
strengths.528:
- Crafty ballhandler with a wiry, long build and a deceptive first step that keeps defenders off balance  
- Shifty quickness and excellent dribbling skills to navigate through traffic and evade defenders  
- Versatile with both hands, using body control and length to finish around the basket  
- Effective midrange shooter with balanced, rhythmic floaters and runners in the lane  
- Unselfish play, strong court vision, defensive aggression, and ability to pressure opponents and generate deflections
strengths.529:
- 6’10” stretch four with a 7’3” wingspan and 9’2” standing reach, known for using his size effectively as a PF/C hybrid  
- Fluid athlete with strong coordination, agility, and defensive versatility, excelling as a rim protector (1.4 bpg as a freshman) and pass disruptor  
- Effective offensive player who scores via transition dunks, pick-and-pop opportunities, and slasher moves; shows developing southpaw jump shot (74% FT) and 3-point shooting (44.4% in EYBL)  
- Strong defensive presence with high energy, ability to defend perimeter players, disrupt passes, and block shots (1.6 bpg in EYBL)  
- High potential for growth with added strength and improved contact play; stood out at the Draft Combine with shooting and athleticism, though struggled to showcase it at Auburn
strengths.531:
- High basketball IQ with exceptional court vision and ability to see plays before they happen  
- Skilled ball handler with creative dribbling, quick first step, and excellent lateral quickness  
- Effective floor general who leads transition play, pushes the pace, and makes smart, accurate passes  
- Master of pick and roll, able to find big men, third options, cutters, and manipulate defense  
- Creative finisher at the rim, solid defensive presence with good footwork, anticipation, and help defense
strengths.532:
- Smart player with a high basketball IQ  
- Decent athlete with solid vertical leap  
- Good ball handling and ability to cut left or right to beat defenders  
- Solid shooter from the field  
- Strong defensive skills, including good shot-blocking timing, ball-stealing ability, and offensive rebounding
strengths.533:
- Versatile combo guard with elite length (7' wingspan) and NBA-level athleticism  
- Explosive first step, great leaper off one foot, and strong motor enabling success in transition  
- Excels in isolation and as a slasher, attacks the rim confidently and finishes strong  
- Improves as a shooter, especially from spot-up 3s, and shows solid passing in transition and pick-and-roll situations  
- Effective defender with good lateral quickness, shot-blocking ability, and versatility across perimeter positions
strengths.534:
- Phillips is a 6’8” prospect with a near 7’ wingspan, offering excellent size for a wing/forward at the NBA level.

- A defensive standout with "defensive versatility," capable of guarding both the 3 and 4 positions; known for tenacity, aggression, and strong rebounding (averaging nearly 2 offensive rebounds per game).  
- Displays strong defensive instincts, frequently taking charges and hounding ball handlers, making him a disruptive presence on defense.  
- Offensively, he is raw but has decent finishing ability, can catch alley oops and lobs, and shows potential to improve as a shooter (82% from the free-throw line).
strengths.535:
- Efficient scorer from beyond the arc with a quick release and excellent floor spacing, thriving in fast-paced offenses  
- Strong inside game with a reliable floater and excellent touch around the rim, especially on aggressive closeouts

- Versatile offensive player who scores in spot-ups, off the dribble, and via backdoor cuts and transition plays  
- Clutch performer with game-winning shots in key moments and a disciplined, effective defensive presence
strengths.536:

- Effective on pick and pop and post-up plays due to his ability to create scoring opportunities  
- Dominant on both offensive and defensive rebounds, with natural instincts and relentless effort

strengths.537:

- Versatile wing player who can score from the 3-point line, mid-range, and in the paint; improved shooting efficiency significantly  
- Strong, explosive finisher with a quick first step, excellent leaping ability, and ability to attack closeouts  
- Unselfish and effective passer, especially in transition, with strong vision and ability to find open teammates

strengths.538:

- Effective post moves: nice baby push shot (left shoulder) and developing left-handed hook (right shoulder)  
- Strong mid-range shooting (feathery touch up to 16 feet) and excellent free-throw shooter (over 78% in 2011–2012)

- Fluid stroke with range extending to NBA 3-point level; quality spot-up shooter with quick release

- Uses foot speed to beat defenders, effective pump fake/jab step, and dribble to get to the rim  
- Strong free throw shooter (over 80%), skilled with a righty jump hook, spin move, and fadeaway jumper; handles ball well for a 4-guard
strengths.540:

- Capable of running in transition, finishing in traffic, and creating scoring opportunities through ball handling and driving.  
- Can score with either hand, finds angles in traffic, and has the potential to develop into a “3 and D” player at the NBA level.
strengths.541:

- Fluid athlete with a 6-foot-11 wingspan and 6-foot-8 frame, allowing him to play as a stretch-four, wing, or low-post player with excellent size and length.

strengths.542:
- Wiry, athletic wing with excellent fluidity and experience  
- Excels at finishing plays on the break and scoring off motion  
- Strong ability to generate free throws and average over 8 per game last season

- Solid rebounder (7.5 rebounds per game) and defensive player with good IQ and athleticism
strengths.543:

- Effective in the paint as a dive big man, especially in pick-and-roll offenses  
- Strong defensive presence around the rim, can distract and smother guards using length and agility  
- Skilled finisher and transition player, excels in converting paint touches and running down the court  
- Has guard-like abilities and ball-handling skills, though still developing outside shooting and footwork timing
strengths.544:
- Among the leading scorers in the nation during his junior and senior seasons  
- Explosive and shifty combo guard with strong athleticism  
- Finishes many plays at the rim with dunks  
- Versatile scorer capable of shooting from three, pull-ups, and spot-ups  
- Efficient shooter (51.1% FG, 42.5% 3P, 86.4% FT) and solid passer with mature leadership as a senior
strengths.545:
- Played four seasons for the Boise State Broncos, improving in all phases of the game.  
- Primarily played guard and is expected to be a spot-up shooter in the NBA.  
- Career three-point shooting percentage: 41%, with a 96% free-throw rate as a senior.  
- Maintained strong three-point shooting despite increased volume and improved from 73% to 96% free throw accuracy.  
- 6’7 frame, low turnover rate (1.3 per game), and solid shooting make him a strong fit for NBA teams prioritizing size and shooting.
strengths.546:
- Elite speed, athleticism, and basketball body with a solid wingspan and strong body strength for his age  
- Versatile scorer capable of spot-up shooting, finishing at the rim with dunks, and creating off the dribble  
- Dangerous in open space due to speed, leaping ability, and excellent lateral movement  
- Efficient and reliable left-handed shooter with steady 3-point production (39%, 1.4 makes per game)  
- Strong ball defense, smart decision-making, low turnover rate, and solid court awareness with good body control
strengths.547:
- Standing at 6’11” with a robust 280 lb frame and a 7’2” wingspan, he is well-suited for post play at the NBA level.

- Has soft hands and touch, capable of finishing with a hook shot in the paint using both hands (with room for improvement on his left).  
- Solid free throw shooter who draws contact effectively due to his physical presence.  
- Strong defensively with lateral speed, quick hands, and good basketball instincts, often orchestrating offensive plays.
strengths.548:
- Allen has a physical build of 6’3 and 200 pounds with long arms, allowing him to match up well with NBA guards despite lacking height.  
- He demonstrates solid vision and accurate off-the-move passing, showing potential as a half-court facilitator and effective offensive creator.  
- Allen excels at getting to the rim using quickness and strength, and has improved as a shooter, particularly off the catch, with a 40% three-point shooting rate (30-of-72).  
- He is a strong defensive player with elite lateral quickness, a long wingspan, and physicality, averaging 1.6 steals per game and defending multiple positions.  
- His defensive potential, rebounding ability (over 4 rebounds per game), and offensive improvements make him a compelling late draft pick with NBA draft radar.
strengths.549:
- Scored 20+ points in 30 of 35 games as a junior, averaging 24.2 ppg and finishing 4th in the nation in scoring.  
- Led the nation in assists per game with 9.3 apg, outpacing the next closest player by 1.2 apg.  
- Demonstrated explosive scoring in key matchups, including 37 points in a double OT loss to Michigan State and 38 points in a win at Washington.  
- Excels in fast-paced games, has elite one-on-one quickness and blow-by ability, and is difficult to defend due to his agility and court vision.  
- Improved in efficiency, shot selection, and ball handling, with a 2.7:1 assist-to-turnover ratio, strong free-throw shooting (85%), and strong defensive presence as a small guard.
strengths.550:
- Explosive athlete with high motor, exceptional vertical leap, and strong finishing ability  
- Fast and agile on the perimeter with quick feet, capable of creating space and defending small opponents  
- Athletic stretch five who defends effectively on the perimeter and in the post, with versatile defensive range  
- Strong perimeter shooter, including 39% from three-point range (up 10% from freshman year) and 63% inside the arc  
- Developed significantly in shooting, rebounding, and overall efficiency, with a 14.2-point improvement in Net Rating and rising NBA draft stock
strengths.551:
- Shifty left-handed combo guard with advanced ball skills, strong scoring instincts, and underrated playmaking upside.  
- Averaged 19.2 points, 5.9 assists, 4.5 rebounds, and 1.4 steals per game in senior season at Marquette.  
- Excels in isolation and ball screens using sharp crossovers, hesitation dribbles, and creative footwork to get to the paint.  
- Uses floaters, flips, and unorthodox one-handed finishes; strong finesse and body control despite limited power.

strengths.552:
- Size and skill set position him to make a strong impact at both ends of the floor in the NBA  
- Hard worker with strong character, competitive nature, and a great feel for the game  
- Offensive potential with consistent shooting, excellent touch, and a developing hook shot  
- Strong rebounder with big hands, solid post positioning, and energy; capable of playing 260–270 lbs comfortably  
- Good vision, passer, and shot blocker with excellent timing and length, plus solid defensive foot speed and perimeter defense
strengths.553:
- Strong physical build with wide shoulders, 7-foot wingspan, and above-average length  
- Excellent game awareness, basketball IQ, and motor skills  
- Efficient floor movement, strong leaping ability, and explosive finish at the rim  
- Effective in pick-and-roll as a screener, rolls hard to the basket and shows potential as a passer in short rolls  
- Good shooting mechanics, mid-range potential, strong rebounding (especially offensively), and solid defensive presence on both ends
strengths.554:
- Fits the modern big guard archetype with size (6'6", 200 lbs) and adaptability to different game paces through overseas experience.

strengths.555:
- Hybrid forward with exceptional length, high energy, and strong motor  
- Plays primarily on the perimeter but can post up effectively with a reliable turnaround jumper  
- Long strides and agility allow him to cover large areas on the floor  
- Improved shooting from freshman to sophomore seasons, especially from outside with a difficult pull-up jumper  
- Strong defensive instincts, capable of contesting shots, disrupting passing lanes, and grabbing rebounds well outside his typical range
strengths.556:

- Effective floor spacer with consistent shooting from all three levels, nearly 40% 3PT shooting, strong long-range and pull-up ability  
- Excellent first step, adept at slipping through defenses, spinning in tight spaces, and finishing with finesse or authority  
- Strong post-up player, especially against smaller opponents, with good command of jump hooks and up-and-under moves  
- High IQ, efficient shot selection (54% FG career), strong rebounding (nearly 9 RPG, 3 ORPG), solid shot-blocking (1.6 BPG), and low turnover rate (1.1 TOG)

- He demonstrates speed, particularly in transition, enabling him to drive to the rim and facilitate offense.

- His mid-range post game is strong and underrated, complemented by a high basketball IQ and maturity developed through four years at a top-tier program.

- Developing post game featuring a soft touch, solid footwork, and a highly effective right-handed jump hook  
- Capable of creating scoring opportunities through pick-and-pop plays and uses his size to dominate in the low post and fight for position  
- Coachable, plays with intensity, and demonstrates a growing understanding of the game, including strong ball protection and rebounding instincts
strengths.559:
- Supremely talented 7'0" big man with prototypical size, length, and athleticism suitable for the NBA center position  
- Excellent shot form, efficient scoring in the paint, and effective turnaround jumper with strong hands and rebounding (10 rebounds per game)  
- Shows strong defensive ability, including shot blocking (1.9 blocks per game) and rim protection with good leaping ability and positioning  
- Develops as a reliable catch-and-shoot threat with range out to three, capable of becoming a floor spacer despite not frequently drifting to the perimeter  
- Strong athlete with explosive rim-running ability, foot speed (sub-11 second shuttle), and solid improvement in scoring (from 6.6 to 15.9 ppg) and overall game intelligence
strengths.560:
- Explosive first step and strong athleticism enable him to get past defenders and finish around the basket.  
- Effective three-point shooter (38% from deep) and reliable free throw shooter (74.6%–87.5%).

- Solid defensive presence with good length, effort, and ability to guard multiple positions, including drawing fouls and making steals.  
- High basketball IQ, leadership qualities, and unselfish play that make him a versatile and impactful player on both ends of the floor.
strengths.561:
- Averaged 18 points per game on 66% field goal percentage in college, showcasing strong scoring ability.

- Strong rebounding, solid defensive presence, and consistent effort with excellent shot-blocking and positioning.  
- Efficient mid-range shooter (82% from the free throw line) and shows potential to develop a three-point game despite current low volume and efficiency.
strengths.562:
- Kelly Oubre’s 6’7” height and 7’2” wingspan align with ideal NBA wing dimensions, enhancing his defensive and offensive potential.

strengths.563:
- Exhibits explosive speed, quickness, and agility, moving at "warp speed" while maintaining control and ball security.

- Combines offensive creativity and facilitation (4.5 apg), showing leadership, passion, and a warrior’s mentality, with potential to become a top-tier pro point guard.
strengths.564:

- Unselfish with solid vision and passing (1.9 assists per game), confident in decision-making and decisive downhill drives

strengths.565:
- Brilliant floor general with exceptional basketball IQ and advanced understanding of game flow  
- High offensive awareness and height allow him to see the entire court at all times  
- Efficient, creative passer with a low turnover rate and mastery of post-entry and lob passes  
- Consistently makes outlet passes look effortless and demonstrates excellent timing and tempo control  
- Smart defender with great reaction time, anticipation, and ability to operate effectively in both half-court and fast-paced situations
strengths.566:
- Lightning-fast first step and elite speed downhill, creating significant space on the drive  
- Strong mid-range shooting and effective use of a runner to compensate for limited rim finishing  
- Improved 3-point shooting dramatically, shooting 57% from deep in March with over four attempts per game  
- Accurate, crafty passer who excels at finding cutting wings and big men in the paint  
- Defensive disruptor with above-average steals, strong anticipation, and ability to contest shots and generate turnovers
strengths.567:
- Excels in rebounding, defense, and post play with a high level of energy and aggressiveness  
- Possesses huge hands and an athletic build that aid in securing rebounds and compensating for lack of ideal size

- Leads the nation in rebounds (13.3 rpg) and ranks top in the OVC in steals (2.3 per game)

strengths.568:

- Efficient off-ball scorer: 58.8% AFGP in spot-up situations, 50% off screens, and 1.275 PPP in right-side pick-and-roll plays.  
- Elite defensive presence: strong defensive positioning, length to disrupt shots, tenacious passing lane defense (2 SPG), and effective rebounding (7.1 REB).  
- High upside as a shooting specialist with size, defensive skills, and rebounding ability; confident, fluid, and consistently threats from deep or mid-range.
strengths.569:
- Freakish athleticism with exceptional ability to play above the rim  
- Excellent shot blocker and rebounder  
- Significantly improved offensive game, including ability to post up and take his man off the dribble  
- Performs well in crunchtime, as demonstrated in the game versus DePaul  
- Strong character and competitive mindset
strengths.570:
- 6’5”, 186 lbs with a 6’8” wingspan, ideal size for a wing player and capable of gaining weight as he matures  
- Exceptional defender with sharp instincts, reflexes, and intense effort, featuring quick lateral movement  
- Explosive athlete who can hang in the air, take contact, and finish at the rim with strong elevation  
- Uses driving angles and body control to create scoring opportunities and generate clean shot angles  
- Makes spectacular plays regularly but plays sound basketball without sacrificing fundamentals
strengths.572:
- Shot 34% from deep in his most recent season after a strong start  
- Career shooting splits: 45 field goals, 34 three-pointers, 80% field goal percentage  
- 65% shooting success at the rim  
- 75% of his three-pointers were assisted, indicating strong catch-and-shoot ability  
- High motor, consistent rebounder (averaged 8.5+ rebounds over three years), effective without the ball, and capable of scoring in the low post and playing as a playmaker
strengths.573:

- He excels as a catch-and-shoot player, both stationary and off screens, with excellent shot preparation, rotation, and deep range.  
- Huerter improved his field goal percentage by eight points due to effective mid-range shooting and better shot selection.

- Despite not being the most physically gifted, he contributes on defense with solid footwork, positioning, and rebounding (5 rebounds per game), and plays hard on both ends.
strengths.574:
- Standing 6’9” and weighing 218 pounds with a 7’1” wingspan, he has exceptional size and length that create matchup difficulties for opposing teams.

- Excels in transition, runs lanes well, and is a reliable finisher with aggressive attack and excellent footwork and lateral quickness.  
- Shows steady improvement defensively, particularly in staying in front of ball handlers, and has a high motor with strong rebounding and offensive board conversion.  
- Young (will turn 19 in August), with significant growth potential and a well-rounded skill set that positions him as a versatile, all-around player with NBA-level tools.
strengths.575:
- 6’7” guard/forward with length, strength, and mobility, proven scorer at the college level (18.8 PPG, 45% FG in 2023–24).  
- Strong floor awareness, skilled at backdoor cuts and open-court scoring, with growing confidence in spot-up 3-point shooting (39 made 3s in 26 games).

- Effective ball-handler with decent floor vision and a career-best 4.1 APG in 2023–24, contributing to offensive flow.  
- Elite defensive presence, consistently among the best in college, capable of defending all perimeter positions and frequently matched up against top wing scorers in the NBA.
strengths.576:
- Explosive scorer with a wide offensive skill-set  
- Good length for either wing position and quick feet  
- Gets squared up to the hoop quickly and is a volume shooter (41.6% from three as a senior)  
- Deadly step-back jumper that is difficult to defend and uses hesitation moves and pump-fakes to create space

strengths.694:

strengths.760:

strengths.762:

strengths.766:

- Excellent playmaker in pick-and-roll situations, sets up teammates effectively, and frequently kicks out for open threes when driven out of position.  
- High-effort defender with strong lateral movement and steals (3.2 avg. at U16 Americas), showing maturity and team-first mentality despite being a top scorer.
strengths.816:
- Legitimate 7-footer with bulk, playing an old-school post game featuring back-to-the-basket moves.  
- Outstanding free throw shooter (~80%) with consistent ability to get to the line.  
- Smart defender and solid shot blocker, relying on size, positioning, and lateral quickness rather than explosiveness.  
- Significant improvement since arriving at Gonzaga, with development across all aspects of his game.  
- Capable of hitting face-up jumpers from the 12–15 foot range, showcasing offensive versatility.
strengths.818:
- Possesses a near 7’6 wingspan and 240-pound frame, providing elite length, shot-blocking ability, and physical dominance at the rim.  
- Excels in rebounding with a high offensive (12%) and defensive (23.7%) rebound rate, using two hands and positioning to secure boards inside and outside his area.  
- Demonstrates strong shot-blocking skills through anticipation, length, and verticality, averaging 2.5 blocks per game.  
- Developing an offensive game with ability to create offense facing up, on the low block, and as a surprising mid-range shooter (12–15 feet).  
- Combines explosive athleticism, good body control, and a physical, aggressive style that allows him to draw fouls, finish strong, and play both post positions effectively.
strengths.819:
- Averaged a steal and a block per game as a sophomore, showcasing strong defensive presence both on the perimeter and in the post.  
- Possesses a 7’2 wingspan that enables disruptive handoff plays and elevates effectively against taller big men.  
- Improved three-point shooting from 27% to 43% and averaged over 11 points per game in his second season.  
- Developed elite driving ability with strong bounce, allowing him to finish above and around the rim and maneuver effectively in the air.  
- Known for relentless hustle, high work ethic, and smart ball movement, making him a consistent and valuable contributor off the bench.

weaknesses.0:
- Lacks offensive skill, particularly in ball-handling, shot creation, and perimeter shooting, with poor footwork and a slow, deliberate shooting motion.  
- Struggles significantly from the free-throw line (42% in college) and has limited range, making him inconsistent beyond the paint.  
- Has a narrow, undersized frame that limits his ability to defend against bigger forwards in the NBA and raises concerns about durability and positioning.  
- Poor lateral movement, awkward running style, and tight hips hinder his defensive efficiency and ability to move quickly on offense.  
- Unclear NBA position due to being a "tweener" with strong explosiveness but lacking perimeter skills, foot speed, and defensive flexibility.
weaknesses.1:
- Listed at 6’1”, 185 pounds, small for an NBA point guard despite a good wingspan.  
- Limited by size and leaping ability, making it difficult to finish at the rim.  
- Needs to adapt to NBA pace, improve floater execution, and get shots off quickly.  
- Struggles with fighting through screens and closing out on shooters, allowing open shots.  
- Has a poor assist-to-turnover ratio (5.8 assists, 3.8 turnovers) and needs better ball security.
weaknesses.2:
- Did not sustain breakout performance beyond 14 games and missed nearly all conference play.  
- Shot only 33.7% from three as a freshman, with efficiency declining in conference games.  
- Lacks fluidity, quickness, and a strong first step, making it difficult to create scoring opportunities against top defenders.  
- Poor ball-handling and low assist-to-turnover ratio (0.9 to 1.7) highlight struggles in creating and managing the ball, especially under pressure.  
- Defensive weaknesses, lack of rebounding, and hesitation in contact at the rim limit his readiness for the NBA level.
weaknesses.3:
- Poor defensive performance with frequent lapses in positioning and focus  
- Limited perimeter shooting, especially as a senior (only 59 shots)  
- Frail build and lack of strength development during college  
- Unflattering, "hunched" posture and poor body control  
- Struggles defensively against post-up plays and fails to finish at the rim under pressure
weaknesses.4:
- Shoots a soft jump shot that is streaky and occasionally flawed due to poor fundamentals.  
- Tends to stick his leg out when spotting up and fades away under pressure, leading to missed shots.  
- Lacks explosiveness and is more of an open-court finisher, with shots often rejected or altered at the rim.  
- Struggles with shot selection, frequently settling for jumpers (1.8 FTA per game) and making few assists (1.6 apg).  
- Performance varies significantly based on competition; excels against strong teams but underperforms against weaker opponents.
weaknesses.6:
- Struggles with shot creation off the dribble, lacks shifty movement and consistent penetration into the paint.  
- Tends to settle on jump shots, often off-balanced, and has poor efficiency in scoring due to suboptimal shot selection.  
- Inconsistent 3-point shooting with a high arc; capable but streaky, and can improve release on pull-ups.  
- Poor ball-handling with high dribble, upright stance, lack of wiggle, and frequent turnovers; needs better court vision and offensive feel.  
- Defensive tools are good but inconsistent; can improve by tightening up on closeouts and making shooters uncomfortable, while also needing better emotional control and physical development.
weaknesses.8:
- Averaged only 26.5 minutes per game and was fouled out 7 times during the season, reaching 4 fouls 12 times.  
- Needs better defensive discipline and more aggressive rebounding, especially on offensive boards.  
- Lacks passing ability and has not attempted any three-pointers in college; limited mid-range shooting and poor three-point shooting (20% 3PT% on just 0.5 attempts per game).  
- Offensively raw, with weak shooting form outside of dunks; needs improvement in footwork, technique, and left-hand shooting.  
- Free throw performance is inconsistent (ranging from 36.7% to 61.1%), and he has limited scoring range and is not a consistent threat to stretch the floor.
weaknesses.9:
- Averaged 2.3 free throw attempts per game, indicating limited driving fouls despite being a big, physical player.  
- Capable of creating pressure on defense when cutting or driving to the lane due to his size and athleticism.  
- Shoots well from three-point range (4.8 3PA per game, 42% success rate) but often settles, limiting interior offensive impact.  
- Struggles defensively against quicker players, despite having lateral quickness and strength, and underperforms in steals and blocks.  
- Projected as a second-round NBA draft pick with potential to move into a fringe first-round selection based on strong workouts and talent.
weaknesses.10:
- Limited offensive production, especially on the perimeter, with poor shot creation and a lack of advanced ball-handling  
- Inconsistent shooting: 25.6% from three and 68.6% from the free-throw line as a junior, with regression from 80% as a sophomore  
- High turnover rate (1.7 per game) and poor decision-making, indicating deficiencies in ball-handling and offensive execution  
- Functions primarily as a finisher rather than an initiator; struggles to collapse defenses or create consistent advantages  
- Questionable positional fit as an undersized four with limited perimeter skills, and lacks reliable jump shot or offensive versatility
weaknesses.11:
- Has excellent physical tools, including a 7' wingspan, but struggles with smart shot blocking due to poor defensive timing.  
- Lacks threat to create for teammates or make plays off the dribble; limited perimeter game with inconsistent shooting and poor ball-handling.  
- Strong rebounder historically, but rebounding numbers slightly declined (7.6 to 7.3 rpg) despite increased playing time.  
- May struggle with extended minutes due to reported reduced lung capacity, though projected to play around 28 minutes per game as a role player.  
- Takes time to set his jump shot, often brings the ball down to waist, but has a high final release point; shows poor shot selection and tendency to settle for weak jumpers.
weaknesses.12:
- Offensively, he is still raw and not comfortable with the ball outside of the low post.  
- Lacks a reliable midrange game.  
- Has poor positioning in the offensive paint, limiting quality entry passes to the low post.  
- Needs to gain more bulk to compete with bigger players and improve his low post effectiveness.  
- Currently working on building strength and improving his physical presence.
weaknesses.13:
- Lacks foot speed, making it difficult to blow by opponents and leading to crowding on the perimeter.  
- Slow to react defensively, vulnerable to back cuts and prone to getting stuck on screens.  
- Primarily a shooter and complementary scorer at Duke, with limited production in other areas and questions about carrying a team.  
- Struggles with aggression and consistency as a shooter, especially in key games like Duke’s Final Four loss to UNC.  
- Shows predictable dribbling patterns, relies on right-hand attacks, and has a hesitation tell with his left hand, allowing defenders to anticipate his moves.
weaknesses.14:
- Struggles with consistency and assertiveness on the court, often disappearing for stretches of the game.  
- Advanced age (24 in August) limits his prime window, especially as a big man entering the NBA.  
- Poor passer, frequently drawing double- and triple-teams in the post with limited offensive creation.  
- High turnover rate, prone to being stripped and making poor passes from the post.  
- Off-court discipline issues, including suspensions, and a history of losing starting spots due to performance or misconduct.
weaknesses.15:
- Lacks physical strength and contact handling, making him vulnerable defensively and limiting his ability to finish plays.  
- Shooting is inconsistent, with below-average efficiency (36.5% FG, 22.4% 3PT) and a low release point; free throw percentage declined in senior year.  
- Struggles with scoring volume and consistency, with no high-scoring season in high school or college; remains an unproven volume scorer.  
- Needs improvement in ball handling, particularly in avoiding over-dribbling and utilizing the offense more effectively off the ball.  
- Has potential as a combo guard with creativity and skill, but lacks development in playmaking, rebounding, and overall consistency to contribute at the NBA level in the near term.
weaknesses.16:
- Lacks explosive first step and lateral quickness, limiting his ability to drive to the basket and evade defenders.  
- Struggles with jumping ability, making it difficult to finish at the rim or shoot over elite NBA athletes.  
- Has a weak jumpshot with low field goal and three-point percentages, and often settles for safe shots instead of going for strong finishes.  
- Injury-prone history, including a torn ACL and a near-fatal brain hemorrhage, with ongoing concerns about durability.  
- Off-court issues, such as the laptop burglary incident, continue to raise concerns despite maturity and on-court contributions.
weaknesses.17:
- Mitchell has shown significant improvement in three-point shooting, raising his percentage from 27% last year to 47% this season.  
- He is a key player on a successful UCSB team that consistently wins, though the team has faced only one ranked opponent in the past three years.  
- His performance against ranked teams, such as Baylor in 2023, was solid but not standout.  
- Questions remain about his strength, control, and ability to create separation at the NBA level.  
- His defensive potential and ability to function effectively off the ball are still uncertain.
weaknesses.18:
- Lacks range and consistency from the outside, unable to pull up off the dribble or shoot effectively from beyond the arc.  
- Struggles with touch around the basket when not close to the rim and has limited shooting ability.  
- Prone to turnovers and awkward when taking his man off the dribble; poor ball-handling diversity.  
- Relies heavily on a quick first step and mainly drives to the right, limiting offensive versatility.  
- Needs improved physical strength, defensive consistency, and perimeter shooting to develop as a viable 3 or power forward.
weaknesses.19:
- Alec lacks physical strength, making him ineffective in post play and unable to guard NBA-level centers.  
- His defensive skills are weak, limiting his potential as a rotational player at the next level.  
- He struggles to create shots in the post due to his lack of strength.  
- His rebounding of 5.7 per game is below expectations for a 7-footer in a mid-major conference.  
- He performed poorly against top opponents, including fouling out against Wisconsin and failing to score in double figures versus Virginia, Wisconsin-Milwaukee, and Belmont.
weaknesses.20:
- Needs to add bulk and muscle to his 192-pound frame to improve strength and explosiveness for finishing at the rim in professional play.  
- Struggles with shooting from downtown (29% success rate) and lacks consistency, especially from outside 15 feet due to poor mechanics and a push-style shot.  
- Midrange shooting is inconsistent, often relying on off-balance, off-the-dribble jumpers that lack reliability.  
- Requires development in off-the-ball movement, screen-setting, and defensive one-on-one matchups against quicker guards.  
- Needs to improve shot selection, discipline, and spot-up shooting ability, which remains his biggest offensive weakness.
weaknesses.21:
- Below-average athlete by NBA standards at 6'8", with a center-like frame in a small forward's body  
- Lacks size and strength to defend in the post and struggles to defend small forwards due to poor lateral speed and slow reaction time  
- Not fast in open floor play; described as a "plodder" with limited athleticism  
- Season-ending stress fracture in his foot jeopardizes draft prospects  
- Shot only 36.3% from three-point range as a senior, a drop from 44.0% and 46.6% in previous seasons, potentially due to defensive game planning limiting his shot attempts
weaknesses.22:
- Limited offensive skills off the dribble due to lack of speed and ball-handling ability  
- Struggles to score against bigger, stronger opponents and lacks explosiveness  
- Poor defensive performance due to lack of speed, lateral quickness, and physical toughness  
- Inadequate athleticism for either power forward or small forward positions in the NBA  
- Needs to improve body composition, ball handling (especially with right hand), and overall physicality
weaknesses.24:
- Undersized for a center and not athletic enough for a power forward  
- Struggles with reading game situations and rushes decisions too frequently  
- Fearless but lacks elite athleticism, making it difficult to finish in traffic or compete against experienced opponents  
- Inconsistent shooter with poor ball handling and tendency to lose control of the ball  
- Below-average defensive skills, including poor guarding of pick-and-roll plays, lateral quickness, and lack of rim protection
weaknesses.25:
- Lacks elite athleticism and explosiveness, making physical impact less effective against NBA-level athletes.  
- Shot mechanics are flawed with a slow, predictable release and pause, reducing shot effectiveness and consistency.  
- Struggles with shot selection, midrange game, and using screens effectively to create shooting opportunities.  
- Defensively limited by slow lateral speed, especially when guarding quicker, smaller opponents.  
- Has a short temper and inconsistent attitude, though recent improvements suggest better self-control and leadership potential.
weaknesses.26:
- Needs to add strength to withstand contact and maintain balance  
- Lacks toughness and poor footwork, with issues in coordination and posture  
- Doesn’t attack the rim enough and lacks a reliable midrange game  
- Requires improvement in pivot foot usage and offensive fakes to become a better offensive threat  
- Poor lateral quickness and defensive attitude hinder effectiveness against guards and small forwards at the NBA level
weaknesses.27:
- Len struggles with decision-making and ball-handling outside a few feet, lacking a developed face-up game despite high agility.  
- Limited offensive effectiveness in pick-and-pop situations and poor performance in shot creation (8.5 FGA, 4 FTA per game).  
- Underdeveloped defensive discipline, frequent foul trouble, and weak lower body strength hinder post defense and offensive positioning.  
- Ankle issues have plagued him throughout college and limited his participation in combine drills and practice time.  
- Shows potential as a good rebounder and lottery-pick prospect, but needs improvement in ball security, assertiveness, and game awareness.
weaknesses.29:
- Lacks ideal foot speed and lateral quickness, making it difficult to consistently defend NBA wings and defend in switches.  
- Limited vertical pop (26.5″ standing, 31.5″ max) reduces finishing ability in traffic and defensive impact around the rim.  
- Struggles to elevate in traffic and relies heavily on craft to score against defenders.  
- Has weak shooting stats (31.0% from three, 72.6% from the line), requiring increased volume and accuracy to secure a role.  
- Limited shot creation and poor dribble decision-making; needs to improve handle and develop more off-the-dribble counters.
weaknesses.30:
- Has solid wiry strength but lacks imposing physical presence for an NBA athlete.  
- Questionable potential to become a high-level scorer due to inconsistent three-point shooting (sub-30%).  
- Shows promise as a face-up threat, but three-point shooting remains unreliable.  
- Defensive potential is strong, with real ability to anchor the defense; success depends on leadership and defensive awareness.  
- Limited playmaking and passing ability; poor execution when finishing short of the rim.
weaknesses.31:
- Martin is an undersized, tough two-guard with limited prototypical traits for either backcourt position in the NBA.  
- His height (6’1.5”) and reach (8’3.5”) make him a defensive fit issue at the next level.  
- Not a point guard by trade; averaged only 2.2 assists and lacks offensive creation or ball-handling ability.  
- Struggles with separation against longer defenders and relies on straight-line driving rather than shifty or deceptive moves.  
- Lacks elite athleticism (29.5” no-step vert, 38.0” max vert), is prone to turnovers under heavy usage, and often takes contested jumpers.
weaknesses.33:
- Lacks consistency in creating his own shot off the dribble and relies primarily on catching and shooting.  
- Struggles with 1-on-1 situations against defenders, particularly along the wings.  
- Limited ball-handling ability; lacks decisive dribbling moves beyond two dribbles.  
- Appears nonchalant and disengaged on the court, both offensively and defensively.  
- Needs to improve ball-handling, finish in the paint, and maintain poise under contact while developing defensive intensity.
weaknesses.34:
- Small and undersized for a center (closer to 6-9 than 6-10) with limited length (7-foot wingspan)  
- Lacks elite athleticism and explosiveness, though improves in conditioning and weight  
- Shooting range is limited to the paint; shaky jump shot and poor footwork reduce consistency  
- Struggles to finish at the rim, especially with his left hand; overly reliant on dominant side  
- Poor lateral quickness and lack of size/athleticism limit defensive versatility against elite defenders
weaknesses.35:
- Lacks elite explosiveness and has stiff, slow feet, limiting his agility and overall fluidity.  
- Poor footwork in the post and underdeveloped back-to-the-basket game, making him ineffective against skilled opponents.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from long range, and struggles to finish against defensive length.
