- Relies heavily on physical attributes rather than skill, with limited game comprehension and competitive intensity.  
- Defensive shortcomings include weak rim protection, poor positioning, lack of aggression in closing out, and inconsistent stance.
weaknesses.36:
- Acts more as a tweener than a combo guard, with limited playmaking instincts and natural game feel for a point guard.  
- Lacks confidence in his 3-pointer and size to play full-time on the wing in certain matchups.  
- Poor decision-making and tendency to force the action, with inconsistent handling of help defense, traps, and hedges.  
- Inadequate jump shooting mechanics, including a slow release and low elevation, limiting efficiency against elite defenders.  
- Below-average scoring efficiency, especially at the free-throw line (70% FT%), and limited creative offensive versatility.
weaknesses.37:
- Clunky form and lack of rhythm limit his potential as a reliable perimeter threat.  
- Limited outside shooting touch and difficulty finishing in traffic without physicality.  
- Needs to be more decisive and aggressive near the rim, often avoiding contact.  
- Predictable post game due to consistent right-shoulder, left-hand finishing patterns.  
- Lacks vertical explosiveness and second-jump quickness, limiting recovery and effectiveness against elite defenders.
weaknesses.38:
- Struggles with jump shot mechanics, has a rigid form and awkward follow-through, especially under pressure  
- Lacks mid-range or floater ability, making him vulnerable in isolation or screening situations  
- Minimal scoring threat in half-court offense when not handling the ball; not a natural scorer or playmaker  
- Poor decision-making in slow-paced play, overreaches as a passer (3.3 topg, 1.74:1 A:TO), and fails to read defenses  
- Below-average free throw shooter (65%) and defensive issues including poor dribble denial, being washed out when screened, and overcommitting to steals or transitions
weaknesses.39:
- Drummond lacks consistent post moves and often shoots awkwardly, with limited mastery of go-to offensive actions.  
- He rarely uses a one-handed hook and tends to shoot with both hands, lacking a developed shot routine.  
- Most of his points come from teammates’ creativity, making him vulnerable if playmakers are restricted.  
- Poor free-throw shooting (29%) and a short, low-release jumper limit his scoring efficiency and range.  
- Key concerns include maturity, work ethic, offensive development, and the need for strength and a killer instinct to grow into a dominant player.
weaknesses.40:
- Lacks offensive touch, confidence, and consistency in scoring, resulting in a low career scoring average of 6.0 ppg.  
- Is unselfish to a fault and struggles to take ownership of his shots, limiting his impact as a two-way player.  
- Has poor free-throw efficiency (64%) and an awkward shooting motion, suggesting ongoing struggles as a jump-shooter.  
- Shows some willingness to shoot from the perimeter (2.5 attempts per game), but efficiency remains low at 28%.  
- Despite quickness and athleticism, his thin frame and lack of aggressive shot-taking prevent consistent shot creation and free-throw opportunities.
weaknesses.41:
- Below average ball skills, shooting ability, and limited range, especially on the wing  
- Slightly low release on shot, struggles to create separation and performs poorly under pressure  
- Very limited offensive production; primary offense comes from defense and hustle  
- Inconsistent shooting, poor foul-line percentage, and declining offensive efficiency  
- Lacks size for the post and handle/shooting ability for small forward, requiring strength development and skill polish before NBA viability
weaknesses.42:
- Undersized for a shooting guard at the next level  
- Limited paint presence due to below-average athleticism and size  
- Primary offensive contribution comes from the perimeter  
- Solid passer but not a true point guard  
- Prone to turnovers (3.2 per game) and best suited playing off the ball
weaknesses.43:
- Struggles with shot selection, decision-making, and finishing in traffic, especially near the basket.  
- Lacks NBA-level speed and lateral quickness, limiting his ability to defend or create plays.  
- Has a questionable attitude and body language, contributing to negative perceptions among scouts.  
- Not a natural playmaker; tends to be ball-dominant, forces turnovers, and struggles to read defenses.  
- Needs to improve off-hand shooting, defensive awareness, and team-oriented play to earn NBA minutes.
weaknesses.44:
- Lacks elite speed and athleticism suitable for the NBA level  
- Jump shot is still developing and not a consistent or major part of his game  
- Struggles to create scoring opportunities like he did in college  
- Projected to score no more than 10 points per game in the NBA  
- Upside is below that of other point guards in his draft class  
- Did not face top-tier competition in his final WCC seasons, though excelled at Florida  
- Struggled against high-end competition in his final season
weaknesses.45:
- Lacks bulk and upper body strength, making it difficult to compete inside and establish low post position against stronger players.  
- Frequently gets off balance and pressured into poor shot attempts, especially when contested.  
- Has a questionable motor, often taking plays off, particularly in the final stretches of games.  
- Inconsistent in transition, selective about running the floor and prone to turnovers, especially when out of double teams.  
- Struggles in traffic, tends to attack and spin into help defenders; needs to expand his low post repertoire, particularly with left-handed counter moves.
weaknesses.46:
- Questions remain about Wiggins' mental makeup, including focus, passion, and assertiveness, despite his physical gifts.  
- Plays too upright on offense, leading to turnovers and poor ball security; struggles with moving without the ball and ball-handling.  
- Defensive awareness is lacking; relies on length and athleticism rather than positioning and effort.  
- Inconsistent shot selection, poor post positioning, and lack of mid-range shot creation limit offensive effectiveness.  
- Needs to develop toughness, upper body strength, and attention to detail to become a reliable, vocal leader at the next level.
weaknesses.47:
- Lacks athleticism, size, and strength, limiting his effectiveness against other shooting guards.  
- Has a slow first step, making him primarily a catch-and-shoot scorer.  
- Struggles with consistency from deep, as he relies heavily on three-point shooting and lacks offensive creation.  
- Rarely finishes at the basket, as he seldom gets into scoring positions except in transition.  
- Inadequate defensively, unable to match up against quicker or stronger guards, especially at the 1 and 2 positions.
weaknesses.48:
- Labeled as a combo guard with room to improve point guard and playmaking skills  
- Averaged only 1.3 assists per game in the Under Armour Association  
- Finished the NBPA Top 100 with 12 assists and 13 turnovers, indicating a turnover-prone game  
- Has a quick shot but a low release point, limiting efficiency  
- Lacks strength and is only 181 pounds, with limited explosiveness and below-average athleticism compared to peers
weaknesses.49:
- Limited versatility, able to play only at the center position  
- Average athlete by NBA standards with deficiencies in athleticism and size  
- Needs to build strength and improve footwork on both ends of the floor  
- Struggles with finishing at the rim, especially with his left hand and in traffic  
- Poor ball handling, passing, and decision-making; high turnover rate and low assist-to-turnover ratio
weaknesses.50:
- Bennett is slightly undersized for a full-time power forward but could transition to the small forward position, though his defensive pace is a concern.  
- His major pre-college health issues, including missed junior and senior seasons, raised concerns, though he has remained injury-free in college.  
- Despite his large frame and strength, he is sometimes perceived as lacking toughness and ferocity, especially on the defensive boards.  
- He averages a team-high 8 rebounds per game but occasionally shows disinterest in defensive effort.  
- Initially defended the center, but as Khem Birch became eligible, his defensive responsibilities shifted, raising questions about his defensive desire.
weaknesses.51:
- Lacks explosive speed and quickness, often failing to separate from defenders off the dribble  
- Not a natural scorer with a limited mid-range game and inconsistent shooting (30% 3PT% on 2.8 attempts per game)  
- Struggles with jumpers off the dribble due to poor shot elevation and follow-through, leading to missed shots  
- Inefficient passer due to poor decision-making and tendency to force passes when walled off; also telegraphs his intentions and leaves his feet when passing  
- Shows potential as a team contributor but lacks standout traits; needs improvement in shooting consistency, ball-handling under pressure, and defensive awareness
weaknesses.52:
- Struggles with quick reads and finding open teammates after defensive rotations  
- Inconsistent when shooting off the dribble despite being a good shooter  
- Has difficulty finishing over taller opponents and avoids contact in the paint  
- Poor defensive performance: lacks agility, slow reaction time, and struggles with closing out or fighting over screens  
- Minimal impact on offensive rebounding due to frequent jump shots and reluctance to attempt put-backs
weaknesses.53:
- Davis is physically thin and lacks overall strength, with limited potential to bulk up without compromising speed and agility.  
- His frame has a chiseled upper body but a thin waist, limiting substantial muscle gain and affecting core and lower body strength.  
- Poor core and lower body development lead to frequent pinning and difficulty maintaining position in the paint.  
- Offensively, he lacks refinement in his jump shot, often rushing attempts and lacking consistency in post moves and face-up play.  
- While athletic and a good leaper, his size, lack of offensive discipline, and potential knee issues due to growth could hinder development against elite competition.
weaknesses.54:
- Needs to improve assertiveness on both ends of the court, frequently bailing out defenders and underutilizing physical gifts.  
- Struggles with shot selection, often settling for low-percentage jumpers and lacking a reliable mid-range game.  
- Should develop a floater or pull-up jumper between 12–15 feet to keep defenders honest and limit open looks.  
- Displays poor defensive awareness, especially in half-court, leading to backdoor cuts and missed opportunities to box out.  
- Has room to grow in basketball IQ, passing efficiency (2.8 A:TO, 130 usage rate), and midrange scoring, with potential to improve through better floor awareness and movement.
weaknesses.55:
- Poor free throw shooter, consistently hovering between 55% and 70%.  
- Lacks high-level scoring ability and reliable go-to moves.  
- Has good size but limited athleticism, especially in vertical leap (max 32 inches).  
- Below average leaper for an NBA forward, limiting lob threat.  
- Will turn 24 in October.
weaknesses.56:
- Turns 24 at the start of his rookie year after five years of college basketball.  
- Defensive effort improves, but lacks the ability to be a consistent stopper.  
- Limited bounce on drives; primarily finishes below the rim.  
- Prone to going cold, especially in high-pressure games (e.g., 2-9 vs. UNC, 7-25 vs. Kansas).  
- Lacks elite athleticism, a strong dribble handle, and versatility, limiting his ceiling and role as a wing-only player.
weaknesses.57:
- Needs to build muscle and improve physical toughness to withstand stronger opponents.  
- Lacks proper positioning and defensive fundamentals, particularly in post-and-rim (P&R) scenarios.  
- Often delays defensive switches, leading to loss of direct opponent control.  
- Offensively lacks solid post skills despite having good touch and a solid hook shot with both hands.  
- Needs to work on his release, which is currently too slow.
weaknesses.58:
- Poor shooting form with a flat, low-trajectory jumpshot lacking touch, consistency, and fluidity  
- Inability to shoot effectively (17-of-64 from three, 26.6%) and struggles from the free-throw line (64%)  
- Lacks point guard vision, decision-making, and ball-handling, often forcing plays and drawing charges  
- Tends to play hero ball, loses team cohesion, and fails to develop off-the-ball skills or screen-setting  
- Defensive issues including poor ISO numbers, overreaching, and being vulnerable to fakes; needs fundamentals and discipline to improve
weaknesses.59:
- Overly hyped and energetic, leading to turnovers and bad fouls  
- Poor offensively limited to the paint with no shooting range  
- Struggles with game awareness, forces plays, and telegraphs passes  
- Below average passer with a negative assist/turnover ratio  
- Foul-prone, physical on defense, and tends to bite on pump fakes
weaknesses.60:
- Needs to improve passing, tempo control, and half-court play as a floor general  
- Struggles with decision-making in transition and creating offense for teammates  
- Has a high turnover rate (3.8 per game) but a solid assist-to-turnover ratio (1.50)  
- Lacks a reliable three-point shot and needs to develop perimeter shooting  
- Should enhance defensive anticipation, use length to disrupt passing lanes, and become a more vocal leader
weaknesses.61:
- Transitioned from a small forward to a power forward role this season, showing versatility by playing both positions despite lacking true post skills.  
- Displays potential as a dynamic face-up player with good range shooting and ball-handling ability, though technical refinement is needed for NBA-level play.  
- Struggles with free throw consistency (54%), turnovers (2.2 per game), and poor passing efficiency (0.6 apg to 1.7 to), requiring improvement in decision-making and ball handling.  
- Has significant physical advantages (6’11”, leaping ability) but lacks shot-blocking output (0.9 bpg), with potential to double that as a freshman with better defensive awareness.  
- Needs to add bulk, improve contact resistance, enhance rebounding technique, and reduce selfish tendencies when facing the basket.
weaknesses.62:
- Lacks elite explosiveness and a strong first step, making it difficult to blow by athletic opponents.  
- Needs to add significant weight and improve strength to play effectively at the next level.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially when shots don’t fall; shows anxiety and poor decision-making under pressure.  
- Struggles with ball handling, finishing in traffic, and establishing position when posting up smaller players.  
- Poor defensive instincts against explosive wings and on the weakside; lacks court vision and decision-making in pick-and-roll situations.
weaknesses.63:
- Undersized and lacks speed or range to defend the wing effectively  
- Very limited shooting range, with no college pedigree for outside shooting past 12–15 feet  
- Struggles with length and strength on both ends of the floor  
- Slow straight-line speed and lacks blow-by ability against bigger defenders  
- Limited offensive creation beyond offensive rebounds; body type closer to a SF than a PF
weaknesses.64:
- Lacks quickness despite gaining size and strength, limiting his effectiveness in fast-paced play.  
- Minimal ball-handling and playmaking; only 3 assists in 19 games, with little initiative to create for teammates.  
- Struggles defensively as an average rebounder and poor rim protector, often failing to box out bigger opponents.  
- Poor shooting mechanics with a slow, unorthodox release that limits range and shot fluidity.  
- Needs improvement in post play, moves, footwork, and fakes—especially when back to the basket—leading to turnovers.
weaknesses.65:
- Needs to add strength to improve post defense, rebounding, and overall physicality in the paint.  
- Struggles as a post defender due to lack of strength; opponents can back him down effectively.  
- Rebounding ability is inconsistent, with defensive rebounding needing improvement in positioning, boxing out, and anticipation.  
- Efficient at the rim (56% of shots, 60% of baskets), but needs to develop scoring and offensive creation skills, especially in pick-and-roll situations.  
- Shows potential as a 3-point shooter with a quick release, but 29% three-point percentage requires improvement in consistency.
weaknesses.66:
- Majok has not completed a full season and lacks consistent competition, experience, and coaching compared to peers.  
- His shot-blocking judgment is immature, often challenging shots within 8ft of the rim without proper timing.  
- He frequently commits cheap fouls and causes turnovers due to exuberance and loose footwork.  
- Despite being active on both offensive and defensive boards, his size and lack of strength limit his rebounding effectiveness.  
- He struggles with foul trouble in the 4/5 position and needs to improve his off-ball play, including pick-setting and cutting.
weaknesses.67:
- Reliant on athleticism; struggles to improvise when quickness and burst are insufficient  
- Not a natural scorer with limited offensive nuance and poor shot creation  
- Jump shot is inconsistent, both spotting up and off the bounce; only 65% from the free-throw line  
- Poor shot selection in halfcourt; takes too many off-balance, dribble-heavy shots that act as bailouts  
- High turnover rate (3.3 TOPG), careless ball handling, defensive lapses, and tendency to gamble or leave teammates exposed
weaknesses.68:
- Needs to add substantial weight and muscle to reach his true potential  
- Mediocre explosiveness due to limited leaping ability and average first step  
- Struggles to finish in traffic despite a soft touch and inability to absorb contact  
- Easily thrown off balance and forced into difficult shots by stronger defenders  
- Inconsistent shooting, predictable off-hand usage, and reliance on shot fakes lead to poor efficiency and downward game spirals
weaknesses.69:
- Highly ball-dominant and aggressive, but prone to over-aggression, turnovers (2.3 per game in freshman season), and poor shot selection  
- Lacks elite vision and floor generalship, often passing only as a last resort despite being a creative finisher  
- Struggles defensively with limited effort, few steals, and poor competitiveness; limited defensive potential  
- Tends to rush shots, shoot from difficult angles, and has a low free-throw percentage (65.8%) for a player creating high contact  
- Has potential as an electric offensive player but lacks the maturity, consistency, and team-oriented play to be an effective starting point guard in the NBA
weaknesses.70:
- Undersized for a shooting guard position  
- Lacks extreme quickness and necessary skills for lead guard role  
- Tunnel vision; focused solely on getting his own shot  
- Poor decision-making and subpar passing abilities  
- Tends to settle for outside shots instead of driving to the lane and drawing contact
weaknesses.71:
- Lacks elite speed and athleticism, relying on ball-handling and a quick first step to compensate  
- High turnover rate (3.3 per game) and poor free-throw shooting (career 75%) are significant concerns  
- Prefers extended mid-range shots (over 40% of attempts) and often forces passes that lead to turnovers  
- Struggles with defensive consistency, lacks strength off the dribble, and frequently overhelps  
- Has potential to play either guard spot but shows disinterest with the ball and limited offensive efficiency in half-court play
weaknesses.72:
- Limited three-point shooting ability and never attempted one during college  
- Poor passer and offensive creator, with minimal scoring opportunities generated  
- Low rebounding output (less than six per game) despite being the tallest player  
- Lacks athleticism and foot speed, making it difficult to defend perimeter players  
- Inconsistent free throw shooting and limited post-up scoring ability
weaknesses.73:
- Poor free throw shooting (65.3% as a freshman) and lack of consistent finishing in the paint  
- Inconsistent defensive rebounding and poor rotation, often missing key defensive positions  
- Limited basketball IQ, struggles with double teams and decision-making on the court  
- Lacks a reliable post move, poor back-to-the-basket game, and no go-to move at the low block  
- Needs improvement in shooting range, midrange, and face-up game; struggles with ball handling and finishing against taller defenders
weaknesses.74:
- Below average athleticism with poor quickness, leaping ability, and explosiveness compared to NBA standards  
- Lacks foot and lateral speed, making defensive matchups against quicker players challenging  
- Relies heavily on pulling up for scoring, especially when slashing, with limited rim contact at the college level  
- Struggles against quick opponents in space and is often targeted in switches due to limited defensive creativity  
- Conservative defensive style and limited upside as a 24-year-old rookie with a 5-year college career trajectory
weaknesses.75:
- Undersized power forward (6’8") with a large wingspan (7’1") and solid reach (8’9"), but lacks athleticism and fluidity on the floor.  
- Poor rebounding (7.7 rebounds in 34 minutes per game) and limited defensive presence at the rim, including weak rim protection and a lazy stance.  
- Most effective as a stretch five, but lacks the skills to compensate for his height; inefficient scorer with only 33% from three-point range and 38.7% from two-point range.  
- Relies heavily on assists (89% of 3-pointers, 67% of 2-pointers) and rarely creates his own shots; not comfortable creating off the dribble except in straight-line drives.  
- Shows significant stat fluctuations (6.4 ppg to 21.1 ppg), is underweight at 230 lbs, and has limited upside due to age (21 years old) and lack of development potential.
weaknesses.76:
- Lacks assertiveness and shows passivity, though some attribute this to inexperience rather than lack of competitiveness.  
- Struggles with focus and mental toughness, particularly under pressure, but a single poor performance (e.g., 2 points vs. North Carolina) may not reflect his overall ability.  
- Needs to improve ball handling, shot selection, and isolation game while developing better defensive awareness and effort.  
- Has a naive, humble demeanor and requires time to mature, gain confidence, and adjust to NBA lifestyle and pressure.  
- Despite a tough upbringing and academic redshirt, he shows potential for growth and may develop into a more consistent and confident player over time.
weaknesses.77:
- Lacks elite athleticism and burst, limiting isolation effectiveness and change-of-speed ability  
- Struggles with consistency from deep, shooting only 22% from three-point range  
- Free throw shooting is passable but lacks confidence for long-term development  
- Defensive limitations include lack of lateral quickness, footspeed, and off-ball awareness  
- Needs to improve strength, rebounding (averages 2.2 per game), and scoring aggression
weaknesses.78:
- Has a modest vertical jump of 35.5 inches and is more quick than fast, relying on shiftiness to create space.  
- Faced lower-level college competition compared to draft peers, though combine scrimmages suggest he belongs among top contenders.  
- Only two scrimmage performances are available, making evaluation challenging when combined with four years of film.  
- Turns 22 on July 16, which limits long-term upside and potential for elite development.  
- Shows steady improvement in three-point shooting (reaching 40% as a senior), but free throw percentage remains flat and below 70%, raising doubts about his ability to function as a floor spacer.
weaknesses.79:
- Lacks a reliable jumpshot, particularly from spot-up situations, and struggles with balance and shot form.  
- Confidence in his shot contributes to scoring droughts, and his unselfishness can limit scoring opportunities.  
- Should focus on improving his outside shooting, staying balanced, and attacking the rim to draw fouls.  
- While advanced for his age and physically developing, he is not at LeBron’s level in athleticism or leaping ability.  
- Needs to become more aggressive in creating his own shots, improve ball movement, and stay focused amid hype.
weaknesses.80:
- Struggles to create offense consistently off the dribble and lacks reliable self-creation.  
- Defensive intensity and effort are inconsistent, with issues in box outs and poor rebounding.  
- Lateral quickness and anticipation lag behind, making him vulnerable to smaller guards.  
- Tends to get lost on defense due to ball-watching and can be bullied when engaging in box outs.  
- Free-throw percentage dropped to 76% despite improved volume and sound mechanics.
weaknesses.81:
- Lacks offensive versatility, with no effective post game beyond his jump hook and poor fluidity with the ball.  
- Inconsistent shot creation; non-existent jump shot and slow reaction time when contesting shots.  
- Weak right hand usage, poor footwork, lower body strength, and poor hand control leading to bobbled passes.  
- Struggles defensively in switch situations, lacks lateral movement and knee bend, making him vulnerable to quick stretch forwards.  
- Poor free-throw shooting (50% as a senior), limited offensive development potential, and minimal improvement outlook due to age and lack of high-level basketball experience.
weaknesses.82:
- Needs to bulk up significantly, especially in the upper body, to withstand physical defenders and avoid being bullied by bigger wings.  
- Still raw offensively, lacking consistency in shot creation and decision-making; struggles to create his own shot.  
- Ball handling is inconsistent, particularly with his left hand, and lacks control in traffic or during dribbling.  
- Poor performance when the game slows down; tends to disappear offensively and make indecisive passes, especially when driving into traffic.  
- Shooting release varies, mid-range game is unreliable, and spot-up three-point shooting requires more reps to become consistent.
weaknesses.83:
- Offensive game is highly undeveloped and far from NBA readiness, with poor footwork, awareness, and fundamental execution.  
- Lacks confidence in left hand and relies solely on his right hand to finish inside; outside shooting is unpolished.  
- Passing is only average, and overall basketball knowledge and court awareness reflect limited experience.  
- Basic offensive and defensive concepts are not yet automatic; often loses rebounds by jumping uncontrolled instead of boxing out.  
- Poor jumpshot technique (set shot, no clean release, two-foot leaper), low ball carry inside, and slow reaction speed hinder offensive effectiveness.
weaknesses.84:
- Entered Kentucky with high expectations as a lottery pick but fell short due to physical limitations and poor performance.  
- Has a thin frame and lacks strength to finish through contact, often leading to blocked layups and awkward shot attempts.  
- Struggles with decision-making, angles, and creating passing lanes, resulting in turnovers and inefficient shot attempts.  
- Inconsistent shooting (35% from the field, 38.4% from two) and poor confidence, especially in adjusting to defensive pressure.  
- Despite evident shooting talent and court vision, his development as a playmaker and physical durability remains a major concern.
weaknesses.85:
- Doesn't always perform at his full potential despite his capabilities.  
- Lacks consistency in dominating games as a center.  
- Needs to develop a stronger "killer instinct" to sustain intensity throughout games.  
- Not a strong shot blocker, but has solid leaping ability.  
- Can improve as a defender with focused effort and practice.
weaknesses.86:
- Needs improvement in body control, particularly in stepping in front of smaller defenders to take charges.  
- Should develop the ability to step out and shoot three-pointers at a consistent rate.  
- On fast breaks, tends to hesitate for alley-oop opportunities instead of cutting to the basket for layups.  
- Often leaves teammates without the ball when they expect him to drive hard to the hoop.  
- Highly confident, which can lead to flashy plays over safer, more reliable scoring options.
weaknesses.87:
- Unheralded recruit with limited playing time; currently a bench player averaging 19 mpg through consistent, mistake-free performance.  
- Shows significant potential but faces competition from more senior players on the depth chart, raising concerns about a one-and-done decision.  
- Needs to improve off-the-ball movement, stay in motion, and develop better timing when setting up defenders for screens.  
- At 185 lbs, could benefit from gaining 10–15 lbs through an NBA-style weight training program to increase strength, balance, and ability to finish at the rim.  
- Currently shoots 65% from the free-throw line; his shooting mechanics suggest he can improve to 80%+ with proper development.
weaknesses.88:
- Fundamentally sound but lacks explosiveness and elite athleticism  
- Needs to improve post moves and footwork around the basket  
- Projects as a power forward but lacks a developed post game  
- Strong defender on the perimeter but weak in post defense and shot blocking  
- Ceiling is limited compared to other big man prospects due to rawness and lack of post versatility
weaknesses.89:
- Lacks athleticism, speed, and explosiveness, with underwhelming vertical leap (32 inches) and poor lane agility  
- Struggles with ball handling, creating opportunities, and finishing at the rim, especially against contact  
- Prone to turnovers (A/TO ratio of 0.7 to 1.6) and lacks ball security, dribbling, and decision-making  
- Physically undersized and unbalanced; lacks strength, toughness, and instincts to compete with NBA frontcourt players  
- Limited upside and game feel; needs significant development in strength, speed, and defensive awareness to succeed at the NBA level
weaknesses.90:
- Lacks elite explosiveness and a quick first step, limiting his ability to create shots from the dribble in isolation.  
- Struggles with decision-making under pressure, especially when facing press defense.  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly his pull-up jumper in pick-and-roll situations, with a streaky and unreliable scoring touch.  
- Unable to change game rhythm off the bench, which limits his fit for an NBA role requiring impact in key moments.  
- Poor lateral speed on defense, making it difficult to match up against quicker, smaller guards.
weaknesses.91:
- Needs to improve overall physical strength and finish through contact against pro-level defenders  
- Frequent fouls due to late closeouts or upright positioning (averages over 3 per game)  
- Lacks quick-twitch lateral burst, limiting effectiveness in switch-heavy defensive schemes  
- Defensive awareness is inconsistent, with occasional delays on rotations and slow recognition of back cuts  
- Struggles with decision-making under pressure, turnover-prone (2.4 TOPG), and needs to speed up shooting release for consistency
weaknesses.92:
- He is an average athlete by NBA standards.  
- He lacks lateral quickness, making it difficult to defend NBA shooting guards and small forwards.  
- He doesn't excel in any specific offensive area, limiting his overall effectiveness.  
- At 22 years old, his growth and development potential is limited compared to other players.  
- His upside is constrained by his current skill set and age.
weaknesses.93:
- Dubljevic is a skilled power forward but lacks athleticism.  
- He struggles against stronger, faster defenders, especially when guarding the center or faster players.  
- His low rebounding numbers (3.7 RPG in ACB, 4.8 RPG in EuroCup) reflect his limited physicality.  
- He lacks speed, both in running down the floor and lateral movement, affecting his defensive performance.  
- He is best suited for a half-court, slower-paced team due to his defensive limitations.
weaknesses.94:
- Suffered a stress fracture in his left foot, playing in only 9 games at Oregon and raising durability concerns.  
- Has a wiry build (226 lbs), limited strength, and struggles with contact, making it difficult to withstand pressure around the basket.  
- Poor hustle and intensity; often the last player up the floor, lacking urgency and defensive recovery.  
- Inconsistent shot release due to a low release point, allowing smaller defenders to contest shots—needs to improve this.  
- Requires significant development in post-game skills, passing, and defensive footwork, with questions about competitiveness and work ethic.
weaknesses.95:
- Beal is of average height for an NBA shooting guard at 6'4" with a solid but not outstanding 6'7" wingspan.  
- Struggles with shot blocking, ball-handling, and getting to the rim in traffic, especially when defending against defenders.  
- Inconsistent shooting from beyond the arc, with only a 34% field goal percentage and early-season struggles.  
- Prefers to pull up for jumpers rather than finish around the rim, limiting his effectiveness in close-quarters situations.  
- Has the talent to be a long-term starter, especially on teams with a strong point guard and established scorers.
weaknesses.96:
- Dawson's performance varies significantly within a single game, dropping off when not engaged, leading to poor shooting and reduced impact.  
- Despite being a key offensive contributor, he has scored 7 or fewer points 15 times in two seasons.  
- Physically, he is a 6'7" power forward with limited strength, lateral quickness, and poor shooting range—no three-pointers made in college.  
- His defensive limitations include slow acceleration, poor lateral agility (lowest lane agility at combine), and inability to effectively guard small forwards or defend pick-and-roll plays.  
- He is a below-average free throw shooter (49% this season) and lacks a reliable post game, limiting his effectiveness as an NBA power forward.
weaknesses.97:
- Lacks speed and a strong first step, making it difficult to create shot opportunities against defenders  
- Has a respectable 39-inch vertical but does not exhibit overt athleticism on the court  
- Struggled to break into the rotation at Illinois before excelling at Santa Clara as a sophomore  
- Limited physical attributes: short wingspan (6'5.5") and standing reach (8'0.5"), below NBA standards for a shooting guard  
- Inconsistent shooting efficiency (below 80% from the free-throw line) and questions about his ability to operate off the ball or as a primary ball handler
weaknesses.98:
- Lacks ideal size and strength for post play with a 6'8.25" frame and only 207 lbs at the Combine  
- Has a limited and inconsistent jump shot, shooting just 27% from three-point range and struggling when not wide open  
- Poor free throw shooting (69%) and limited shooting range make him a potential liability at the next level  
- Inadequate ball handling and careless play can lead to turnovers and discipline issues, especially against NBA-level defenders  
- Needs to improve playmaking, shooting range, and defensive discipline to be a versatile and reliable offensive option
weaknesses.99:
- Extremely frail, weighed 196 lbs at the 2015 Nike Hoop Summit  
- Needs to develop body, strength, and lateral quickness to handle physical play  
- Poor defensive skills, including weak footwork and frequent out-of-place positioning  
- Lacks explosive athleticism and can appear slow or lazy in gameplay  
- Has potential to improve as a playmaker and develop into a vocal leader
weaknesses.100:
- Inconsistent jumpshot, especially in half-court, allows defenders to clog lanes and play off him  
- Poor ball handling and carelessness lead to turnovers; lacks control of tempo and plays at full throttle constantly  
- Strong preference for left-handed finishing, often switching to weak hand, exposing the ball to defenders  
- Physically frail frame (20–25 lbs too light) limits ability to drive, absorb contact, and compete against stronger guards  
- Needs to build muscle mass and improve decision-making, shot selection, and defensive positioning to succeed at the college level
weaknesses.101:
- Ball handling is weak, with a high dribble that is easy to defend and a lack of intuitive passing.  
- Prone to turnovers, especially when attempting ambitious or "home run" passes; often takes conservative approaches.  
- Shooting efficiency is low (42.3% from the floor) and highly streaky, with inconsistent performance.  
- Limited vertical leaping ability and lack of strength at 177 lbs make him vulnerable to stronger guards in the NBA.  
- Struggles with scoring in the paint, particularly with floater shots, and often forces offensive plays instead of adapting to defense.
weaknesses.102:
- Lacks burst, explosiveness, and slashing ability, making it difficult to separate from defenders or finish at the rim.  
- Inefficient scorer with poor 3-point consistency (27.6% from three) and struggles to convert inside the arc (46% FG).  
- Flat shooting mechanics; could benefit from adding more arc and better shot selection.  
- Limited offensive presence in half-court offense and lacks strength to post up effectively against NBA-level defenders.  
- Good defensive versatility and emotional intensity, but needs muscle to defend bigger players and improve ball-handling in traffic.
weaknesses.103:
- Will need to build strength and bulk to match up at the next level, despite his natural frame and college success.  
- Struggles to develop as a consistent floor spacer, relying heavily on inside scoring and showing reluctance to shoot outside the paint.  
- Must improve court vision, passing, and offensive versatility to function as a perimeter playmaker beyond the paint.  
- Strong rebounder at the college level, but needs to work on boxing out opponents and relying less on length and quickness alone.  
- Defensive issues include poor close-out technique, being caught on pump fakes, lack of low-stance defense, and difficulty guarding quicker or stronger players.
weaknesses.104:
- Has an unconventional physical build with average height and length for NBA standards, despite a wider frame, and is more powerful than athletic.  
- Not especially quick or light on his feet; leaping ability and speed are modest and suited for a "build-up" style, making him primarily a below-the-rim player in halfcourt situations.  
- Defense suffers from poor fundamentals, awareness, and off-ball positioning, leading to defensive lapses, easy shot attempts, and team defensive collapses.  
- Struggles against height and length, especially in defending perimeter shooters and post-ups, with a low defensive rating (0.4 BPG) and limited impact on turnovers (0.5 SPG).  
- Limited passing (1.2 APG), relies heavily on personal shots, shows tunnel vision, and requires close monitoring of conditioning and diet due to body type vulnerability.
weaknesses.105:
- Bronny James is undersized for an NBA off guard (6’1.5” barefoot) and lacks the experience and skill to play either guard position effectively.  
- He has good vision and smart decision-making but lacks experience as a primary point guard and has not demonstrated ability to play lead guard.  
- Defensive tools are present, but he lacks the ferocity and tenacity expected from a player with his skill set.  
- Offensively, he is not a high-volume scorer, lacks slashing ability, and has limited diversity in scoring; he tends to "fit in" rather than dominate.  
- His standout potential lies in developing as a reliable outside shooter and/or a monster defender, though his 3-point shooting consistency and free throw accuracy need improvement.
weaknesses.106:
- Limited offensive ceiling due to lack of burst, dynamic handle, and shooting consistency  
- Shot only 26.6% from three in 2024–25, showing poor spacing and spot-up shooting accuracy  
- Stiff mechanics and slow release hinder effectiveness as a floor spacer  
- Struggles to initiate pick-and-rolls or create separation off the dribble due to lack of pace and shiftiness  
- Defensive value depends on system fit, effort, and adaptability to NBA pace and spacing
weaknesses.107:
- Struggled with shooting efficiency, making only 7 three-pointers in 46 attempts and shooting just 62% from the free throw line.  
- Inconsistent shooting fundamentals, including mediocre touch, varying releases, and poor performance under pressure.  
- Not a significant scoring threat; relies heavily on length and strength rather than refined skill.  
- Shows limited comfort driving to his off-hand and lacks reliable touch around the rim when contested.  
- Inconsistent performance throughout the season, with a noticeable dropoff from his freshman year and signs of rushing his pro decision due to age.
weaknesses.108:
- Bruno has limited NBA readiness due to a short career played primarily in Brazil.  
- He lacks proven experience, having played in minor leagues with few minutes and low-level competition.  
- His game is currently suited more for a power forward, with limited ball-handling skills needed to transition to small forward.  
- He must develop intensity, aggressiveness, strength, and defensive skills (rebounding and lateral movement) to compete at the NBA level.  
- Despite challenges, Bruno is a hardworking, physically gifted player who requires several years of development to reach NBA potential.
weaknesses.109:
- Still developing against top-level competition, with offensive potential but inconsistent performance  
- Averaging 15 points per game, up from 10 as a freshman, but remains below elite scoring levels  
- Improved ball handling, though still prone to turnovers and slow in recognizing double teams  
- Limited shooting range (max 12–15 feet); lacks a reliable three-point shot despite promising form  
- Solid passing for a big man (2 assists per game), but needs to improve shot blocking and rim protection
weaknesses.110:
- Lacks strength, toughness, and base strength, leading to poor shot execution and off-balance attempts.  
- Struggles with shot selection (40% FG as a freshman), often settling for questionable shots early in the shot clock.  
- Inconsistent offensive efficiency and defensive engagement; prone to turnovers (1.9 TO/1.8 AST) and getting bottled up by physical defenders.  
- Poor defensive fundamentals, including lack of stance, high center of gravity, and inability to contain dribble penetration or backdoor plays.  
- Needs significant improvement in shooting efficiency (currently 27% 3FG) and shot decision-making to justify his volume and develop into a reliable NBA prospect.
weaknesses.111:
- Buddy is short for a shooting guard, limiting his physical advantage in the NBA despite improvements in handling and offensive versatility.  
- He has significantly reduced his three-point shooting percentage (now under 50%) and focuses more on driving to the hoop to keep defenders off balance.  
- Buddy still lacks development in his mid-range game, shooting only 29.6% from two-point range in 2013–14.  
- His offensive game is heavily reliant on three-pointers, leading to inconsistent performances (e.g., 7-for-7 vs. 1-for-8 in a single game).  
- While he has become a very complete player through self-improvement, his reliance on long-range shooting risks pigeonholing him as a specialist.
weaknesses.112:
- Cunningham is a physically gifted but not explosive athlete with limited lateral quickness and speed, lacking the explosiveness to be a dynamic scorer or leaper.  
- Struggles with footwork, often making wild closeouts or wasted steps, which can lead to turnovers or easy steals.  
- In offensive situations, he frequently forces passes through multiple defenders, telegraphs his intentions, or drives aimlessly, leading to turnovers and offensive inefficiencies.  
- A below-the-rim finisher with limited ability to elevate and score in traffic; relies on body shielding rather than authoritative finishes.  
- Shows promise as a scoring threat from three (35% 3PT at Nike EYBL), but consistency is lacking, with poor shooting performance in key tournaments.
weaknesses.114:
- Lacks fluidity, explosiveness, and run-and-jump athleticism as an athlete  
- Poor shot-creation abilities and inconsistent ball-handling, especially off the dribble  
- Inconsistent production; flashes of brilliance but often underwhelming performance  
- Slow shot release and inefficient perimeter shooting due to mechanics  
- Struggles with consistency, endurance, and rebounding despite being physically sized for his position
weaknesses.115:
- Lacks great speed and foot speed, making it difficult to defend on the perimeter  
- Struggles with body fat, which weighs him down despite working on his physique  
- Not a rim protector due to lack of explosiveness for consistent shot blocking  
- Has good length but is limited by height and lack of athleticism as a center  
- Physical attributes significantly restrict his potential at the NBA level
weaknesses.116:
- Lacks significant strength and muscle, making him vulnerable to physical contact on both ends of the court.  
- Not an explosive or shifty athlete; relies on long strides and buildup speed rather than quick changes of direction.  
- Poor finisher at the rim with a low field goal percentage inside the arc (42%) and limited attempts to score in the paint.  
- Avoids contact and plays cautiously, with low free throw attempts (2 FTA/G) and a tendency to settle for jumpers, reducing overall efficiency.  
- Struggles defensively due to a thin frame and lack of lateral quickness, making him ineffective against both bigger wings and smaller guards.
weaknesses.117:
- Limited foot speed and lack of size make it difficult to defend against NBA athletes.  
- Struggles to create shots off the dribble and is not effective on the ball defensively.  
- Better as an off-ball spot-up shooter; relies heavily on open shots.  
- History of injuries, including hip surgery, has impacted his development and availability.  
- At 24 years old, his upside is limited, and a role player role is likely the best fit.
weaknesses.118:
- Struggles with efficient shot creation, often relying on physical ability rather than ball-handling or court vision  
- Inconsistent offensive execution; frequently slashes into the lane without a clear plan, leading to turnovers or poor shots  
- Limited shot variety, relying mostly on 3-pointers or driving, with little use of pull-up floater or mid-range options  
- Poor free-throw shooting (45.7% EYBL, 62.5% U18 Americas) and low usage (2.5 FTA/G), with only 64 attempts in 26 games  
- Lacks playmaking, with below-average assist-to-turnover ratio and minimal passing, raising concerns about team chemistry and defensive awareness
weaknesses.119:
- Born in 1990, he will turn 24 later this season, which significantly reduces his upside.  
- Below-average athlete by NBA standards, particularly below the rim.  
- College success came from overpowering opponents, but this may not translate to the professional level.  
- Relatively inexperienced, having played a major role only in his senior season with the Lobos.  
- Struggles with game awareness, prone to tunnel vision offensively and poor performance on the fast break.
weaknesses.120:
- Johnson is a redshirt senior who turned 23 in early March, indicating he is past the typical draft age for first-round consideration.  
- At best an average athlete with lacking speed and leaping ability, he struggles with perimeter defense and contesting shots.  
- Limited in post play, he rarely finishes in the paint and settles for jump shots, showing poor aggression and low free-throw attempt volume.  
- Averages only 2.4 assists and is not a playmaker, excelling primarily off catch-and-shoot opportunities.  
- Physically limited at 210 pounds and 6'9", with poor rebounding and durability concerns, making him unsuited for NBA physical demands.
weaknesses.121:
- Has a thin body frame and lacks strength, limiting his physical advantages  
- Poor explosiveness off the dribble and weak first step hinder offensive effectiveness  
- Struggles with jumping, finishing, and shooting in traffic; lacks rim protection  
- Tends to take difficult, low-percentage shots and settle for floaters instead of finishing at the rim  
- Inconsistent decision-making, turnover-prone, and struggles in pick-and-rolls due to inability to fight through screens
weaknesses.122:
- Struggled with consistency and competitiveness at Duke, often deferring to teammates and lacking assertiveness.  
- Poor shot selection, tendency to settle for jumpers, and weak first step limit his ability to create scoring opportunities.  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting, with below-average efficiency (28.6% from three in EYBL) and streaky performance patterns.  
- Limited athletic ability; relies on handles and ball-handling rather than explosiveness or quickness; struggles in tight spaces and with balance on pull-ups.  
- Needs improvement in leadership, intensity, work ethic, and ball-handling control, particularly in high-pressure or contested situations.
weaknesses.123:
- Lacks well-rounded skills, primarily focused on scoring with limited contributions in playmaking or defensive effort.  
- Has average physical and athletic traits for a backcourt prospect, lacking speed and consistency in getting to the rim.  
- Frequently faces shot-blocking, especially near the rim, with limited offensive presence above the rim.  
- Poor shot selection, relying heavily on low-percentage, contested shots; inconsistent 3-point shooting with a tendency to go through slumps.  
- Minimal passing, often acts as a "black hole" offensively, and does not demonstrate leadership or defensive intensity.
weaknesses.124:
- Has undergone three surgeries on the same foot area within 22 months and has a history of foot/ankle injuries.  
- Struggles with strength due to a skinny frame, making him vulnerable to stronger guards and limiting his ability to finish inside.  
- Tends to settle for midrange shots, shooting under 30% between the paint and 3-point line last season.  
- Inconsistent in half-court playmaking and defense, often jumping passing lanes and lacking defensive technique.  
- Needs to establish himself as a catch-and-shoot player; his performance this season will determine if he becomes a lottery pick or fails to live up to his potential.
weaknesses.125:
- Lacks a strong first step and separation from defenders, making it difficult to get by opponents.  
- Prefers pulling up or shooting over challenging defenders at the rim, partly due to lack of strength and leaping ability.  
- Struggles with shot selection and consistency, especially against elite defenders, with shooting performance declining mid-season.  
- Has solid lower body strength and muscle mass but needs improved upper body strength to handle stronger opponents.  
- Still developing as a point guard; performance is hampered by limited opportunities and a lack of elite athleticism and speed.
weaknesses.126:
- Rudimentary offensive game with limited ball creation, relying mostly on inside or outside shooting  
- Lacks a reliable midrange game and struggles with finishing outside the paint  
- Shows better potential in transition and fast-paced offenses compared to half-court situations  
- Low free throw percentage (66.4% in senior year) with a significant decline from prior seasons  
- Prone to mistakes under pressure and can accumulate fouls due to aggressive defensive play
weaknesses.128:
- Struggles with consistent scoring and playing time, averaging just 6.5 PPG as a freshman with limited shot volume.  
- Inconsistent offensive approach, frequently disappearing for stretches and lacking reliable shot selection.  
- Needs improvement in decision-making, ball-handling in traffic, and ability to create shots off the dribble.  
- Free throw percentage (69.5%) and low free throw attempts suggest room for development as a pure shooter.  
- Questions remain about effort, toughness, and competitiveness, with limited impact when not scoring.
weaknesses.129:
- Productivity and efficiency (11.7 ppg, 34.6% 3P%) were below expectations for a talented player.  
- Athleticism was less evident at Lexington compared to high school, with limited explosiveness.  
- Struggles in creating his own shot opportunities; relies on catch-and-shoot, not natural three-point creation.  
- Limited free throw opportunities and occasional struggles from the line, despite a 76% shooting percentage.  
- Needs to diversify offensive attacks, improve consistency from three-point range, and reduce turnovers—especially in traffic.
weaknesses.130:
- Scores most of his baskets in transition and on catch-and-shoot opportunities.  
- Lacks strong dribbling, handling, and passing abilities with limited vision and poor court awareness.  
- Struggles with isolation plays and creating offense off the dribble; needs improvement in iso ability.  
- Physically limited as a wing with only a 6'7" wingspan and solid but not standout defensive reach.  
- Shows inconsistency in shooting, especially during a stretch in December/January, and can be overly aggressive defensively.
weaknesses.131:
- Winston lacks NBA-level athleticism, standing at 6'1" and 190 lbs with limited speed and explosiveness.  
- He struggles with finishing at the rim and defending against other point guards due to his physical limitations.  
- His body type is described as unathletic, limiting his physical advantages in competitive settings.  
- He is prone to turnovers, which have at times cost his team games despite his cerebral play.  
- His shooting release is slow and easier to defend, and he lacks effectiveness in open-court play or on-ball defense.
weaknesses.132:
- Struggles with muscular structure, making him vulnerable to contact during attacks.  
- Lacks an explosive first step and reliable crossover dribble, limiting shot creation in isolation.  
- Needs to improve shooting mechanics, adding fluidity and quickness to his release.  
- Has a weak midrange game, with an unreliable pull-up jumper compared to his performance in the paint or beyond three.  
- Lacks elite lateral quickness, reducing effectiveness as a 1-on-1 defender, especially against faster players.  
- Ankle injury caused him to miss the latter part of the season.
weaknesses.133:
- Not an explosive athlete and struggles to create separation or elevate in traffic against NBA-level defenders.  
- Primarily scores within rhythm and lacks consistent ability to blow by defenders or generate paint pressure alone.  
- Slight turnover rate (2.2 per game) and limited natural lead playmaking; more of a connector than a creator.  
- Defensively, has length to help, but needs improvement in lateral quickness and recovery when switching on quicker guards.  
- Played only six games in 2024–25 due to a season-ending shoulder injury, limiting evaluation of progression.
weaknesses.134:
- Needs to gain weight and strength; 200 pounds is too light for his size and age, impacting guarding of 4’s and some 3’s.  
- Shows streaky performance, with inconsistent scoring (30 points in some games, 10 in others).  
- AST:TO just above 1.0, indicating poor ball handling and decision-making, especially at the next level.  
- Lacks a reliable mid-range game and shoots only 34% from two-point range; needs improvement in three-point shooting and defense.  
- Primarily a spot-up shooter (78% of threes assisted), lacks explosive jumpers, and must improve decision-making with and without the ball.
weaknesses.135:
- Not very aggressive as a scorer; prefers to operate within the offense and take what the defense gives him.  
- Highly unselfish for a player with his offensive abilities.  
- Lacks explosiveness around the rim and is an average athlete.  
- Only an average defender; prone to being beaten off the dribble and struggles against athletic small forwards.  
- Poor free-throw shooter, hitting just 55.7% in his senior season.
weaknesses.136:
- Needs to improve shooting consistency, especially from three-point range and with his off-hand (left) shot.  
- Struggles with ball handling, decision-making, and footwork, often rushing plays and getting off balance.  
- Lacks defensive versatility, particularly in defending pick and rolls and on the perimeter; prone to turnovers and unnecessary fouls.  
- Has significant room to develop post moves, finesse, and court vision, with current game relying heavily on athleticism and overpowering defenders.  
- Shows high upside and potential for growth, but remains raw, inconsistent, and undeveloped, with questions about age and readiness for the next level.
weaknesses.137:
- Not a true point guard; relied on ball-dominance rather than playmaking  
- Struggles defensively, especially off the dribble and against elite competition  
- Limited defensive potential due to average athleticism and lack of physicality  
- Played in a mid-major conference (CAA) with limited exposure to top-tier competition  
- Offensive workload was high, but most games were against non-RPI top 100 teams, reducing credibility of stats
weaknesses.138:
- Lacks great lateral quickness and foot speed defensively  
- Feel for the game and ball movement are still developing  
- Strong in transition and improving in half-court set plays  
- Needs to become a better rebounder, especially defensively  
- Midrange game requires refinement; still learning timing and off-the-ball movement
weaknesses.139:
- Unorthodox shooting form with a low release point, effective at the college level but potentially more easily contested in the NBA.  
- Struggled to create scoring opportunities, especially against elite defenses like Houston’s, limiting clean looks.  
- Lacks elite quickness or burst and can be neutralized by longer, faster defenders off the dribble.  
- Primarily a scorer with limited playmaking (1.5 APG) and does not project as a lead guard at the next level.  
- Slightly undersized for an NBA shooting guard, lacks verticality, and may struggle defending bigger wings; age and lack of proven performance against NBA-level speed and length are concerns.
weaknesses.140:
- Poor offensive decision-making with only 1 assist and 17 turnovers despite a 21.7% usage rate.  
- Limited shot creation and lack of ball movement, often making lazy passes due to poor adjustment to game pace.  
- Solid free throw percentage (54.7%) but inconsistent shot mechanics and a hitch in shot movement.  
- Lacks a developed low post or back-to-the-basket game; relies heavily on physical tools and natural ability.  
- Needs significant strength development and physical maturity to become a viable NBA contributor.
weaknesses.141:
- Lacks physical strength (195 lbs), with insufficient upper and lower body strength that limits agility, mobility, and durability.  
- Struggles to maintain balance under contact, especially when driving or in the post, and turns the ball over in traffic.  
- Inconsistent performance against elite competition; limited production in high-pressure games and low three-point volume (1.3 per game).  
- Poor free-throw shooting in college (71%), though projected to be an 80% shooter in the NBA due to form and touch.  
- While mobile and has length, lacks the quickest feet for perimeter defense and has room to develop his post game.
weaknesses.142:
- Needs to improve consistency with jump hooks and other offensive moves, especially when contested.  
- Struggles to finish through contact and in traffic; lacks physicality inside the paint.  
- Must better read defenses and make timely decisions, avoiding forcing shots when no opportunity exists.  
- Tends to take extra steps after pivoting, leading to travel violations; needs to establish position and stay on the floor before moving.  
- Requires improvement in left-handed finishes, head control, and overall maturity, with concerns over reckless behavior and professionalism.
weaknesses.143:
- Limited offensive capabilities with poor floor stretching, post moves, and lack of speed or footwork  
- Plays upright, prone to getting off balance, and struggles with finishing due to lack of explosiveness  
- Poor free throw shooter with low usage at the line and limited shot attempts per game  
- High turnover rate and poor decision-making as a passer, often gambling with the ball  
- Medical concern: diagnosed with Wolf-Parkinson-White syndrome requiring surgery and ongoing monitoring
weaknesses.145:
- Duarte's advanced age at 23 limits his potential as a draft-eligible player.  
- He averages only 2.3 assists per game, showing limited playmaking ability.  
- Limited athleticism, including lack of speed and vertical leap, hinders his effectiveness as a defender and shot creator.  
- His scoring and defensive contributions provide a solid floor, but his ceiling is constrained by physical limitations.  
- He averaged nearly as many turnovers as assists, indicating a need to improve ball security to play as a playmaker.
weaknesses.146:
- Lacks offensive versatility, relying primarily on rim finishes and three-point shots with limited mid-range options.  
- Poor ball-handling and lack of creativity; limited wiggle, no off-the-dribble moves, and tendency to stick the ball.  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting (23–29% in key tournaments) and poor efficiency, often settling for deep shots.  
- Slow, two-footed drives with limited ability to create separation or finish efficiently at the rim.  
- Needs improvement in lateral quickness, defensive commitment, and consistency in decision-making and scoring.
weaknesses.147:
- Needs to improve his post play, shot selection, and ability to fight for position on both ends of the floor.  
- Lacks effective back-to-the-basket moves and needs to develop better offensive counters near the basket.  
- Ball handling, release point on jump shots, and shooting consistency require significant development.  
- Rebounding fundamentals are weak; needs to improve boxing out and body positioning to be more effective on the boards.  
- Struggles with awareness, focus, communication, and leadership, and is prone to fouls due to lack of strength and concentration.
weaknesses.148:
- Lacks smooth ballhandling and consistent shot creation due to poor ball control.  
- Not a great scorer; relies heavily on physical strength rather than touch or feel around the basket.  
- Has size and length to score backdoor but is hindered by poor footwork and body control.  
- Inconsistent shooter, especially when moving or off the dribble; often shoots without squaring his body.  
- Poor balance and awkward movement due to being top-heavy and having skinny legs; free throw percentage improved but still subpar.
weaknesses.149:
- Inconsistent as a three-point shooter, despite being a reliable spot-up shooter with decent efficiency in limited minutes.  
- Solid free-throw shooting (73–74%) but not elite, showing he lacks elite shot-making consistency.  
- Often lacks confidence, passes up open shots, and can be unselfish to the point of being a liability in offensive flow.  
- Lacks a mid-range game and struggles with defensive versatility due to minimal length and poor ability to create off the dribble.  
- Mediocre ball-handling and no standout perimeter skills, with limited offensive creativity or facilitation at the professional level.
weaknesses.150:
- Lacks NBA-level experience and fundamentals, especially offensively  
- Struggles to beat defenders off the dribble and is overly predictable in his driving motion (mostly to his right)  
- Relies too heavily on jumping ability for rebounds; needs better positioning and fundamentals  
- Inconsistent jump-shot and 3-point shooting; must develop reliability as an outside shooter  
- Defensively relies on athleticism rather than fundamentals and needs significant improvement
weaknesses.151:
- Lacks offensive versatility, primarily a lob threat or rim runner with limited scoring ability beyond the rim.  
- Struggles when away from the basket, lacking touch and consistency on mid-range or three-point shots.  
- Inconsistent defensively, often plays too upright against outside guards, leading to fouls and open driving lanes.  
- Limited ability to execute crossovers or misdirection in space, making him vulnerable to switch-heavy defenses.  
- Prone to fouls (2.8 FPG as a junior) and overreacts to pump fakes, though showed improvement this season; development potential remains uncertain.
weaknesses.154:
- Lacks speed in the open floor and foot speed, hindering both offensive and defensive performance  
- Struggles to create scoring opportunities; relies heavily on catch-and-shoot opportunities off screens  
- Takes a high number of contested jump shots; shot selection needs significant improvement  
- Unorthodox playing style; performs better when he has time to gather himself and is closer to the basket  
- Limited defensive and offensive reach due to a 6’7 wingspan and 8’4.5” standing reach, which restricts his ability to defend and contest shots effectively
weaknesses.155:
- Profiles as a natural scoring, combo guard with a primary role at point guard due to his 6'3 frame.  
- Has a 2.7:2.4 assist-to-turnover ratio, indicating weaknesses in ball distribution and team facilitation.  
- Lacks explosive quickness or leaping ability, relying more on court craft and change of pace.  
- Three-point shooting shows potential (32–34%) with good mechanics, but needs refinement.  
- Defensive limitations include poor footwork, lack of fluid lateral movement, and a weak isolation defense.
weaknesses.156:
- Limited offensive production with poor scoring ability and minimal threat as a driver in the NBA  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from long range, with a tendency to settle and lack offensive versatility  
- Struggles from the free-throw line and rarely gets to the line  
- Defensive awareness and tenacity are questionable, raising concerns about NBA translation  
- Physical limitations including a slight build, foot stress fracture history, and a lack of elite ball-handling ability
weaknesses.157:
- Needs to improve off-the-dribble game, avoiding tight quarters and lacking creativity  
- Poor decision-making with the ball in hand (2 turnovers) and limited passing feel  
- Steady range shooting (35%) but should not attempt five threes per game in college  
- Physically limited in post defense due to size and tendency to lose focus when not primary defender  
- Has strong skills but key limitations label him a "tweener"; physical upside capped at 23 years old
weaknesses.158:
- Lacks basketball IQ and often appears lost, making poor decisions during plays without understanding the game situation.  
- Shows lack of aggressiveness on both ends of the court, frequently playing passively behind opponents in the post and failing to defend properly.  
- Defensive technique is flawed, with poor stance and fundamental weaknesses that allow opponents to easily finish over him, especially on the block.  
- Struggles with strength and positioning around the basket, easily pushed by smaller players and failing to secure rebounds despite his size and athleticism.  
- Needs to rebuild shooting mechanics and develop a consistent post move, using his strength to create reliable offensive opportunities.
weaknesses.159:
- More effective as a scorer than a playmaker, though he can improve as a playmaker and decision maker  
- Good athlete with solid court vision but lacks explosive leaping ability and a standout physical frame  
- Struggles with shot selection, tunnel vision, and frequently passes up open teammates for forced shots  
- Tends to commit turnovers (2.7 per game in freshman season, 2.4 in EYBL) and needs to improve shot selection and free throw shooting  
- Limited rebounding, low release point, and difficulty finishing at the rim due to lack of strength and frame
weaknesses.161:
- Adding muscle, especially in the lower body, could significantly improve athleticism and on-court performance.  
- Has decent build-up speed but lacks static explosiveness, limiting effectiveness in half-court scenarios.  
- Struggles to create space off the bounce without screens and shows mediocre creativity and first-step quickness.  
- Not a natural scorer; hesitant shot selection and limited shot attempts (13 FG in any game as a freshman) reflect a cautious approach.  
- Lacks physicality, has poor rebounding for a 6'8" player (3 RPG), and shows defensive weaknesses due to a high center of gravity and limited turnover generation.
weaknesses.162:
- Not a strong shot blocker or defensive presence; lacks vertical explosiveness and post strength  
- Moves are often predictable, leading to blocked shots and limited offensive effectiveness on the block  
- Unselfish and humble to a fault, which hinders his potential to become a superstar or vocal leader  
- Struggled defensively against length and zone defenses, particularly in the tournament; showed limited improvement in sophomore season  
- Has potential as the most talented of the Zeller brothers but lacks a fiery personality and consistent offensive development
weaknesses.163:
- Below average leaping ability and speed limit separation against elite athletes.  
- Good shooter from distance (over 40% early in JR season), but inconsistent production (75 threes over 86 games, 34.4% shooting).  
- Struggles in isolation situations and has low efficiency on pull-ups; poor free-throw percentage (dropped from 76% to 65%).  
- Limited dribble creation and poor ability to finish around the rim due to lack of explosiveness and quickness.  
- Questionable ability to function as a point guard against faster, more athletic opponents; not an offensive-minded scorer.
weaknesses.164:
- Length and agility make him effective despite not being an explosive or freakish athlete.  
- Unorthodox shooting style appears inefficient but is actually efficient; has strong short to mid-range development.  
- Even-keeled demeanor like Tim Duncan, with competitiveness when challenged.  
- Shows speed in open floor, though lateral defensive speed is slow for a center.  
- Needs to bulk up, become more physical, and develop a stronger on-court presence to compete at the NBA level.
weaknesses.165:
- Struggles with shot selection and decision-making, leading to inefficient shooting (38% FG, 34% 3PT) and inconsistent performance, especially against stronger opponents.  
- Overdribbles frequently, is turnover-prone (averaging 3.5 assists to 3.9 turnovers), and lacks natural playmaking ability, making it difficult to function as a true floor general.  
- Has poor free throw shooting (75% as a freshman, down from 90% in high school) and a slow release, making him ineffective against closeouts despite confidence in deep shooting.  
- Shows defensive potential with many steals but tends to gamble, often making poor defensive choices like going underneath or staying too tight.  
- Possesses high-level scoring ability and confidence, but must develop consistency, efficiency, and leadership to transition from a scorer to a reliable lead guard at the NBA level.
weaknesses.166:
- Undersized at 6’6.5” barefoot with limited vertical explosiveness and lateral quickness, hindering his ability to finish over defenders or defend quicker forwards.  
- Offensive game relies heavily on physicality; struggles with consistency, shooting efficiency (26.5% from three, 66.7% at the rim), and limited perimeter scoring.  
- 75% of shot attempts come at the rim, with low conversion rate, raising concerns about effectiveness against NBA-level rim protection.  
- Struggles to create in space, lacks reliable shooting mechanics, and remains dependent on bully-ball tactics rather than offensive polish.  
- Turnover-prone (2.4 per game) and lacks vision/ball-handling, limiting potential as a playmaker; long-term success hinges on developing a consistent three-point shot.
weaknesses.167:
- Sexton has explosive athleticism but needs to add finesse and consistency in his shooting, particularly with balance and shot selection.  
- He is more of a combo guard than a traditional point guard, with a below-ideal assist-to-turnover ratio (1.1 to 2.6) that limits his effectiveness as a facilitator.  
- Sexton tends to force the offense, make flashy plays, and lack decision-making, often prioritizing showmanship over efficiency.  
- He is a streaky shooter, not a consistent outside threat, and struggles with shot selection and reliability beyond high-impact moments.  
- Needs to improve his movement without the ball and develop greater composure and team-oriented play to succeed at the NBA level.
weaknesses.168:
- Very unathletic with limited defensive ability, especially against quicker players  
- Minimal shooting range; took only nine jump shots in senior season  
- Turns 24 shortly after the draft, with little potential for growth  
- Relies on overpowering smaller defenders, which is rare in professional NBA matchups  
- Poor shooting efficiency (59% free throw), slow game pace, and ineffective shot blocking limit his effectiveness in modern NBA play
weaknesses.169:
- Has room to gain strength and improve mobility, with potential to add muscle without losing agility  
- Solid ball-handler for his size but lacks creativity and consistency, especially under pressure; needs to tighten up handling and decision-making  
- Offensive upside hinges on development as an isolation scorer; current scoring is adequate but not explosive (not a 50-point player)  
- Shooting mechanics are improving but inconsistent, with a slow or unorthodox jumpshot and limited three-point consistency  
- Strong defensively with elite potential, but lacks a clear positional fit; versatility as a "tweener" could be an asset despite strength concerns
weaknesses.170:
- Lacks above-average foot speed and lateral quickness, making it difficult to stay in front of ball handlers.  
- Has an average first step and struggles to create offensive looks due to limited athleticism.  
- Already 22 years old, suggesting limited developmental potential left.  
- Needs improvement in defensive stance, screen fighting, and defensive intensity against perimeter players.  
- Played mostly against weaker competition in the WCC, raising questions about effectiveness against top-tier athletes.
weaknesses.171:
- Lacks ball-handling skills and lateral mobility, limiting his ability to defend bigger players or take them off the dribble.  
- Relies heavily on physical tools and has a narrow frame, making it difficult to gain position in the post.  
- Inconsistent shooter, with limited range and a tendency to overuse his jumper at the expense of finishing efficiently.  
- Struggles with defensive awareness and effort, lacking strength, particularly in his lower body, and is easily overpowered.  
- Needs to improve his post game, rebounding, and passing, with limited upside due to age and lack of development in key areas.
weaknesses.172:
- Very green as a prospect and leaving college prematurely as a PG could hinder long-term development  
- More of a shooter than a scorer, with limited offensive creation  
- Raw as a floor general; questions remain about whether he's a combo or true point guard  
- Limited experience in creating offense off the dribble and uncomfortable around the rim  
- Strong outside shooting (42.8% from inside), but mediocre slashing and cutting skills indicate poor in-transition offensive efficiency
weaknesses.173:
- Brackins often settles for fadeaway shots instead of using his size, strength, and talent to dominate one-on-one situations.  
- He struggles with consistency, particularly in shot selection, offensive rebounding, and free throw shooting (70%).  
- His jump shot is unreliable, and his outside shooting lacks range and consistency, despite being a good midrange shooter.  
- Defensively, he lacks lateral quickness and strength, making it difficult to defend athletic forwards and disrupt passing lanes.  
- He is not aggressive in the paint, operates off balance when taken out of position, and has a predictable shooting tendency due to preferring right-hand turns.
weaknesses.174:
- Still unproven as a point guard; uncertain how long it will take to become effective at the NBA lead guard position  
- Below average run/jump ability; length helps defensively but lacks explosiveness  
- Needs to improve defensive intensity and consistency throughout a season  
- Shot is solid but requires slight adjustment in release to extend range; tends to turn into shot  
- Struggles in transition, finishing, and shot selection; benefits from increased leg strength and explosiveness
weaknesses.175:
- Not an elite-level athlete with limited quickness and explosiveness  
- Plays primarily below the rim and struggles to beat defenders off the dribble  
- Ball handling is adequate but uncomfortable with multiple dribbles; poor ability to create for self or others  
- Often overwhelmed by top competition, leading to high volume of low-percentage shots and three-point attempts  
- Lacks lateral quickness and should leverage his 6'7", 225-pound frame in the paint against smaller opponents; struggles defensively against quicker wings at the next level
weaknesses.176:
- Lacks the killer instinct to take over a game and relies too heavily on strength rather than first-class first steps.  
- Struggles with ball handling during dribble moves and prefers operating from the triple threat.  
- Shooting mechanics are smooth but hindered by slow footwork, dipping the ball, and an off-balance stance leading to fadeway shots.  
- Prone to turnovers due to leaving his feet when passing and committing offensive fouls by bullying to the basket.  
- Defensively, has strong tools but plays lackadaisically with hands down; should force more steals despite playing in a conservative system.
weaknesses.177:
- Limited offensive game with few post moves and poor jump shot range  
- Struggles on defense due to poor box-out technique, lack of lateral quickness, and tendency to get out of position  
- Not athletic or quick, leading to slow rotations and being left behind in transition  
- Fairly foul-prone and a poor free-throw shooter despite modest improvement  
- Lacks above-the-rim ability and has limited defensive length and effectiveness despite his 7’1" frame
weaknesses.179:
- Struggles with fluidity in his first step and upright playing style with stiff hips.  
- Needs improvement in scoring ability, averaging only 6.6 ppg in Tucson and 8 ppg as a sophomore on a top-seeded team.  
- Inconsistent shot selection and decision-making, with poor mid-range shooting and lack of efficiency from the spot-up position.  
- Shooting mechanics are flawed: long shot motion, hesitation, hitch in form, and imbalance due to a high center of gravity.  
- Requires better footwork, balance, and development of a floater to improve efficiency against interior defenders.
weaknesses.180:
- Doesn't stand out as a lockdown defender despite length and mobility.  
- Has never averaged more than 0.8 steals or blocks.  
- Could use his length more effectively, especially in defensive positioning.  
- Limited playmaking ability; lacks consistent passing and ball movement.  
- Physically dominant against younger college players, but questions remain about effectiveness and adaptability at the professional level.
weaknesses.181:
