- Struggles with intensity, focus, and competitiveness, often disappearing from games and lacking tenacity inside.  
- Poor rebounding output (6.9 per game) for his physical attributes, with insufficient defensive prowess to match his potential.  
- Post skills have not developed as expected and require significant improvement.  
- Productivity and efficiency declined as a junior, with a drop in scoring, field goal percentage, free throw percentage, and minutes.  
- High turnover rate, limited assist production, and difficulty passing out of double-teams despite strong physical frame.
weaknesses.182:
- Not a true point guard; prioritizes his own shot and struggles with team play, raising concerns about development at the next level  
- Improved decision-making over time but takes questionable shots when going hot and struggles in high-pressure situations  
- Dominates on offense with strong outside shooting and drive-to-the-rim ability, but lacks consistency in midrange shooting  
- Good assist-to-turnover ratio, though it could improve to 1.5-to-1; occasionally careless with the ball and inconsistent in big games  
- Plays against weaker competition, limiting growth and raising doubts about his ability to elevate his game in critical moments
weaknesses.183:
- Shooting remains the biggest flaw, with mechanics needing improvement in quickness and release, especially from three-point range.  
- Game comprehension and playmaking are underdeveloped, leading to poor decision-making and frequent turnovers under pressure.  
- Transitioned from physical dominance at youth level to reduced effectiveness against higher-level opponents.  
- Defensive potential is strong but inconsistent, lacking fundamental positioning in structured systems.  
- Poor focus, attitude, body language, and determination raise concerns about consistency and long-term appeal despite first-round talent.
weaknesses.184:
- Lacks a clear offensive role and struggles with ball handling, often putting the ball on the deck insecurely.  
- Has a weak first step and poor dribbling mechanics, limiting his ability to create shots or drive to the basket.  
- Relies heavily on jump shots, especially from long range, with inconsistent shot selection and poor free throw shooting (career 63%).  
- Shows insecurity under pressure, turning his back to defenders when faced with contact and rarely attacks all the way to the cup.  
- Despite talent and improvement in mechanics, he remains a complimentary player, not a primary offensive option due to lack of perimeter skills and defensive size.
weaknesses.185:
- Dotson has defensive potential but needs to improve strength to compete against bigger NBA guards and wings.  
- Offensively, he lacks versatility, with limited ability to create shots off the dribble or in pick-and-roll situations.  
- 84.5% of his field goal attempts were jump shots, indicating inefficient scoring methods for the NBA.  
- His low free throw frequency (1.7 attempts per game) and minimal assist production (1.5 per 40 minutes) raise concerns about offensive impact and playmaking.  
- He struggles with off-the-ball defense, takes poor angles when chasing cutters, and has issues closing out, while also being 23 years old with limited time to develop.
weaknesses.186:
- Has good size for his position but a short wingspan of 6'6"  
- Lacks explosive speed and lateral quickness, especially on defense  
- Not a strong ball handler and doesn't possess the ball frequently  
- Compensates for physical limitations with strong fundamentals  
- Reliable shooter, but struggles in one-on-one defensive situations against fast guards
weaknesses.187:
- Lacks natural touch and consistency on shots, with poor wrist action and a reliance on arm movement  
- Inefficient offensive player, averaging below 60% on free throws and limited scoring ability  
- Struggles defensively due to a high center of gravity, upright stance, and difficulty checking quicker guards  
- Limited vision and passing, averaging only 0.8 assists per game  
- Needs improvement in strength, toughness, and mental toughness to succeed at the NBA level
weaknesses.188:
- Shot only 42% inside the arc and 39% overall last season, with poor shot selection in half-court situations.  
- Lacks speed, craftiness, and consistency in getting to the rim; often drives into defenders and forces tough shots.  
- Inadequate defensive skills: poor lateral quickness, lack of effort, awkward footwork, and frequent failure to stop ballhandlers.  
- Underperformed at the combine, with one of the lowest vertical leaps, indicating below-average athleticism.  
- Extremely skinny (under 200 lbs at 6'8"), limiting his physicality and making him vulnerable at the NBA level; limited free throw opportunities in college.
weaknesses.189:
- Still a raw player with inconsistent high-level performances despite strong potential  
- Frequently makes poor decisions, including careless passes and outlet throws when off balance  
- Missed most of his high school senior season due to left knee surgery  
- Struggles with smooth movement and energy efficiency on the court  
- Lacks confidence in scoring, poor free throw shooting, and limited experience in the post offense
weaknesses.190:
- Has solid weight (240 lbs) but could benefit from adding more muscle, especially in the upper body  
- Movement is slightly mechanical; lacks fluidity, particularly in jumpshot and transition  
- Limited explosiveness and strength make it difficult to finish drives or engage in contact; tends to avoid contact  
- Shooting shows promise from distance but lacks consistency (19-of-52, 0.6 makes per game) and has a slow, inefficient release motion  
- Needs improvement in pick-and-roll execution, footwork, and decision-making, with potential to adapt to NBA level over time
weaknesses.192:
- Wolf has excellent vision and game sense as a playmaker but struggles with a high turnover rate (25.2%, 3.2 per game, 21st worst in college, 3rd worst in Big Ten).  
- His ball handling and decision-making can lag, especially under pressure, limiting his effectiveness in high-stakes or fast-paced situations.  
- While strong in setting up offense and running pick-and-rolls, he needs to improve as a roll man to secure consistent playing time.  
- He shows defensive promise but lacks the strength or speed to consistently handle perimeter threats or interior players, especially under high pressure.  
- Struggles with high-half-court presses and outside shot consistency, leading to reduced offensive opportunities and a need for more conservative shot selection in the league.
weaknesses.193:
- At 6-8 and 230 lbs, Cunningham lacks the size and bulk to excel as a power forward at the next level.  
- He lacks offensive skills for a wing position, with subpar ball handling and limited shot variety.  
- His face-up game is limited to jab steps and bull rushes; he has no range and has never shown three-point ability at the college level.  
- Despite being a solid athlete, he lacks explosive speed and often has his shots sent back in competition.  
- He struggles to get shots off inside against taller, more athletic defenders and is not quick off the floor, relying on hustle for rebounds.
weaknesses.194:
- Exum has not consistently proven himself against high-level competition, despite strong performances in youth tournaments like the U-19 World Championships.  
- He struggles with ball-handling, frequent fumbles, and poor decision-making, often making ill-advised passes and lacking point guard presence.  
- His shooting is inconsistent, with a flat trajectory, low release point, and tendency to settle for long-range threes; he lacks a reliable mid-range game.  
- He is not vertically explosive, struggles to finish with authority, and has a limited ability to drive and finish against defenders.  
- Despite being one of the world’s top under-20 players with elite speed and potential, he needs significant development in ball-handling, shooting, and contact absorption to reach NBA star status.
weaknesses.195:
- Inconsistent scorer with frequent shooting droughts and difficulty establishing shooting rhythm  
- Poor shot selection, frequently taking off-balanced and early-in-offense shots  
- Struggles with off-the-dribble shooting and poor body control, especially when shooting jumpers  
- Aggressive, fast-paced play leads to poor decision-making and high-risk situations in traffic  
- Lacks upper body strength, right-hand finishing ability, and consistent free-throw shooting; prone to emotional outbursts and overreliance on finesse shots
weaknesses.196:
- Saric lacks foot speed and athleticism, making it difficult to keep up with NBA wing forwards and defend against quick attacks.  
- He has limited strength to compete with NBA power forwards on the block and struggles to finish above the rim.  
- Projected as a stretch 4, he lacks consistent shooting ability, with only 30% three-point accuracy and poor mechanics for a pure shooter.  
- His game is described as a "jack of all trades, master of nothing" due to inconsistent shooting, poor decision-making (3 turnovers per game), and lack of explosiveness.  
- Defensively, his small wingspan (6'10") and lack of foot speed make it hard to guard guards or forwards, despite an aggressive playing mentality.
weaknesses.197:
- Struggled with athleticism and leaping due to a lingering foot injury, impacting dunking and rebounding  
- Inconsistent shot creation, especially inside the arc, with only a 41% field goal percentage and limited offensive aggression  
- Poor free throw efficiency in games (28 attempts in 28 games) and lack of rebounding (2.4 rebounds per game)  
- High turnover rate (39) relative to assists (27), indicating poor offensive decision-making and ball-handling issues  
- Foot injuries, including a pre-season fracture and post-season surgery, have limited his availability and made him a questionable first-round pick
weaknesses.198:
- Lacks a strong feel for the game, particularly in knowing when to score or move the ball in offense  
- Inconsistent half-court scoring and jump shot accuracy, with elbow flaring leading to off-target misses  
- Needs to develop a post-game skill to leverage his size advantage against defenders  
- Has room for strength development, currently weighing around 200 pounds and struggles to maintain drive momentum  
- Needs to improve ball handling, reduce turnovers (1.9 per game), tighten his game control, and increase in-game consistency
weaknesses.199:
- Has a decent wingspan of 6’5 and standing reach of 8’2, but lacks strength.  
- Weighs only 173 pounds, making rim finishing challenging at the college level.  
- Very quick but not explosive, limiting power and impact in physical confrontations.  
- Can shoot from outside but is inconsistent (streaky) in scoring.  
- Good assist production (2.6 per game in Nike EYBL) but needs to reduce turnovers.
weaknesses.200:
- Stands 6’2" and lacks the point guard skills for full-time play.  
- In uncomfortable around the rim in college and will likely struggle against taller NBA players.  
- Has solid passing ability and moves the ball well, but won’t be expected to create opportunities off the dribble.  
- Ideal role: plays two-guard off the bench, focusing on spot-up shooting from the perimeter.  
- Possesses high basketball IQ, wingspan, and a strong frame, which may mitigate defensive struggles; could be a second-round pick if catch-and-shoot ability is proven.
weaknesses.201:
- Not particularly quick or fast, with limited athletic ability  
- Limited rim attack and poor free-throw shooting (averaged 1.7 attempts per game in senior season)  
- Improved ball-handling but not a high-scoring or aggressive offensive player  
- Limited upside and not a standout athlete or skill set  
- Valuable for team chemistry and role-playing; best suited on teams with established stars; attractive in late-first or early-second round draft picks
weaknesses.202:
- Lacks outstanding athletic abilities, including explosive leaping and speed  
- Poor understanding of transition play; often forces shots when outnumbered  
- Underdeveloped catch-and-shoot ability; prone to ill-advised shots  
- Ineffective from beyond the arc (only 25% from three last season)  
- Average defender due to lack of lateral speed and struggles to recover after being screened; age offers upside but delays NBA readiness
weaknesses.203:
- Has stiff movement and tight hips, limiting his athletic appearance despite being a capable player.  
- Improves jump off two feet, which slows his initial acceleration before explosive finishes.  
- Age 22 (turning 23) gives him senior-level experience but suggests youthful potential compared to other late first-round prospects.  
- Shows promising outside shooting potential, with a career 67% FT percentage and a junior improvement to 71%, though still unproven at the professional level.  
- At 235 lbs and nearly 22, has limited capacity to gain significant muscle; likely remains a wiry big, but the weight is acceptable for today’s NBA.
weaknesses.204:
- Small body size and lack of strength make him vulnerable to bigger, stronger guards, especially in physical confrontations.  
- Needs to build muscle and improve body strength to withstand contact and compete at the NBA level.  
- Has a unique but slow-release jumpshot with limited range, requiring consistent shot-making ability at the professional level.  
- Struggles with catch-and-shoot situations and closing out games, limiting his go-to scoring and defensive effectiveness.  
- Relies on quickness and smarts defensively; must develop game leadership and decision-making, especially in high-pressure situations.
weaknesses.206:
- Lacks quickness and ability to create separation or pressure defenses off the dribble  
- Improved spot-up shooting, but inefficient with contested mid-range shots and poor free-throw percentage (65%)  
- Doesn’t effectively space the floor by failing to make defenses pay on pick-and-roll screens  
- Turnover-prone with limited playmaking ability; middling decision-making and ball-handling  
- Versatile but inconsistent as a lead guard; can be too deliberate or lose possession due to poor dribbling and passing execution
weaknesses.207:
- Needs to gain weight and build strength, especially in the upper body  
- Lacks elite court vision and struggles with decision-making, including pace management  
- Frequent turnovers exceed his assist numbers due to poor shot selection  
- Tends to force shots and relies heavily on an inconsistent outside shot  
- Must improve as a vocal leader on the court
weaknesses.208:
- Struggles with defensive containment due to slow foot speed and poor lateral quickness.  
- Lacks verticality, making it difficult to finish or rebound against taller opponents.  
- Flat footwork and slow hips lead to poor closeouts and leaving defenders exposed.  
- Prone to fouls from over-committing or recovering late; vulnerable to guards with strong first steps.  
- Big men can dominate in the post, limiting his ability to fit into defensive schemes; needs improvement in perimeter and paint defense.
weaknesses.209:
- Struggles as a pick and roll facilitator, often leaving himself out of position  
- Poor free throw shooting (66.7%) raises concerns about overall shooting consistency at the next level  
- Too aggressive on defense, frequently drawing charges that result in blocks  
- Inefficient offensive rebounding and lacks a strong shot-hunting mentality  
- Limited leaping ability and minimal length (wingspan just an inch or two over height) hinder rim finishing
weaknesses.210:
- Lacks great run/jump athleticism for a shooter  
- Foot speed is below average by NBA standards  
- Weighs only 211 pounds, making him thin for a forward position  
- Struggles as a rebounder and defender  
- Has average wingspan (6’10") and tends to force questionable shots
weaknesses.211:
- Not aggressive in attacking or creating his own offense  
- Overly reliant on other players for offensive contributions  
- Lacks elite athleticism and has limited mobility for his height  
- Poor floor presence; shooting doesn't significantly impact gameplay  
- Limited playmaking ability despite being a willing defender
weaknesses.212:
- Struggles with shooting, missing both three-point attempts and shooting only 50.5% from the free-throw line  
- Inefficient scoring with under 10 points per game and poor two-point shooting (38.7%)  
- Limited defensive impact with fewer than one block per game and only 4.2 defensive rebounds per game  
- Poor defensive consistency, often getting caught out of position when guarding the pick and roll  
- Lacks speed, leading to difficulties in helping on defense, perimeter guarding, and transitioning effectively
weaknesses.213:
- Fox struggles with an inconsistent jump shot, making only 31% of pull-up jumpers and 20% of spot-up attempts this season, though performance improved in March.  
- His lack of NBA range and reliance on shots inside the arc limits his effectiveness, and he needs to improve balance and release mechanics (e.g., reducing elbow flex).  
- He tends to rush when driving to the rim, lacks control, and often turns the ball over, especially in pick-and-roll situations.  
- Fox is physically wiry (175 lbs), lacks upper and lower body strength, and may struggle against bigger guards in contact, though he shows potential as a defender.  
- He has strong passing ability and court vision but needs to improve decision-making, patience, and game tempo control to become a more reliable floor general.
weaknesses.214:
- Averaged 8.3 points per game in 27 minutes of play, showing modest scoring output in college.  
- Shot only 28.4% from three-point range and 70.6% from the foul line, indicating inconsistent shooting.  
- Is a more effective scorer inside the 3-point line than beyond, with a strong right-hand dominance.  
- Needs to improve finishing with his off hand, avoid forcing finishes, and develop better shooting consistency.  
- At 190 pounds, lacks physicality for the NBA; missing a sophomore season adds uncertainty about his growth and draft potential.
weaknesses.215:
- Defensive awareness is lacking, particularly in guarding pick-and-roll situations, helping defense, and contesting shots on the perimeter.  
- Shot selection and jumper quality need improvement; the jump shot is flat with poor arc and often misses with hard bounces off the rim.  
- Offensively, he tends to settle for jumpers, roam to the perimeter, and lacks go-to moves, with limited impact in traffic and post play.  
- Immaturity and lack of experience contribute to poor decision-making, poor positioning, and an inability to handle double teams or stronger post opponents.  
- Needs to improve intensity, competitive drive, and work habits to avoid complacency and maintain development amid media pressure.
weaknesses.216:
- Offensive shooting efficiency is low, with only 26.6% from three-point range and 65.7% from the free throw line, raising concerns despite a solid shooting form.  
- Shows flashes of NBA athleticism but is not a finished product; lacks consistency and refinement.  
- Defensive intensity and technique are lacking, especially in isolation situations, leading to missed opportunities and fouls.  
- Has potential as a defender but currently plays with inconsistent energy and focus, resulting in a "Swiss cheese" defense.  
- Struggles with decision-making, forcing shots and over-penetrating, and lacks a clear NBA position due to poor shooting and size.
weaknesses.217:
- Lacks size and defensive technique, often too upright in stance, limiting his ability to defend larger forwards  
- Inconsistent offensive production with 11 single-digit scoring games, showing lack of assertiveness  
- Does not excel in any one offensive area, relying on a collection of above-average but unelite skills  
- Struggles to create opportunities for teammates, averaging only 0.4 assists per game  
- Vulnerable to NBA-level defenders who limit his post game and contest his perimeter shooting
weaknesses.218:
- Has offensive versatility but lacks a clear game-breaker ability or consistent offensive impact in high-pressure situations.  
- Can shoot the NBA 3-pointer but has limited attempts and is not a "shoot-first" player.  
- Ideal position remains uncertain, with questions about whether his skills suit a shooting guard or power forward role.  
- Considered a "versatile and possibly elite defender" by scouts, though his steal and block rates are low relative to that projection.  
- Shows potential as a complementary, high-level starter or versatile 6th man, fitting well with modern NBA systems despite not excelling in any single skill.
weaknesses.219:
- Poor free throw shooter with inconsistent shot-making  
- Career high of 19 points at Kentucky; never averaged more than 8.6 points per game  
- Funky, inconsistent jump shot; struggles to make shots after putting the ball on the floor  
- Lacks offensive consistency, poor movement, and difficulty finishing in traffic  
- Emotional demeanor, limited offensive development, and history as a reserve role player
weaknesses.220:
- Below average athleticism by NBA standards with limited bounce and average first step  
- Short wingspan (6-8) limits defensive impact and ability to guard multiple players  
- Poor ball handling with loose dribbling, tunnel vision, and inability to create separation  
- Ineffective offensively when shots don’t fall; lacks post-up game and offensive versatility  
- Defensive weaknesses include poor positioning, inability to contain bigger players, and limited rebounding threat
weaknesses.221:
- Needs to improve strength to finish drives and maintain balance after contact.  
- Lacks experience with only one season of college play.  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from three-point range (sub-30% as a freshman).  
- Must enhance decision-making, ball awareness, and reduce turnovers.  
- More of a scorer than a playmaker and needs to develop offensive creation for teammates.
weaknesses.222:
- Lacks mobility and quickness at current weight, hindering performance in an up-tempo game  
- Severe undersizing at the post (6'6.5") despite a 7'3" wingspan, limiting effectiveness in post play  
- Poor explosiveness and difficulty creating shots against more athletic opponents  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from the free-throw line (61%) and lack of reliable mid-range jumper  
- Strong will and determination, but risk of being unresponsive to coaching and instruction due to independent play style
weaknesses.223:
- Wright performs best when he has full control of the ball and can create plays in his comfort zone.  
- He lacks initial freedom and will need to prove effectiveness without those advantages in his early years.  
- His body strength is a weakness, particularly when playing near the rim.  
- His success depends on consistent shooting, including improved range, especially from beyond the 3-point line.  
- He needs to stay engaged and active in every possession, and must improve his on-ball defense.
weaknesses.224:
- Plays hard but rarely full throttle, lacking intensity and consistency  
- Struggles with outside shooting, shooting only 17% from three and 65% from the free throw line  
- Limited ball handling, predictable movement, and poor creativity in face-up situations  
- Minimal post-up usage despite size and strength; not a strong passer (1.5 assists per game)  
- Has superstar potential but needs to improve range, intensity, defensive effort, and game versatility
weaknesses.225:
- Lacks maturity and composure, often appearing disinterested or frustrated when things don't go his way.  
- Inconsistent free throw shooting and declining focus as games progress, with bouts of poor decision-making.  
- Lacks explosiveness and athleticism, making him vulnerable against more agile or athletic defenders.  
- Poor lateral movement, limited rebounding, and lack of hustle on defense, especially when not engaged.  
- Needs to improve footwork, decision-making, and defensive leadership, with consistency lacking against top competition.
weaknesses.226:
- Lacks necessary polish and go-to moves to be a serious offensive threat  
- Incomfortable when forced to take shots in the low or mid-post areas  
- Struggles to compete against bigger players due to insufficient body mass  
- Has a poor foul shot record throughout his career  
- Shows limited ability as a shot blocker
weaknesses.227:
- Standing at 6'1" with an 8-foot wingspan, he is on the smaller side for his position.  
- Older than his peers (will be 22 in September), but lacks strong point guard skills and leadership.  
- Struggles to create easy baskets for teammates and is inconsistent in defensive focus, especially off the ball.  
- Shows streaky shooting, with a drop in three-point (34% from 42%) and field goal (44% from 50%) percentages this season.  
- Needs to improve as a passer, handle faster and bigger defenders, and prove consistency in production despite a potential hamstring injury impact.
weaknesses.228:
- Below average wingspan (6-9) for his size with limited elite burst and poor ability to blow by athletic opponents from a standstill  
- Struggles with separation against long, athletic defenders and has difficulty creating space on the perimeter  
- Inconsistent decision-making, tendency to rush or slow down, leading to turnovers and off-target passes  
- Poor free throw shooting (below 70%), streaky shooting, and lack of reliability in spot-up and catch-and-shoot situations  
- Limited mobility, flexibility, and ability to finish through contact; predictable right-handed driving and underdeveloped left-hand skills
weaknesses.229:
- Has a 3.3 to 2.9 a/to ratio indicating a need for improved ball protection and distribution.  
- Shot lacks range and NBA-level extension, relying on a set shot with only 40% 3-point shooting.  
- Lightweight frame (168 lbs) and narrow shoulders limit physicality, making him vulnerable to stronger guards.  
- Needs to develop left-hand ability to balance defense and improve spot-up shooting effectiveness.  
- Requires experience against high-level competition, leadership growth, and improved pick-and-roll execution.
weaknesses.230:
- Smith relies heavily on physical gifts and struggles with attention to detail and offensive execution.  
- His decision-making, especially off the pick and roll, needs improvement, and he often fails to initiate or facilitate the offense consistently.  
- He shows inconsistent effort, lacks defensive intensity, and frequently misses opportunities to help teammates or close out on shooters.  
- His shooting, particularly from three-point range, is inconsistent with poor form and hesitation, and he often settles for contested jumpers.  
- Despite high athletic talent and upside, Smith must improve his work ethic, leadership, and defensive commitment to reach NBA potential.
weaknesses.231:
- Slow and unathletic small forward with limited ability to beat defenders off the dribble  
- Most effective when the offense runs through him; uncertain if he has the quickness to do so in the NBA  
- Minimal impact when playing off the ball; often gets trapped or forced into turnovers  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting; possible fluke in senior year percentage  
- Below-average athleticism at the next level; unsure of his defensive position and potential development
weaknesses.232:
- Averaged only 5.2 points per game as a freshman with a 15% three-point shooting percentage and 60% free-throw accuracy.  
- Lacks lateral quickness and struggles to stay on the court late in games, especially against pick-and-pop shooters.  
- Minimal offensive impact as a back-to-the-basket scorer; relies on a rarely used hook shot and has a lean, undersized frame.  
- Struggles with rebounding (5 rebounds per game), foul trouble (2.7 fouls in 20 minutes), and frequently gets displaced under the basket due to lack of strength.  
- Limited offensive awareness, poor play anticipation, and significant development needed in creating opportunities and offensive execution.
weaknesses.233:
- Struggles with on-ball defense and lacks true defensive skills to lock down the paint, making him unsuitable for anchoring a pro or college defense.  
- Offensive-minded but inconsistent, scoring in single digits multiple times, and relies heavily on close-range scoring without stretching the floor.  
- Limited ability to stretch the floor; shot 0-7 or 0-6 from three-point range across all elite high school competitions and lacks a clean shooting form.  
- Poor free throw shooter with fluctuating percentages (33.3% to 58.3%) and minimal shot-blocking presence (averaging 0.3–0.6 blocks per game).  
- A traditional post player with limited athleticism and style, who stays in the paint and does not defend the perimeter or pick-and-roll situations effectively.
weaknesses.234:
- Limited first step and weak handles make it difficult to drive past defenders off the dribble  
- Passive playing style with frequent deference to teammates and poor ball-handling initiative  
- Slow release and inability to maintain shooting form under pressure lead to inconsistent shot-making  
- Inconsistent pullup jumper and predictable back-to-the-basket shooting (relying on right-side fadeaway)  
- Defensively lacks lateral quickness and upper body strength, making it hard to guard perimeter players
weaknesses.235:
- Had discipline issues in high school that led to being kicked off the Louisville team by Rick Pitino.  
- Can be lazy and goes through the motions, lacking consistent effort.  
- Not always active on the defensive end, especially on the glass, with inconsistent motor.  
- Good length but undersized; lacks enough mobility to compensate, especially on breaks and loose balls.  
- Relies heavily on power and strength near the basket and must improve his activity on the glass and overall movement.
weaknesses.236:
- Needs to increase jump trajectory and range while improving strength and conditioning.  
- Ball handling and footwork require significant improvement, especially in face-up situations.  
- Struggles with double teams, poor court positioning, and a tendency to predetermine moves rather than react.  
- Lacks consistency, often tires in later game stages, and has mechanical issues offensively.  
- Must develop perimeter skills (ball handling, passing, shooting) to better suit an NBA small forward role.
weaknesses.237:
- Lacks burst and shiftiness, limiting his ability to get to the rim or stay ahead of quicker guards at the NBA level.  
- Too upright in his stance, reducing explosiveness during offense and defense; struggles to finish over NBA-length rim.  
- Has only an average first step from the triple threat position, making it difficult to turn the corner efficiently.  
- Struggles to change pace quickly and often settles for off-balanced jumpers, especially in pick-and-roll situations.  
- Defensive versatility is questionable due to lack of size to guard bigger guards and insufficient speed to match explosive point guards.
weaknesses.238:
- Williams sees himself as a shooting forward but has limited SF experience and struggles defensively against NBA-level SFs due to mediocre lateral quickness and lack of length.  
- He is a modest defensive player with poor lateral movement, weak rim protection, and inconsistent intensity, often allowing easy baskets against bigger, more athletic forwards.  
- Defensively, he is a poor rebounder (5.5 per game) and tends to ball-watch, giving opponents second-chance opportunities.  
- Offensively, he lacks a reliable mid-range shot, has slow shot execution, poor post vision, and struggles with consistency from the free-throw line.  
- Williams is undersized and lacks strength for a power forward, relying on speed and length, and would benefit from developing a more consistent offensive game.
weaknesses.239:
- Lacks lateral speed and quickness, making it difficult to defend NBA small forwards or guard larger power forwards  
- Inadequate foot speed limits offensive effectiveness off the dribble and defensive ability to stay in front of opponents  
- Poor rebounding rate in college, especially compared to other power forwards  
- Offensive production relies heavily on overpowering weaker defenders, which won't translate well in the NBA  
- Underwhelming athleticism and stiff body/ankles limit flexibility and overall defensive versatility
weaknesses.240:
- Desmond Bane has a wingspan of 6'4.25", shorter than his height of 6'6", limiting his defensive potential in the NBA.  
- He lacks lateral quickness and agility, making it difficult to guard quick players or navigate the lane off the dribble.  
- His physical profile is strong in vertical ability but not well-suited for positional versatility.  
- He is likely to play as a shooting guard on offense and defense, with limited roles on the small forward position.  
- Bane is not viewed as a high-upside player but as a reliable, long-term role player in the NBA.
weaknesses.241:
- Lacks explosive first step and vertical leap (35.5 inches), making it difficult to separate from defenders  
- Small body frame with underwhelming wingspan (6’6¼) and limited length, hindering perimeter shot contesting  
- Struggles with foot speed, defensive aggression, and physicality, especially against quicker, athletic players  
- Poor ball-handling instincts, limited ability to create separation or penetrate, and struggles on half-court drives  
- Inconsistent rebounding, low steal/block rates, and tendency to foul when getting into the paint or facing physical contact
weaknesses.242:
- Carter is a tweener guard at 6'3" who lacks consistent offensive creation or scoring versatility to fit exclusively as a PG or SG.  
- He is unlikely to have significant offensive freedom or possession volume in the NBA compared to his college role at Providence.  
- Not a natural playmaker (1.3 A/TO), with poor decision-making and tendency to dribble into no man’s land during complex plays.  
- Struggles with game pace, often losing control against savvy defenders, leading to wasted possessions and charging fouls.  
- His shot is deliberate and easily contested, especially off the dribble; midrange game is underdeveloped, and durability is questionable due to reckless play.
weaknesses.243:
- Struggles with ball handling, lacking quickness and being right-handed dominant  
- Ineffective against defenders due to poor dribble control and inability to finish through contact  
- Limited shooting range, with only 11% three-pointers this season and poor offensive versatility  
- Requires significant strength development, especially in core and lower body, to improve balance, leaping, and durability  
- Role is primarily as a scorer, but lacks court vision and frequently misses free throw opportunities
weaknesses.244:
- Can improve his isolation scoring ability, though limited usage in the FSU system this year  
- Needs to add bulk to his frame and develop core strength for long-term NBA health and performance  
- Limited contact with the line (2.2 times per game) highlights need to force the issue and get to the line more often  
- Struggles with scoring consistency, including multiple games scoring in single digits and inconsistent shooting (73.8% FT sophomore, 67.9% freshman)  
- Inconsistent shot release due to misaligned elbow and tendency to jet out; nonchalant demeanor may suggest lack of effort at times
weaknesses.245:
- Not a big scoring threat; more of a methodical, low-explosiveness offensive player  
- Struggles with consistency and natural shot-making, shooting only 44% inside the arc  
- Lacks creativity as a shot creator, relying on a straight-line slasher without quickness  
- Not effective in transition play and better suited to slower-paced teams  
- Limited defensive impact, with only 0.5 steals per game and difficulty containing explosive players
weaknesses.246:
- Devonte Graham is considered a bit thin for an NBA guard, listed at 185 pounds and could benefit from gaining strength.  
- He has average athleticism with good quickness and burst, but lacks standout traits by NBA standards.  
- He can become mentally frustrated in games and may stop seeking shots, leading to poor shot selection and excessive ball distribution.  
- His poor shooting performance in college (e.g.,.40% from the floor in his senior year) was due to heavy offensive responsibilities, not lack of ability.  
- Graham’s age (23) and limited development potential likely impact his draft stock and perceived upside.
weaknesses.248:
- Limited to center position due to lack of ability to step out, shoot jumpers, or move to the perimeter  
- Physically undersized at 6’10” and struggles with stamina, movement, and court coverage  
- Poor foul shooter and prone to fouls, committing one every 5 minutes on average  
- Struggles with lateral quickness, footwork, and movement, often pushing off in the paint and committing unnecessary fouls  
- Lacks a face-up game, midrange jumper, and post game development; needs improvement in strength, stamina, and offensive versatility
weaknesses.249:
- Limited offensive impact off the rim, often failing to contribute meaningfully when not finishing at the basket  
- Defensive lapses in awareness, particularly when guarding stretch fours and prone to fouling  
- Inconsistent performance on the court with long stretches of minimal impact despite showing flashes of ability  
- Struggles with free throw shooting (63% as a freshman) and lacks confidence, leading to a timid game approach  
- Predictable post moves and poor ability to counter defenders when first moves are contested, showing lack of adaptability
weaknesses.250:
- Limited athletic ability, especially in speed and explosiveness, despite having good size and length  
- Immature attitude and lack of focus, often drifting or losing composure during possessions  
- Poor practice habits and inconsistent motor, leading to erratic effort and performance  
- Struggles with finishing through contact and scoring against longer defenders  
- Inadequate defensive rebounding, poor box-out ability, and not a reliable rim protector
weaknesses.251:
- Brooks' lack of length (6-6 wingspan) limits his ability to finish over NBA-level defenders and guard quicker guards effectively.  
- He struggles with creating offense against NBA wings due to limited ball-handling skills, lack of shiftiness, and poor change of direction.  
- Brooks lacks burst from a standstill, making it difficult to turn corners and execute effective drives in the pros.  
- He averages 3.3 turnovers per 40 minutes and only 5.1 rebounds per 40 minutes, indicating inconsistent defensive and rebounding effort.  
- Without reliable isolation scoring or offensive versatility, Brooks will need to contribute beyond shot-making to fit into an NBA offense.
weaknesses.252:
- Lacks below-the-rim athleticism and speed, making him a poor fit for NBA-level open-floor play  
- Struggles with three-point shooting, with only a 32% career clip and no significant development as a shooter  
- Has a quick first step but is extremely slow for a wing, limiting both offensive and defensive efficiency  
- Relies on pick-and-roll play and team setups, often over-dribbling and favoring East/West actions  
- Faces significant challenges adapting to NBA speed, athleticism, and competition due to playing against lower-level opponents and being 22.5 years old as a junior
weaknesses.254:
- Not a point guard, with questions about halfcourt facilitation and offensive effectiveness.  
- Struggles to create good shots in halfcourt situations.  
- Should attack the rim more often due to athleticism and explosive bounce.  
- Needs improvement as a free throw shooter (currently at 70%).  
- Lacks experience defending elite perimeter players in isolation within a zone defense.
weaknesses.255:
- Wilson has strong physical tools but struggles to consistently maximize them, often settling for contested jumpers instead of finishing at the rim.  
- He tends to float on the perimeter when matched up with smaller players and fails to post up effectively.  
- Wilson lacks strong defensive awareness, is late to hedge on ball screens, and often gets beaten on cuts.  
- He has poor rebounding production (5.3 rebounds per 30 minutes last season) despite his size, length, and athleticism.  
- He needs to improve his toughness, physicality, and positioning in the post, as well as his vision and ability to anticipate the ball.
weaknesses.256:
- Below average wingspan and lack of length/athleticism for his size (6-11 feet) affect draft stock  
- Struggles against long and athletic defenders due to limited physical tools  
- Mid-range shooting is below average, though FT% and practice performance indicate potential for improvement  
- Inconsistent shooting mechanics and overreliance on dominant hand hinder offensive efficiency  
- Limited vertical jump and short wingspan make him ineffective as a shot blocker at the NBA level
weaknesses.257:
- Lacks a true NBA position but can play either guard spot, with a likely long-term fit at the 2G position.  
- Limited by size, which caps his overall potential and raises concerns about performance against taller, more athletic defenders.  
- Struggled to reach his potential before his junior year, indicating developmental issues.  
- Outside shooting is inconsistent and streaky, with limited lift on his jump shot due to his size.  
- While not an explosive athlete, he has sufficient hops and strength to finish at the rim.
weaknesses.258:
- Lacks strength and upperbody power, particularly compared to other NBA big men  
- Poor rebounder and often avoids "dirty work," leading to missed opportunities  
- Defensive lapses due to poor anticipation and lack of intensity; appears uninterested at times  
- Streaky shooting and inconsistent free throw performance despite good touch  
- Needs maturity, discipline, and focus to match the intensity of established European talents like Gasol or Nowitzki
weaknesses.259:
- Lacks explosive foot speed, leaping ability, and quickness, limiting his effectiveness in close shot attempts.  
- Misses shot opportunities due to slow reaction time in transition, allowing more athletic opponents to contest.  
- Not a strong defensive rebounder (6.5 career RPG, 7.4 RPG overall), especially on the defensive glass.  
- Limited post moves and relies on size rather than skill, with a developing shot arsenal.  
- Inconsistent shooting from distance and free throw line (under 60% FT%), raising concerns about shooting range and efficiency.
weaknesses.260:
- Mitchell is a stronger leaper with both feet, and his explosiveness diminishes when forced to elevate on one foot, especially with his weaker left hand.  
- He struggles to reach the rim consistently, reflected in a low two-point field goal percentage (46%) and below-average free throw rate.  
- Despite a 6’10 wingspan, his 8’1 standing reach is underdeveloped for an NBA point guard.  
- He is still adapting to the combo guard role and often fails to read defenses, making poor shot decisions and lacking court vision to operate over taller defenders.  
- Mitchell remains a streaky shooter, often settling for contested two-pointers due to limited rim access and subpar shot selection.
weaknesses.261:
- Never a full-time starter at Villanova with inconsistent minutes  
- Limited exposure to opponent game planning due to playing alongside a championship-caliber team  
- Experienced shooting slumps, despite strong free throw shooting (71% compared to team’s 77.9%)  
- Often outperformed on defense by taller guards and forwards  
- Overshadowed by Brunson and Bridges, lacking proven leadership and individual production; listed at 6'5" but may be undersized for shooting guard
weaknesses.262:
- Lacks playmaking ability (1.6 assists as a freshman, 2.8 as a sophomore) to handle the point guard role full time.  
- Insufficient size and strength to effectively defend NBA guards.  
- Primarily a "shoot first, shoot second, create third" guard with limited offensive finishability.  
- No explosive lift or extreme quickness; struggles to attack the rim or get his own shot against elite defenders.  
- Best suited as a complementary role on a winning team, lacking the personality and elite physical tools to lead or take over a game.
weaknesses.263:
- McDermott lacks explosiveness, speed, and vertical leaping ability, making him a poor fit for NBA-level athleticism.  
- At 6'6" without shoes, his height limits his effectiveness as a power forward and makes it difficult to play consistently at the PF position.  
- He struggles with shot creation, especially against NBA-sized defenders, and is easily blocked or rebounded, particularly in the post.  
- His defensive profile is weak—lacking lateral quickness, strength, and rebounding ability, with very low steal and block numbers throughout his career.  
- His effectiveness will largely depend on coaching and system usage, as he cannot generate consistent offensive impact or defend against athletic opponents.
weaknesses.264:
- Limited leaping ability and lack of explosiveness prevent him from playing as a wing at the next level  
- Naturally skinny build; needs to bulk up to compete effectively and withstand physicality  
- Inconsistent shooting mechanics, including unreliable release and poor pull-up jumper  
- Reliant on right hand, leading to predictability; lacks offensive creativity and directional change  
- Struggles with post defense, footwork, finishing through contact, and left-hand finishing; lacks strong defensive and rebounding skills
weaknesses.265:
- Inconsistent scoring with low offensive aggression, especially in showcase settings where he often appeared passive.  
- Averaged only 7.4 points per game as a freshman at UNC and rarely created offense off the dribble.  
- Struggles with pull-up shooting off the dribble and has a low free throw percentage (64.8%), raising concerns about shooting sustainability.  
- Lacks a polished handle, reliable go-to move, and consistent halfcourt offense; shot mechanics include bringing the ball down too far, reducing efficiency.  
- Can be overaggressive on defense, gambling for steals, but possesses strong physical tools and flashes of two-way impact.
weaknesses.266:
- Undersized for a physical forward with limited athleticism compared to a wing  
- Lacks explosiveness, agility, elusiveness, and quickness off the bounce  
- Ineffective at defending quicker guards on the perimeter  
- Under the rim finisher despite his size, making him a weak offensive threat  
- Minimal upside and could benefit from dropping some weight
weaknesses.267:
- Needs to improve fundamental defensive habits, including staying low, keeping hands up, and challenging opponents consistently.  
- Frequently reacts too late, allowing opponents clear looks and failing to stay inside their defensive area.  
- Lacks consistency from the perimeter and struggles with decision-making, often choosing to attack instead of distributing the ball.  
- Exhibits poor off-the-ball awareness, dribble-happy tendencies, and lapses in focus, sometimes falling asleep off the ball.  
- Requires greater game understanding, maturity, and emotional control to demonstrate dedication and professionalism.
weaknesses.268:
- Undersized and lacks great athleticism, though length partially compensates for these issues  
- Inconsistent scorer; ineffective when plays are run for him  
- Poor decision-making and frequent turnovers indicate lack of natural game sense  
- Relies heavily on movement and energy plays; struggles when opportunities don't come  
- Limited post moves, footwork, and touch; prone to losing balance and missing shots
weaknesses.269:
- Lack of consistent progress and poor performance trends, especially in key stats like three-point and free-throw percentages.  
- Limited offensive versatility, relying solely on finishing over his left shoulder with his right hand, making him predictable.  
- Deficiencies in core strength, on-court aggression, and lower body power impact his performance in traffic and overall effectiveness.  
- Declines in rebounding, shooting efficiency, and shot-blocking ability from junior to senior year are difficult to explain.  
- Limited leadership presence, lack of explosive athleticism, and underdeveloped physical attributes (e.g., wingspan, core) hinder his potential.
weaknesses.270:
- Lacks explosive speed and quickness, relying on strength and below-the-rim scoring rather than athleticism.  
- Struggles to finish above the rim and may have difficulty against taller, more athletic defenders.  
- Has a flat shooting arc, leading to line-drive misses; needs better consistency, especially on midrange and 3-pointers.  
- Shows improvement in 3-point shooting but remains inconsistent, with lower percentages in key tournaments.  
- Strong floor game and defensive reliability, but ceiling is below that of some top-5 draft prospects.
weaknesses.271:
- Dylan needs to improve his strength to compete against stronger players at his position.  
- At 195 pounds, he may struggle defensively due to being outmatched by more powerful opponents.  
- He was not a strong shot blocker in college and is unlikely to be effective in contesting shots in the NBA.  
- Windler will be 23 before his first NBA game, giving him a mature game close to his prime.  
- His one-on-one scoring will be limited; he will rely more on screen-running and lacks a dominant presence on the wing.
weaknesses.272:
- Lacks a strong first step and explosive ability, making it difficult to create separation from defenders.  
- Struggles to finish at the rim and has trouble elevating over bigger defenders, despite having length and size advantages against smaller opponents.  
- Often alters his shot instead of taking contact, reducing shot efficiency and making it harder to convert.  
- Limited offensive creativity; lacks outside moves, a quick release, and mid-range game, with poor decision-making and hesitation when shooting from distance.  
- Inconsistent ball-handling, prone to turnovers when carrying the ball high or under pressure, and vulnerable to quicker guards pressing him.
weaknesses.273:
- Inconsistent motor with significant ups and downs in performance during games  
- Below-average athleticism by NBA standards despite being better than most think  
- Poor explosiveness and lack of bounce when driving to the basket  
- Physical limitations: short wingspan (6-9), hunched posture, small chest/waist, weak upper body  
- Multiple defensive and offensive weaknesses, including struggles against athletic wings, poor off-the-ball defense, and tendency to gamble for steals
weaknesses.274:
- Not very athletic, with poor explosiveness and speed, making it difficult to drive the lane or finish at the rim in the NBA.  
- Has a below-average first step and mediocre ball-handling, struggling to create separation or get past defenders.  
- Tends to shoot contested pull-up jumpers when unable to get to the rim, especially when defended by more athletic players.  
- Mediocre to below-average size for a shooting guard, limiting his effectiveness against athletic defenders.  
- Needs to improve strength and size to compete effectively against professional shooting guards and will be inconsistent as a shooter.
weaknesses.275:
- Superior athleticism at the college level allowed him to get by with few moves, often shooting over defenders, but this lacks versatility at the NBA level.  
- His jumpshot remains inconsistent, with a poor form (off his shoulder, elbow out), and he struggles on the perimeter when forced into counter moves.  
- Limited offensive repertoire in the low post; avoids using his left hand in critical situations, even when it's the only option.  
- Frequent careless plays, poor footwork, and 3.2 turnovers per game due to leaving his feet during passes.  
- Needs to improve shooting consistency, especially from beyond the arc, and must develop defensive awareness and steal production.
weaknesses.276:
- Can add 20-25 lbs of strength to become a finesse-oriented 4, similar to Chris Bosh.  
- Improved strength will enhance his ability to finish around the rim, leveraging his length and contact durability.  
- Should focus on extending his range and developing a consistent mid-range shot.  
- Has good touch and potential in his lefty jump hook shot, though it was inconsistent as a freshman.  
- With practice and repetition, he can develop a reliable mid-range game and improve overall scoring consistency.
weaknesses.277:
- Struggles with outside shooting, particularly from three-point range (27% as a sophomore, down from 30% as a freshman)  
- Jumpshot lacks consistency in form and follow-through; needs improvement in spot-up shooting and floater development  
- Poor confidence and poor feel for the game; forces the ball off the dribble and lacks a consistent shot  
- Needs to develop physicality, muscle, and nastiness to handle contact and finish drives effectively  
- Injury history (ACL) raises concerns about long-term durability and recovery, and limited experience due to missing Xavier’s tournament run
weaknesses.278:
- Below-average three-point shooting as a freshman (27% from three, 41% from field)  
- Struggles to score consistently, with only one 20+ point game and five games under five points in 20+ minutes  
- Lacks confidence and fluidity from distance, limiting his offensive effectiveness  
- Most effective with the ball in his hands; less effective off the ball  
- Success in the NBA will depend on developing efficient scoring, potentially as a mid-range scorer akin to Shaun Livingston
weaknesses.279:
- Liddell lacks explosiveness and is undersized for an NBA PF/C role at 6'7", limiting his ability to compete against NBA-level frontcourt players.  
- Poor first-step and lack of quickness make him ineffective at creating off the bounce, especially when facing strong defenders.  
- Inconsistent shooting footwork and poor shot creation in the paint result in a low field goal percentage (49% FG) and frequent blocked shots.  
- Struggles to create separation or daylight when scoring in the low post, leading to shots being blocked or altered.  
- Right-handed dominance and poor off-hand usage reduce his effectiveness when forced to improvise, and he remains prone to fouls and pump fakes.
weaknesses.280:
- Offensive game remains raw with limited consistency and no established go-to move  
- Struggles in the post, often indecisive and uncomfortable with decision-making  
- High turnover rate due to low-percentage passes and frequent overdribbling (2.4 per game)  
- Lacks strength, particularly in lower body, and difficulty holding ground defensively  
- Poor free throw performance (4.5 per game) and lacks explosiveness, killer instinct, and defensive effort
weaknesses.281:
- Needs to improve consistency and range from the outside, particularly from beyond the arc  
- Subpar free throw shooter, averaging under 70% this season  
- Should work on becoming an ambidextrous finisher, currently favoring his right hand regardless of direction  
- Requires more muscle to better absorb contact in the paint  
- Currently averages 3.8 turnovers per game, though assists have increased this season
weaknesses.282:
- Lacks consistent aggressiveness and has fluctuating performance levels, both in intensity and decision-making.  
- Struggles with game reading, shot selection, and timing, often taking contested shots and settling for 3-pointers unnecessarily.  
- Exhibits hesitation before shooting off the dribble and a tendency to pass instead of finish at the rim, leading to turnovers.  
- Needs to improve his offensive versatility by mixing up his play, driving more, and reducing reliance on step-back shots.  
- Defensive issues include poor stance, lack of engagement in pick-and-roll situations, and poor positioning on the weak side.
weaknesses.283:
- Lacks ideal height for wing position  
- Solid athleticism but not elite; frequently has shots blocked near the rim  
- Three-point percentage dropped to 34% under pressure, showing inconsistency from deep  
- Defense is a concern due to poor spacing and man-to-man fundamentals  
- Overreliance on long-range shots and overambitious passing raises questions about shot selection and composure
weaknesses.284:
- Shot 25% from three and 50% from the stripe as a freshman, showing inconsistency from deep.  
- Prefers to walk into shots with dribbles and lacks a developed midrange game.  
- Offensive game is lefty dominant, with most drives and shots coming from his left.  
- Shoots too many lefty layups from the right side of the floor, indicating poor shot selection.  
- Needs improvement in ball handling, passing, and physical development, including adding bulk to his frame.
weaknesses.285:
- Lacks a true position, limiting his overall impact on the court  
- Very skinny for his size with weak upper and lower body strength, hurting both offensive and defensive performance  
- Mediocre athletic ability, especially under pressure or in physical situations  
- Avoids contact and battling for position, reducing his ability to post up or defend effectively  
- Needs to improve shooting consistency, inside game, and overall feel for the game, including range and vision
weaknesses.286:
- Struggles with midrange and outside shooting, often off balance and lacking consistent timing  
- Inconsistent shooting performance (34.2% from 3PT, 57.4% from the free throw line) with a tendency to take bad shots  
- Needs to improve confidence, consistency, and decision-making, especially off the ball and in high-pressure situations  
- High turnover rate (5.9 apg, 1.82 assist-to-turnover ratio) and lapses in defensive stance, requiring better ball control and awareness  
- Has significant upside as a playmaker with scoring ability and leadership potential; development, particularly in shooting and composure, is critical for NBA success
weaknesses.287:
- Mediocre athleticism with poor quickness, explosiveness, and lateral movement; lacks a strong first step and struggles to create clean looks.  
- Fragile frame, short wingspan, and below-average finishing around the rim for a 6’9 player; poor performance against contact and inside shots (47% FG inside arc).  
- One-dimensional scoring profile; relies heavily on shooting (33% 3FG), frequently settles for low-percentage shots, and makes poor shot selections.  
- Poor ball-handling and passing ability; lacks defensive awareness, poor defensive metrics (0.5 BPG, 0.7 SPG), and struggles against quick perimeter attackers or taller frontcourt players.  
- Inconsistent performance, self-centered play style, and off-court concerns (prior drug-related arrest); remains an underachiever despite early hype.
weaknesses.288:
- Has a history of knee problems with concerns about their condition during NBA physicals  
- Possesses good foot speed, but it is not at the level of top interior players in the NBA  
- Played one year of high school at Stoneridge Prep in Simi Valley, California  
- High school performance was somewhat inconsistent  
- Physical health, particularly knee issues, remains a significant concern for scouts
weaknesses.289:
- Will turn 24 in July, making him one of the oldest players in this year’s draft.  
- At 6’7.5 barefoot, he is short for an NBA center.  
- His jumpshot originates from his shoulder, is slow to release, and improves from farther out; needs a higher release point.  
- Playing in a mid-major (Mid-American Conference) limits the credibility of his statistical production due to lack of exposure to elite competition.  
- Struggles with post defense, footwork, and pick-and-roll guarding; lacks strength and could benefit from gaining weight.
weaknesses.290:
- Turnovers occur at a rate of 3 per game due to poor decision-making and frequent fast-break plays.  
- Exhibits tunnel vision toward the basket, often dribbling with his head down and ignoring defensive alignment.  
- Leaves his feet to pass frequently, leading to offensive fouls or unforced turnovers.  
- Relies on wild layups to avoid contact, with minimal three-point shooting and poor shot release mechanics.  
- Struggles with consistency, mentally checking out when games go poorly and fails to impact outcomes when not at his peak.
weaknesses.291:
- Not a top-tier athlete with suboptimal foot speed, explosiveness, and a question about his ability to add weight at 164 lbs  
- Turnovers are a significant issue, especially against top competition, due to ball-handling carelessness and reliance on offense  
- Struggles as a shooter: lacks elevation, has a slow release, low release point, and often settles for jumpers when pressured  
- Defensive lapses include taking plays off and poor positioning, with limited ability to defend against bigs in pick-and-roll situations  
- Has potential for growth as a solid NBA athlete with improved shooting mechanics and off-the-ball movement, though recognition and playing level remain concerns
weaknesses.292:
- Struggles with guarding and rebounding against taller players due to lack of size and agility.  
- Limited offensive fluidity and athleticism, with no explosive first step or elite speed.  
- Ineffective as a spot-up shooter and lacks consistent scoring ability, especially in the post.  
- Overly aggressive defensively, leading to frequent fouls and poor footwork.  
- Average rebounder for his size and lacks the ball-handling skills required at the NBA level.
weaknesses.293:
- Green has superior scoring ability but lacks ideal size and strength for a guard, weighing only 180–185 pounds.  
- He struggles against bigger defenders due to poor balance and limited jumping ability, making it hard to penetrate the paint.  
- His finishing ability, especially with contact, is below average, and he needs to improve his floater to be effective at the next level.  
- There are concerns about his ability to guard multiple positions in the NBA, and he may be forced into a point guard role, requiring stronger passing skills.  
- He focused heavily on offense this season, resulting in less effective defensive output per minute and uncertain efficiency in a secondary role.
weaknesses.294:
- Lacks lateral speed and straight-line speed by NBA PF standards  
- Poor rebounding ability on both ends, despite increased strength in college  
- Short stature hinders defensive and rebounding effectiveness  
- Limited vertical leap, showing weak explosiveness in the NBA Draft Combine  
- Limited shot attempts at the free-throw line, reducing impact of strong shooting despite high-level FT skills
weaknesses.295:
- Below average explosiveness makes finishing at the rim challenging in the NBA.  
- Needs significant improvement in three-point shooting, which remains inconsistent.  
- Must pass better to enhance team play and decision-making.  
- Requires greater confidence when shooting.  
- Needs to improve his ability to finish at the rim effectively.
weaknesses.296:
- Evan Mobley lacks physical bulk, weighing only 215 lbs and needing 20–25 lbs more to compete effectively in the post and absorb contact.  
- His finesse game and current lack of strength make him vulnerable to overpowered NBA bigs, especially defensively.  
- He struggles with defensive patience, falls easily for pump fakes, and needs better timing on jumps for blocks and rebounds.  
- He has strong potential as a ball-handler but could enhance his offense with improved decision-making and a reliable post fadeaway.  
- Mobley is soft-spoken and needs to develop vocal leadership to better command the team.
weaknesses.297:
- Above-average athlete by NBA standards with great length, but occasionally disrupted by physical play.  
- Better at scoring than shooting; had a 44% three-point percentage as a sophomore on low shot volume.  
- Needs to improve catch-and-shoot ability, expand range, and increase consistency from the perimeter.  
- High efficiency driving the basket limits perimeter development, though it's difficult to expect him to shoot more.  
- Struggled in clutch situations and can become overly ball-dependent or careless with passing; limited ability to set defenders up off screens.
weaknesses.298:
- Poor offensive skills with limited post moves, slow execution, and a lack of explosiveness or fluidity  
- Predictable offensive decisions, mechanical movements, and difficulty reading the game  
- Struggles with ball handling, frequently bobbles passes, and has weak hands  
- Inconsistent rebounding, poor foul line shooting, and limited ability to play outside the paint  
- Raw basketball IQ, questionable conditioning, poor motor, and concerns over character and academics
weaknesses.299:
- Lacks athleticism, shot-creation ability, and upside  
- Scores mainly on tips or dump-offs, with no face-up game or jump shot  
- Relies on right-hand shot with poor defensive contestability due to shot orientation  
- Prone to foul trouble, averaging 3.2 fouls in 23 minutes of play  
- Inconsistent performance, poor free throw shooting (never above 64%), and high turnover rate due to poor ball-handling
weaknesses.300:
- Lacks elite burst and explosiveness, limiting his ability to guard power forwards full time and earn consistent minutes.  
- Has average wingspan and poor physicality, struggling in post defense and boxing out stronger opponents.  
- Tends to finish plays with minimal energy, favoring lay-ups over dunks, which makes him vulnerable to defensive stops.  
- Overreliant on finesse plays, which hinders performance against physical or high-motor opponents.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from the free throw line, and prone to turnovers due to poor ball handling and telegraphed passes.
weaknesses.301:
- Played as a point guard at Duke but is more accurately described as a scoring guard with limited PG skills.  
- Limited size (6'3") makes it difficult to defend NBA-sized shooting guards and hindered in creating plays.  
- Low assist-to-turnover ratio (1.7 to 1.4) and only 2.7 assists per 40 minutes, ranking dead last among peers in the draft.  
- Struggles in playmaking and creating opportunities, especially when focused on scoring.  
- Moderate defensive ability with decent footwork but poor at cutting off drives and vulnerable to quicker opponents.
weaknesses.302:
- Struggles with athleticism and lateral movement, raising concerns about his ability to thrive in modern offensive systems.  
- Performed poorly in open-court situations at adidas Nations, highlighting potential issues in high-pressure, isolated play.  
- Theoretically fits well in smart, team-oriented offenses like the triangle or Spurs system, but may struggle in isolation-heavy or ball-usage-heavy systems.  
- Shows improving shooting consistency, but his slow release and lack of NBA-level 3-point efficiency remain significant concerns.  
- Questions remain about his ability to handle NBA physicality, adapt to defensive assignments, and overcome a lack of early college production.
weaknesses.303:
- Frank Mason III is 5'11" tall with a wingspan slightly longer than his height, which limits his ability to finish around the rim in the NBA.  
- His NCAA free-throw conversion rate was around 56%, but he is expected to struggle with conversion at the professional level due to his size.  
- He can be caught in the air without a clear plan and still exhibits occasional recklessness in gameplay.  
- Mason is vulnerable to being shot over, making him a target for opponents in the NBA.  
- While his defensive effort is tough and persistent, his stature limits his overall defensive impact, and his remaining upside is uncertain at age 23.
weaknesses.304:
- Needs to build muscle to improve strength and performance, especially against physical play  
- Shooting release is inconsistent, leading to lack of reliability from long range  
- Must become more vocal and take greater initiative during games  
- Too unselfish and passive, often avoiding contact and settling for mid-range shots  
- Struggles defensively, particularly against physical players and in pick-and-roll situations due to poor stance and reliance on length
weaknesses.305:
- Lacks explosiveness and foot speed, making it difficult to create his own shot in the NBA.  
- Struggled from deep, shooting only 34% from three-point range, which limits his offensive impact.  
- Inefficient at two-point shooting (38%), with low assist rate (7.7%) and tendency to shoot jumpers instead of driving to the hoop.  
- Averaged 12.5 points per game with limited high-scoring games (only six games of 20+ points), indicating a limited offensive threat.  
- Struggles with offensive rebounding (only 20 in 28 games) and showed minimal production growth from freshman to sophomore year.
weaknesses.306:
- Needs to improve his offensive creation beyond garbage bucket shots.  
- His physical build is well-suited for European basketball.  
- May be slightly undersized for effective play in the NBA paint.  
- Has potential to thrive in a European league due to his body type.  
- Offensive development is a key area for improvement in the NBA.
weaknesses.307:
- His defense is improving with quick hands and strong steal stats in tournaments.  
- He needs to gain weight to be more effective defensively and explosively at the NBA level.  
- Some opponents can effectively play post up against him, highlighting a defensive vulnerability.  
- His tendency to disappear during games may stem from the team's reliance on more consistent teammates.  
- With consistent effort in the gym, he has the potential to develop a balanced and fit frame, and possesses clutch ability.
weaknesses.308:
- Undersized for a small forward; lacks polish for a shooting guard role  
- Limited athleticism, especially in slow-paced games due to poor jumping mechanics  
- Needs to build upper body strength to improve shot creation and contact absorption  
- Inconsistent shot creation; relies on catch-and-shoot or catch-and-drive plays; weak left-hand finishes  
- Struggles with defense, particularly against big, athletic wings; poor game reading and passing; needs to develop post-up ability and pick-and-roll skills
weaknesses.309:
- Lawal is raw offensively and lacks a reliable low-post move.  
- He frequently commits travels and struggles to create space or put the ball on the floor.  
- His outside shooting lacks proper mechanics and confidence, with a below-average free-throw percentage (.541).  
- He needs to develop his ability to beat defenders off the dribble to become a difficult opponent to guard.  
- Lawal lacks experience and tends to commit cheap fouls, leading to unnecessary foul trouble.
weaknesses.310:
- Harris is undersized and lacks elite athleticism for a shooting guard, with a 6'6.75" wingspan and limited explosiveness.  
- He struggles to finish at the rim and relies heavily on jumpers due to poor handle and speed, often facing tough defenses.  
- Offensive efficiency is limited, with a low 35% three-point shooting and a tendency to take contested shots.  
- He lacks experience as a lead guard and can be easily hedged out in pick-and-roll situations by taller, more athletic bigs.  
- Needs significant improvement in ball handling, off-the-ball movement, and consistency on mid-range shots to elevate his ceiling.
weaknesses.311:
- Has good shot selection but often forces contested jump shots.  
- Lacks explosive ability, elite lateral quickness, and the speed to play point guard.  
- Solid ball handling with a straight-line driving style; lacks shift and playmaking versatility.  
- Strictly a shooting guard; limited size and skills for forward roles.  
- High school offense centered around him; may struggle in college if he doesn’t maintain leadership and offensive presence.
weaknesses.312:
- A bit too small for the power forward position with limited burst for wing play  
- Ineffective in transition despite improved shooting  
- Not an explosive athlete; shooting still needs work and must be automatic when left open  
- Will need to prove he can shoot effectively against NBA defenders  
- Strong rebounder, but limited value as a guard relying primarily on rebounding; must show effectiveness playing down the court as a forward
weaknesses.314:
- Has a thin frame requiring significant muscle gain without losing flexibility  
- Inconsistent performance with frequent ups and downs during games  
- Struggles with ball handling, shooting selection, and decision-making  
- Inefficient scorer and unreliable 3-point shooter with poor pull-up game  
- Lacks first-step speed, poor isolation ability, and struggles in pick-and-roll situations and against physical defense
weaknesses.315:
- Lacking in lateral quickness and struggles to defend the pick and roll against quick guards  
- Easily pushed around for a player his size; needs to bulk up upper body strength  
- Ineffective in pick and roll situations due to inconsistent rolling to the basket  
- Requires improvement in shot mechanics to become a consistent midrange threat  
- Mediocre free throw shooter; needs work on post play, footwork, finishing through contact, and stamina
weaknesses.316:
- Undersized for a shooting guard, with a game still rooted in the style of a small forward, leading to significant size disadvantages.  
- Shows improved shooting but suffers from inconsistency, especially when fatigue sets in due to a flat shot that results in frequent front rim misses.  
- Game flow is often mechanical and unnatural, with moments of overthinking and attempting difficult plays instead of taking available opportunities.  
- High turnover rate (2.2 per game on average, more turnovers than assists) and struggles with offensive rhythm, making him vulnerable when his shot isn't falling.  
- Poor shooting percentages from three-point range (32% as a freshman) and free throws (62%), with a need to improve consistency and shooting efficiency.
weaknesses.317:
- Lack of shooting efficiency: 38% FG, 32% 3PT, and 42% FG inside the arc, with poor shot selection and frequent "hero ball" plays.  
- Poor court vision and low assist production (less than 1 assist per game as a freshman), indicating limited team play and decision-making.  
- Inconsistent ball handling and defensive lapses, including 2.7 turnovers per game, head-down dribbling, and poor reactions under pressure.  
- Defensive shortcomings: lax effort, unfamiliarity with matchups, high center of gravity, and tendency to ball-watch, especially as a helpside defender.  
- Room for improvement in three-point shooting (25% at NBPA Top 100, 34.2% at EYBL) and free throw shooting (56.6–57.1%), as well as post-game development and finishing ability.
weaknesses.318:
- High level of competition is a concern, as it makes assessing his talent and current skill level difficult; he needs to be tested at a higher level than Greek second division.  
- He has significant physical potential with a large ceiling and basement, but lacks elite explosiveness and is too skinny, especially in the lower body.  
- Requires muscle development, particularly in the lower body, to match NBA opponents without losing speed and mobility.  
- Offensive game off the ball is weak, with poor spacing, screen usage, and cuts; most production comes with the ball, and he lacks a mid-range game.  
- Poor three-point shooting consistency (31.3%), unnatural mechanics, and defensive deficiencies due to poor positioning and understanding.
weaknesses.319:
- Struggles with explosive one-foot moves, limiting transition effectiveness  
- Needs improvement in finishing plays with his off-hand  
- Poor decision-making in trapping situations and weak ball-handling in pressure scenarios  
- Lacks dribble speed, overall pace, and lateral foot speed; weak in post defense  
- Has disciplinary issues, including charges related to a nightclub incident and prior misconduct at Georgia Tech
weaknesses.320:
- Often plays a secondary role, showing selflessness but limited star potential despite being the team’s most talented prospect.  
- Struggles as an outside shooter, with a 31% three-point percentage and inconsistent footwork and shot-form.  
- Lacks defensive effort on the rebounding glass, averaging only 3 defensive boards per game.  
- Needs to improve ball security, reduce mental lapses, and develop quicker first-step reactions to handle bigger forwards.  
- Shows promise in offensive creation and fundamentals but requires greater consistency, leadership, and foul-drawing ability to reach NBA level.
weaknesses.321:
- Lacks explosiveness and is not a strong leaper, with inconsistent second jumps  
- Lower body strength and lateral quickness need improvement, limiting defensive and offensive effectiveness  
- Emotionally driven, leading to focus lapses and poor decision-making during gameplay  
- Struggles with finishing at the rim, through contact, and in traffic; inconsistent shooting and free throw accuracy  
- Poorly reads double-teams, has weak passing, high turnover rate, and struggles in pick-and-roll defense due to lack of lateral movement and poor awareness
weaknesses.323:
- Hayward lacks aggression, particularly in high-pressure situations, and does not consistently dominate games when his team needs him.  
- His lack of a post game allows opponents to guard him with smaller defenders, limiting his impact in key matchups.  
- He rarely scores off the dribble and relies on getting to the rim, which may not translate effectively to the NBA.  
- While a decent defender using his size and athleticism, his limited lateral footspeed and strength make it difficult to match up against NBA small forwards.  
- Scouts describe him as "too nice," reflecting concerns about his lack of competitiveness and commitment to becoming a top-tier NBA player.
weaknesses.324:
- Lacks strong post skills and struggles to score consistently from inside the basket.  
- Low field goal percentage for a post player, indicating poor efficiency in converting inside shots.  
- Midrange shooting is solid, but touch around the basket is average at best.  
- Limited offensive versatility; lacks effective back-to-the-basket game and transition ability.  
- Physical limitations including stiff legs, poor body control, and weak one-foot explosiveness reduce overall effectiveness.
weaknesses.325:
- Lacks length, speed, and explosiveness, with limited ability to create space or break down defenses off the dribble  
- Reliant on timing and anticipatory skills defensively; struggles to contest shots effectively and often gets out of position  
- Minimal slashing ability; primarily relies on straight-line attacks and basic 1-2 dribble pullups with little creativity  
- Poor offensive efficiency in traffic; struggles to maintain dribble or finish in the paint, especially against set half-court defenses  
- Solid FT shooter (84%) and good passer, but limited offensive impact without consistent shot attempts and lacks rebounding and playmaking versatility
weaknesses.326:
- Jerrett lacked standout performance at the college level, raising serious doubts about his NBA readiness.  
- Has a very limited offensive game, relying mostly on spot-up shooting with no inside scoring ability.  
- Poor rebounder for his height, one of the worst in the draft, with a high center of gravity due to awkward body shape.  
- Extremely limited athleticism, with poor leaping ability, lateral quickness, and unathletic movement ("duck feet").
