- Inefficient shooting (41.3% from two-point range), high body fat (10.05%), and questions remain about his draft decision after a subpar season.
weaknesses.327:
- More of a scorer than a facilitator with limited playmaking abilities  
- Severely undersized for a shooting guard at 6'1" in shoes and 8'0" standing reach  
- Thin frame at 180 lbs, making him vulnerable to being posted up by bigger guards  
- Limited athleticism and defensive engagement, especially against higher-level competition  
- Struggles to create scoring opportunities for teammates and lacks defensive versatility
weaknesses.328:
- Williams is an undersized forward at 6'7" with limited athleticism, making most of his impact in the post.  
- He is prone to fouling, fouled out in four games, including two against top-tier opponents like Gonzaga and Kansas.  
- Struggles to keep up with quicker guards and lacks the ability to create off the dribble, relying mostly on post play or catch-and-shoot opportunities.  
- Capable from mid-range and free throw line, but inconsistent from three-point range and hesitant to use his jumper.  
- Projected as a late first-round to early second-round pick; could move into the first round if he demonstrates improved dribble defense and a fluid jump shot.
weaknesses.329:
- Undersized and lacks elite athleticism for a shooting guard, with a 6'4" frame, 6'7" wingspan, and 8'1" reach, limiting his ability to guard elite defenders or penetrate defenses.  
- Inconsistent shooting (only shot 45% or above in one season) and poor decision-making, often compounded by bad shooting performances affecting his overall game.  
- Poor ball-handling, lack of creativity, and limited ability to create plays or make teammates better, with a rigid playing style that limits quickness.  
- History of dirty plays, meltdowns, and poor discipline, which have been scrutinized by NBA teams and raise concerns about his on-court behavior.  
- Struggles defensively, lacks lateral speed, and is ineffective against quicker, more athletic players, often failing to rotate or contest shots effectively.
weaknesses.330:
- Struggles to create offense independently, relying heavily on putbacks and broken plays  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting (season average of 32%) with significant room for improvement  
- Poor assist-to-turnover ratio (0.167) and only 10 assists before NCAA Tournament, highlighting lack of ball handling and playmaking  
- Frequently fouled up (6 fouls per 40 minutes) and limited by foul trouble, impacting minutes and consistency  
- Strong defensive versatility and athleticism, but best fit is a rebuilding team that allows development of shooting and offensive efficiency
weaknesses.331:
- Takes a passive, team-oriented approach with limited individual dominance and a lack of killer instinct.  
- Lacks explosive athleticism, quickness, and leaping ability, with ordinary performance in traffic and poor contact absorption.  
- Struggles in the post due to weak footwork, lack of post skills, and inability to handle contact or maintain positioning against stronger opponents.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from mid-range and free throws (down to 63%), with a high-arched shot and poor shot selection when shut down.  
- Needs significant improvements in ball handling (especially with off-hand), defensive intensity, and game awareness to develop a complete, competitive skill set.
weaknesses.332:
- Lacks aggression, poor body control, and strength, making it difficult to finish plays under contact.  
- Struggles with ball handling, passing, and decision-making, often taking excessive risks.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from midrange and off the dribble, due to poor rhythm and elevation.  
- Weak defensive skills, lacking lateral quickness, effort, and ability to contain guards or defend off the dribble.  
- Physically undersized, lacks balance between scoring and distributing, and has poor range and rim attack technique.
weaknesses.333:
- Undersized at 6'8" for a power forward, with limited flexibility due to thick limbs and a barrel chest  
- Still raw in game fundamentals; lacks experience against elite competition  
- Struggles with feel for the game, body control, and consistency in shooting (especially from three)  
- Poor lateral quickness and post defense; tends to gamble on steals and draw fouls  
- Needs to improve ball handling (especially left hand), footwork, screen-setting, and floor efficiency (58.3% from the line, 51% from the floor)
weaknesses.334:
- Not a fast or athletic player, struggles to contain faster opposing wings  
- Has a good shooting mechanism but is inconsistent, especially in long-range and mid-range shots  
- High 2-point percentage stems from attacking the rim and finishing close to it  
- Lacks physical strength for shooting guard (SF) and mobility for point guard (SG) roles  
- Needs to improve physique, shooting consistency, and decision-making (passing vs. shooting)
weaknesses.335:
- His IQ and effort may not fully compensate for significant athletic deficiencies.  
- He is an imaginative passer but often overconfident, leading to turnovers.  
- At 6'11" in shoes, he lacks explosiveness and is not a reliable rim protector.  
- Offensively, his moves are difficult to finish against NBA-level defenders, and his mid-range shooting remains inconsistent, though he has potential to improve.  
- His NBA future hinges on becoming a consistent defensive player, currently lacking in speed and height to defend effectively, and he may not enter the NBA for several years due to preference for Real Madrid.
weaknesses.337:
- Offensively inconsistent, relying heavily on athleticism rather than steady shot selection or decision-making  
- Struggles with shooting consistency from all ranges, including free throws, midrange, and three-pointers (only 16.7% from three in EYBL)  
- Poor shot mechanics with a low release; lacks threat when shooting off the dribble or in half-court situations  
- Reckless ball handling and tendency to overdrive, leading to turnovers and poor court awareness  
- Limited defensive presence despite physical tools; struggles with closeouts, staying in front, and reacting to opponent movement
weaknesses.338:
- Struggles with outside shooting, rarely scoring beyond 12 feet and lacking consistent touch or confidence from the perimeter  
- Poor shooting mechanics from three-point range, with a stiff and unrefined stance limiting his floor-spacing potential  
- Inconsistent free-throw shooting (67% over two seasons), indicating average touch rather than elite shooting upside  
- Athletic limitations include slow foot speed, minimal burst, stiff hips, and lack of lateral quickness, making him vulnerable in defensive switches  
- Limited offensive and defensive impact due to poor rebounding, inability to guard in space, and questions about long-term upside
weaknesses.339:
- Lacks an explosive first step and struggles to create easy shots for himself offensively.  
- Inconsistent on the perimeter due to poor north/south movement and limited ability to attack the rim off the dribble.  
- Overreliant on athleticism, avoids contact, and lacks explosiveness for finishing at the rim.  
- One-dimensional offensive game with no established aggressive go-to move; performance drops when outside shooting is off.  
- Needs to improve left-hand driving, develop a consistent mid-range and perimeter game, and become more aggressive and physical.
weaknesses.340:
- Standing reach of 8'6.5" and barefoot reach of 6'5.25" are small for a combo forward  
- Lacks speed and athleticism, with a slow first step and poor ability to attack the basket  
- Struggles to create offense independently and has below-average shooting (62% over three years)  
- Foot speed is weak defensively, especially on perimeter and in open space  
- Needs to improve as a post player by developing back-to-the-basket moves to use his strength
weaknesses.341:
- Giles suffered multiple knee surgeries, including two ACL tears (both knees) and an MCL tear (left), before high school, raising serious concerns about his long-term career outlook.  
- His ability to explode with contact was diminished, and he struggled to find his rhythm during his freshman season at Duke.  
- He lacks offensive versatility, has poor footwork and touch, relies heavily on putbacks and dunks, and has a weak three-point shot, often missing shots beyond 12 feet.  
- His decision-making on offense is poor, and he hasn’t developed the ability to play within himself or trust his game.  
- Despite flashes of his former potential, his low scoring (3.9 ppg) and rebounding (3.8 rpg) stats suggest he likely would have gone undrafted, though he could become a late teens to early 20s draft steal if he stays healthy.
weaknesses.342:
- Thabeet remains offensive raw and undeveloped, struggling to maintain position in the post due to being rooted out by smaller players.  
- Foot speed and post footwork are areas for improvement, though footwork has shown progress since his Global Games debut.  
- Offensive rebounding and blocking out need more aggressive effort; back-to-the-basket skills and face-up ability are underdeveloped.  
- Free throw shooting is below average but shows potential with repetition and confidence; offensive court awareness is evolving but still lagging.  
- Still young and lacks flying time; leg strength exceeds upper body strength, and his true age is uncertain (estimated 18–22).
weaknesses.344:
- Needs to strengthen his body, bulk up, and improve overall physical conditioning.  
- Has a slow shooting motion with an elbow that tends to swing to the right.  
- Not naturally fluid in play; relies on work ethic to develop his skills.  
- Lacks explosiveness when moving up or down low with both feet on the ground; needs to improve low-post positioning and pick-setting.  
- Must enhance defensive positioning, anticipation, and awareness, especially on the help side, and avoid falling for shotfakes.
weaknesses.345:
- Below average athleticism with minimal second jump and poor explosiveness, limiting effectiveness as a below-the-rim player  
- Inefficient around the rim due to lack of speed and explosive movement; dunks rare and usually on broken plays  
- Better in half-court offense than in transition; struggles with open-floor ball handling due to slow feet  
- Poor perimeter and post defense due to slow feet and lack of physicality or aggression  
- Limited post versatility and pick-and-roll defense; relies heavily on face-up plays and lacks diverse offensive tools
weaknesses.346:
- Jones has improved as a shooter but still lacks the consistency and confidence needed for modern NBA jump-shooting.  
- Most of his points come from spot-ups or drives, and he struggles to shoot away from the basket on the move.  
- He lacks elite speed, making him vulnerable to defenders taking away his slashing ability.  
- His outside shooting threat is insufficient to support a strong offensive role, limiting his potential to be a reliable off-the-bench player.  
- Jones will be 23 before the season starts, making him one of the older draft picks, and his high turnover rate and aggressive defensive fouls raise concerns about his long-term viability.
weaknesses.347:
- Undersized and average wingspan for a shooting guard, lacking strength and length for consistent NBA-level play  
- Not a true point guard; struggles with ball-handling decisions, especially in pick-and-roll situations  
- Overconfident at times, with wild shot selection and questionable attempts to find a scoring rhythm  
- Poor efficiency and finishing, particularly with his left hand when driving to the rim; prone to missed shots in traffic  
- Limited defensive capabilities due to lack of lateral quickness, size, and strength, making him vulnerable against athletic opponents
weaknesses.348:
- Perimeter shooting is inconsistent, with an unrefined motion and struggles as a spot-up shooter  
- Lacks reliability from beyond the arc and has an average free throw percentage  
- Raw shot creation in half court; limited ability to create separation and prone to turnovers  
- No reliable mid-range game and limited scoring versatility outside of transition and cuts  
- Needs strength development to handle physical defenders and time to grow into a consistent offensive contributor
weaknesses.349:
- Limited shooting ability off the dribble and struggles to separate from defenders  
- Projects primarily as a shooter on the wing, not a playmaker or scorer in transition  
- Below-average athlete who frequently gets beat off the dribble and rarely contests shots inside the arc  
- Lacks defensive length, resulting in minimal steal or block production  
- Provides little value to an NBA team if his shot isn’t falling consistently
weaknesses.350:
- Lacks ideal length and foot speed, making it difficult to defend NBA small forwards effectively.  
- At 6’7 with a 6’8 wingspan, he lacks the quickness and physicality to match power forwards.  
- His 8’5 standing reach is adequate for a shooting guard but small for a guard at the 3 position.  
- Needs to improve vision and playmaking to become elite offensively; otherwise, may be valued only as a spot-up shooter.  
- Solid free throw shooting (77%), but must improve to strengthen his shooting as a primary strength.
weaknesses.351:
- Anigbogu struggles with inconsistent touch around the basket, relying heavily on putbacks and lob passes with poor shooting efficiency (48% after offensive rebounds).  
- He lacks explosive leaping ability and court awareness, often forcing tough shots and failing to know when to kick out or reset possessions.  
- His perimeter game is underdeveloped, with minimal jump shot attempts and mechanical execution, and poor free-throw consistency (53.5%) limits stretch potential.  
- In the post, he has limited effectiveness (30.4% post-up shooting), poor footwork, unsecure hands, and high turnover rates (18% when doubled).  
- Defensively, he shows poor discipline (7.6 fouls per 40 minutes), lacks awareness for defensive rebounds (only 19.9% of available rebounds), and is often out of position when chasing blocks.
weaknesses.352:
- The game feels raw with inconsistent performance.  
- He frequently disappears for stretches during games.  
- Struggles to effectively use his size and strength advantage in the post.  
- Post moves are basic and lack NBA-level skill.  
- Ineffective with his right hand and needs improvement in free-throw efficiency.
weaknesses.353:
- Inconsistent performance with poor shot selection and inefficient decision-making  
- Frequently shoots even when closely guarded, including off the dribble with only 20% success rate  
- Shoots nearly 5 three-pointers per game with a low 27.8% three-point percentage  
- Tends to prioritize flashy plays over simple, safe options, leading to unforced turnovers  
- Lacks improvement in jump shooting and has not developed a strong post game despite his size and strength
weaknesses.354:
- Lacks explosive athleticism and strength, struggling to finish near the rim, especially in traffic  
- Tends to settle for low-percentage shots inside the arc, relying on floaters and runners with little head start  
- Mediocre ball-handler; ineffective in half-court dribbling and limited playmaking ability  
- Unselfish but predictable offensively; low assist rates for a guard with experience  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from the free-throw line, and limited defensive impact despite above-average length
weaknesses.355:
- His frame is too thin and he needs to bulk up to compete at the NBA level.  
- Lacks explosiveness, has an average fist step, and struggles with ball handling under pressure.  
- Poor shooting mechanics, slow release, and choppy form limit his perimeter threat and confidence.  
- Tends to avoid contact, overcommits or is passive defensively, and struggles against explosive guards.  
- Has strong length, basketball IQ, and playmaking ability, making him a promising point forward with potential to become a solid NBA player if he improves his size and shooting.
weaknesses.356:
- Okoro is most effective near the rim and struggles from deep, shooting only 28% from three in his freshman season.  
- His shooting mechanics are inconsistent, often affected by closeouts, and he frequently passes up open shots or drives into defense for lower-percentage opportunities.  
- He has a poor mid-range game and limited creativity in isolation, relying heavily on athleticism as a slasher and becoming turnover-prone when dribbling.  
- His free-throw shooting is mediocre (67% in 2019-2020), and he lacks consistency in ball-handling and offensive creation in half-court situations.  
- While he shows potential to develop into a versatile "Swiss Army knife" player, significant improvement is needed in his shooting, ball-handling, and mid-range game to reach NBA-level potential.
weaknesses.357:
- Narrow frame and lightweight (190 lbs), insufficient to withstand NBA-level contact  
- Relies heavily on quickness and slashing, limiting offensive versatility and isolation ability  
- Handle is a major concern, especially in off-the-dribble situations  
- Limited use of left hand, reducing offensive options and balance  
- Poor shot selection and inconsistent mechanics, with a history of injuries and limited exposure in top-level competition
weaknesses.358:
- Limited by lack of height and vertical explosiveness, making perimeter finishing and defense challenging at the NBA level  
- Poor assist-to-turnover ratio and low assist production (3.8 assists per game, 4.3 adjusted per 40 minutes) indicate limited court vision and playmaking ability  
- Struggles with turnovers (3.2 per game) and tends to take unnecessary charges due to aggressive play style  
- Lacks elite competition in college (OVC), which downplays his statistical performance and limits evaluation of true skill level  
- More of a scoring point guard than a playmaker; difficulty transitioning from leading a dominant offense to a supporting role in the NBA
weaknesses.359:
- Struggles with offensive fundamentals, often strong-arming instead of reading defenses, leading to turnovers, fouls, and poor possessions.  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from three-point range (34% 3FG%, streaky performance), with limited mid-range and free-throw reliability (68% FT%).  
- Poor shot mechanics: lacks elevation, uses upper body, and has an erratic follow-through, reducing shot consistency.  
- Defensive issues include upright closing out, poor screen navigation, and occasional hands-on play (2.4 FPG), despite having good athleticism and potential.  
- Primary strength lies in playmaking and facilitation; scoring is limited and not diverse, with future success dependent on improving fundamentals and shot-making consistency.
weaknesses.360:
- Isaiah struggles with shot selection, particularly when creating off the dribble.  
- His field goal shooting is poor at.413%, below his three-point shooting percentage.  
- He tends to over-penetrate and take bad shots over length.  
- His free throw shooting is subpar at 67%.  
- He could benefit from gaining strength to improve defensively, despite having the size and athleticism to guard point guard.
weaknesses.361:
- Emotionally driven, with body language suggesting cockiness or immaturity that can distract from performance.  
- Above-average athlete with inconsistent shooting mechanics, especially under pressure and when closely guarded.  
- Struggles to finish in the paint against physical defenders, often settling for floaters instead of strong drives.  
- Misses easy layups and has poor free throw accuracy (65–72%), with frequent turnovers due to poor decision-making and telegraphed passes.  
- Defensively lazy, lacks fundamental awareness (e.g., verticality), overcommits in pick-and-rolls, and frequently fouls while trying to block shots.
weaknesses.362:
- Averaged only 8.4 points per game with limited offensive versatility, relying heavily on putbacks, alley-oops, and occasional mid-range shots.  
- Lacks a reliable jump shot, poor technique and balance, and struggles to create offense independently.  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting (as low as 7.1% in EYBL) with limited success in stretching the floor.  
- Poor defensive rebounding due to lack of strength, toughness, and poor shot-blocking discipline despite some defensive potential.  
- Needs to develop post game, footwork, and ball skills; also requires improved mobility, weight management, and discipline to avoid foul trouble.
weaknesses.363:
- Struggled to create his own offense, especially against strong and athletic defenders.  
- Poor shot selection and excessive pressure to create shots reduced efficiency.  
- Lacks strength (170 lbs), making it difficult to finish through contact or defend effectively.  
- Limited leaping ability and explosiveness, though core strength could improve this aspect.  
- Poor court vision and tendency to prioritize scoring over creating opportunities for teammates.
weaknesses.364:
- Slightly undersized at 6'7" for an NBA forward, projecting more as a power forward than a guard  
- Limited separation and poor ball-handling off the bounce, leading to reliance on mid-range shots  
- Struggles to finish above the rim or convert inside the arc, with under 50% shooting inside over two seasons  
- Effective primarily as a catch-and-shoot player; scoring drops off significantly when dribbling  
- Lacks explosiveness, post-up ability, and rebounding consistency (career high 6 RPG), with limited upside at 23 years old
weaknesses.365:
- Has little-to-no shot-blocking or rim protection, averaging under one block and steal per game despite a large wingspan and size.  
- Inefficient two-point scorer (46% field goal, under 50% from inside), with only average free-throw shooting (68%) for his size.  
- Lacks foot speed and explosiveness, limiting defensive switching, hedge ability, and perimeter defense.  
- Needs to improve body strength, aggression, and willingness to force his impact; currently prefers to let the game come to him.  
- Older for his class (turns 20 before freshman season), with potential to grow as a stretch-four if he improves shot-blocking or slim down.
weaknesses.366:
- Isaiah Roby averaged 11.8 points per game as a junior on 45% shooting, dropping to 10.9 points on 41% shooting against NCAA tournament teams.  
- He struggles with creating his own shots and lacks the skills to be a consistent one-on-one threat in the NBA.  
- Roby shot only 33% from beyond the arc, limiting his offensive range and effectiveness compared to NBA standards.  
- His field goal percentage at the rim (59%) is sub-par for a forward with his physical tools, indicating offensive efficiency issues.  
- His offensive limitations may restrict his playing time early in the NBA, requiring the right team fit to maximize his defensive strengths.
weaknesses.367:
- Lacks explosiveness and leaping ability, limiting his effectiveness above the rim and in finishing athletic plays.  
- Struggles with post offense, poor ball-handling, and limited ability to create open looks or convert on oops plays.  
- Limited perimeter skills; lacks a three-point shot and range, making him one-dimensional and vulnerable to defensive pressure.  
- Poor foot speed, lateral movement, and first step hinder defensive performance, especially against stretch 4s and athletic centers.  
- Needs improvement in ball distribution, turnover reduction, and offensive versatility, including better post moves, counters, and finishing with both hands.
weaknesses.368:
- Very small in stature, even for a point guard, making it difficult to compete with NBA players  
- Struggles defensively against NBA guards due to size and lack of physicality  
- Tends to prioritize highlight reel plays over simple, effective passes  
- Prone to over-dribbling and poor decision-making in offensive situations  
- Often forces the offense and struggles with transition plays, especially when leaking out for fast breaks
weaknesses.369:
- Needs to add significant strength to improve physicality, enhance rebounding, and establish interior defensive presence.  
- Must develop better focus, consistency, and game awareness, particularly in decision-making between posting and spacing the floor.  
- Should utilize his superior offensive tools near the basket more often and reduce tendency to drift to the perimeter.  
- Requires improvement in shooting consistency, especially from three-point range and free throws, with a history of low 3PT percentages.  
- Needs to tighten ball handling, reduce turnovers, and become a more creative initiator on the perimeter.
weaknesses.370:
- Struggles with shot selection and decision-making, leading to poor offensive efficiency  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from mid-range and spot-up attempts; lacks rhythm and three-point shooting ability  
- Tends to force bad passes and commit turnovers due to tunnel vision and poor decision-making  
- Difficulty finishing in the paint and against defenders due to lack of strength and rhythm  
- Limited defensive lateral quickness and awareness, with concerns over length at the next level
weaknesses.371:
- Wong has an average physical profile for an NBA combo guard, limiting his potential compared to college.  
- He is more of an undersized shooting guard than a point guard, relying on ball screens and personal scoring.  
- Shows average floor vision and facilitation instincts but struggles with consistency and can overcommit to individual plays.  
- Struggles with contested shots and has mediocre efficiency inside the arc, with a streaky 3-point shot.  
- Lacks defensive impact and would likely guard smaller or less threatening players, with no standout NBA-level skill.
weaknesses.372:
- Needs to gain weight and build muscle, especially in the upper body, to improve competitiveness  
- Limited offensive production and lacks development in basketball IQ and offensive execution  
- Struggles with screen timing and execution in pick-and-roll situations, often setting screens too early or late  
- Poor post-up game due to lack of strength, inconsistent positioning, and predictable fundamentals  
- Inconsistent defensive performance, including poor footwork in switches and tendency to rush offense and pump fake excessively
weaknesses.373:
- Wingspan of 6–5 feet; not long or big enough to guard shooting guards  
- Needs to bulk up to avoid being pushed around at the next level  
- Currently plays as a shooting guard; still developing point guard skills  
- Prone to turnovers due to rushed decisions, over-dribbling, and difficult pass attempts  
- Struggles with ball handling (especially left hand), shooting consistency, and finishing against contact
weaknesses.374:
- Rabb is primarily a post player with limited perimeter skills and lacks ideal floor spacing and shooting consistency.  
- He struggles to create offense off the pick and pop, has poor counter-move ability, and cannot effectively attack closeouts.  
- His post game is limited by lack of strength, body control, and a diverse arsenal of moves—relying mainly on height and athleticism.  
- He is ineffective as a rim protector (1.3 blocks per 40 minutes) and lacks defensive versatility, especially in switching or guarding smaller players.  
- Rabb needs to improve his free throw shooting (currently 66%), develop better off-hand skills, and gain strength and size to adapt to modern NBA play.
weaknesses.375:
- Has a history of major injuries (broken foot in 2014, injured knee in 2015) raising concerns about injury susceptibility.  
- Struggles with footwork and lateral quickness, limiting defensive effectiveness, especially against quick guards.  
- Relies heavily on size and length, making him ineffective beyond 5-7 feet from the basket.  
- Lacks offensive versatility; needs to develop counter moves and reduce excessive passing when posting up.  
- Frequently commits offensive fouls and struggles to finish against long defenders or defend pick-and-roll situations effectively.
weaknesses.377:
- Below-average explosiveness, burst, leaping, and quickness for an NBA wing  
- Poor ball-handling and discomfort creating offense in isolation  
- Frequent ball losses, especially when going left; limited effectiveness in pick-and-roll situations  
- Struggles to finish at the rim; low shooting efficiency inside (42% FG, dropping to 37% in conference play)  
- Defensive issues including rigid stance, lack of footwork, and tunnel vision; shows greenness and needs more game experience
weaknesses.378:
- Needs to improve consistency on three-point shots, currently at 30% with room for growth in muscle memory and rhythm.  
- Must reduce turnovers (4.9 per game) and develop better ball awareness and decision-making.  
- Struggles with aggressive passes and forced drives, leading to turnovers and lost possessions.  
- Requires added strength and muscle to better handle contact, finish drives, and compete against more physical guards.  
- Some teams may question his performance due to perceived lack of competition during his sophomore season, despite strong results against top opponents.
weaknesses.380:
- Defense is Parker's weakest area, with concerns over lateral speed, intensity, effort, and inability to effectively guard wings or handle pick-and-roll situations.  
- He is labeled a "tweener" due to unclear fit between shooting guard and power forward, raising questions about his positional versatility in the NBA.  
- Parker struggles with a slow, hitched release on his jumper and lacks consistency in his outside shooting range, which limits scoring opportunities.  
- He has physical concerns including excess body fat, difficulty maintaining optimal weight, and a lack of athleticism compared to elite prospects, despite high-level talent.  
- While intelligent offensively and a strong all-around athlete, Parker needs to improve defensive technique, shot selection, and effort to reach his full potential.
weaknesses.381:
- Rebounding average (6.2 per game) could improve with increased physicality and better rim protection due to sharing the front court with elite bigman Walker Kessler.  
- Struggles to get to the basket on his own, limiting his ability to create scoring opportunities and leading to reliance on perimeter jumpers and mid-range shots.  
- Needs to improve as an isolation (ISO) player to unlock his outside shooting potential and create more space.  
- Often settles for lower-percentage mid-range jumpers after entry passes, especially at the elbow, due to limited drive and creation ability.  
- Tends to drive only to his right; drives to his left frequently result in turnaround jumpers, and requires better ball-handling to enhance offensive versatility.
weaknesses.382:
- Lacks great foot speed and raw athleticism, limiting his ability to defend or get by opponents off the dribble.  
- Has a solid 6’10.75 wingspan, though appears slightly larger on the floor.  
- Struggles with consistency and lacks physical strength, which hinders finishing after contact.  
- 3-point shooting declined to 34% after a strong freshman season (52% from low volume).  
- Turnover rate increased as a sophomore (1.2 to 2.3), and lacks the speed to play as a full-time wing.
weaknesses.383:
- Can handle the ball fairly well but lacks the ability to distribute and elevate teammates.  
- Not a true point guard due to limited playmaking and decision-making.  
- Small frame, measuring just under 6-foot-1, which is undersized for a combo guard.  
- Offensive-minded player who needs to focus on fundamentals and smaller, consistent contributions.  
- Adequate athleticism but lacks the explosiveness to compete effectively against NBA guards in one-on-one situations.
weaknesses.384:
- Evans showed inconsistent performance throughout the season, with eight games of single-digit scoring.  
- Despite leading the team in scoring, his rebounding was poor, averaging only 4.7 boards and never reaching nine in a game.  
- His size as a big guard hasn't translated into strong rebounding or defensive presence above the rim.  
- He often displayed passive selflessness on offense and missed opportunities to capitalize on key plays.  
- Given his age and limited college experience, Evans lacks the reps and development needed to succeed at the professional level.
weaknesses.385:
- Offensive game is predictable, lacking agility and decision-making in isolation situations.  
- Tends to over-commit, leading to turnovers and poor shot selection.  
- Decision-making and game sense require significant refinement.  
- Shooting efficiency was inconsistent, though showed improvement later in the season.  
- Physically undersized at 6-4 with a solid 6-9 wingspan, limiting defensive potential.
weaknesses.386:
- Ivey's aggressive play style can be a strength or a weakness, often leading to unnecessary fouls, poor positioning, and being out of control on both ends.  
- High-IQ opponents can exploit his aggression through charges or live-ball turnovers by baiting him into poor decisions.  
- Offensively, he lacks patience and shot selection when his speed doesn't break down the defense, and needs to improve his mid-range game.  
- He is an average ball-handler who relies on speed and movement, with limited playmaking ability (3.3 apg/2.2 topg) and often telegraphs passes by leaving his feet.  
- His jumper lacks lift and relies on a set shot, making floaters and runners crucial for NBA development, though he shows improved 3-point range.
weaknesses.387:
- McDaniels is a raw prospect with significant inconsistency and inefficiency, especially in decision-making and shot selection.  
- Lacks physicality, explosiveness, and the ability to sustain intensity, struggling with finishing, post play, and playing through contact.  
- Struggles with defensive fundamentals, frequently fouling and failing to move his feet, with poor defensive instincts in NBA-style defense.  
- Poor shot selection and low consistency on long two-pointers, with a tendency to settle for contested pull-ups and poor offensive execution in half-court.  
- Exhibits lack of maturity, poor body language, and disciplinary issues (5 technical fouls in freshman season), along with a need to improve assertiveness and ball-handling.
weaknesses.388:
- Lacks elite explosiveness and athleticism, relying on angles and defensive reads rather than physical dominance  
- Limited size and length relative to height, with poor finishing below the rim in half-court situations  
- Averages 2.4 turnovers per game, indicating poor ball security and ball-handling discipline  
- Has a mechanically sound but slow and fluid shot; lacks an advanced scoring arsenal and relies on cuts for scoring  
- Solid passer with potential to improve against set defenses, but not an advanced playmaker or top-tier scorer
weaknesses.389:
- Crowder lacks elite physical tools, being 6'5" and undersized for a frontline role, limiting his potential as a prospect.  
- He struggles against athletic frontline defenders, especially those with length or speed, and is ineffective when defending against quick or tall players.  
- Poor ball-handling and limited dribbling ability restrict his offensive versatility, with a reliance on straight-line drives.  
- He is not a strong man-to-man defender, lacks shot-blocking ability, and has minimal potential for steals.  
- Crowder depends heavily on team defense and teammates for scoring, and may need to transition to the wing, though lacks the athleticism for sustained play there.
weaknesses.390:
- Lacks lateral quickness, explosive leaping ability, and athleticism, limiting defensive impact and offensive creation.  
- Struggles in pick-and-roll situations, has difficulty moving laterally, and is ineffective against length and athleticism.  
- Poor free-throw shooting (51.1% for the year) and limited ability to stretch the floor, reducing offensive versatility.  
- Inconsistent effort and high turnover rate, with questions about work ethic and defensive intensity.  
- Strong post skills and rebounding potential, but lacks shot-blocking ability and game-changing defensive presence.
weaknesses.391:
- Undersized at the 2G position at 6'4", lacking elite speed and burst in open floor situations  
- More of a scorer than a true point guard capable of running a team and setting the table  
- First step is average; struggles to separate from quick defenders  
- Shot only 64% from the free throw line, despite being a proficient three-point shooter  
- Can be sloppy with passing; while assist-to-turnover ratio is positive (2.2/2.0), he primarily creates for himself rather than for others
weaknesses.392:
- Has strong straight-line speed but lacks elite burst from a standing start.  
- Demonstrated a strong vertical jump (39 inches) at the NBA Draft Combine, but was not seen as highly athletic during his time at UCLA.  
- Struggles with lateral quickness and can be beaten by quicker ballhandlers in isolation situations.  
- Shooting efficiency from three-point range was inconsistent, with a 31% average at UCLA—only a 40% season was anomalous.  
- Has a relatively small 8.3'5" standing reach for a combo forward and is 22 years old, making him one of the older players in his class.
weaknesses.393:
- Lacks the physical frame and strength to support significant weight, limiting his ability to play as a power forward.  
- Struggles with ball-handling and offensive creation, relying heavily on transition plays and easy shots near the basket.  
- Poor rebounding and limited ability to dominate in the paint due to lack of size, strength, and weight.  
- Inconsistent shooting and limited range, with most baskets coming from dunks or close-range opportunities.  
- Classified as a "tweener" due to lacking both the strength of a power forward and the perimeter skills of a small forward.
weaknesses.394:
- Lack of speed makes it difficult to utilize his skill set effectively at the NBA level.  
- Unlikely to succeed against NBA defenders off the dribble due to slow feet.  
- Struggles defensively and in creating offense due to limited speed and athleticism.  
- Limited wingspan (6'9") hinders his ability to get shots off in the paint.  
- Below-average athleticism and leaping ability, combined with average reach (8'8"), limit his impact on the court.
weaknesses.395:
- Limited ballhandling and shot creation skills restrict his effectiveness as a small forward.  
- As a power forward, his bulked-up frame still makes him vulnerable to traditional 4s.  
- Tends to accept role player duties and sometimes fails to make a significant impact on the game.  
- Athletic but lacks the ability to use his speed to get around defenders; relies more on straight-line driving.  
- Poor rebounder for his size, often pushed off the board and not aggressive in contesting rebounds.
weaknesses.396:
- Poeltl shows promise and a great attitude but may not have the ceiling some believe due to limited offensive tools.  
- He lacks post moves and is a poor free throw shooter, raising questions about his offensive impact.  
- Offensively, his effectiveness depends heavily on teammates creating for him; he struggles to create his own opportunities.  
- Defensively, he needs to improve by contesting shots from the help side and better defending guards driving to the rim.  
- His rim protection is currently underdeveloped and may never be a key strength of his game.
weaknesses.397:
- Brunson is limited to defending only the point guard position and may struggle against taller, faster NBA point guards.  
- His lack of lateral quickness, effort, and shot-contesting ability raises concerns about his defensive effectiveness.  
- Teams can easily force switches, making Brunson a potential liability in match-ups due to his defensive versatility.  
- Over-dribbling leads to turnovers, and improving his mid-range jumper would simplify decision-making and enhance scoring versatility.  
- To succeed in the NBA, Brunson must prove he can defend elite playmakers effectively.
weaknesses.398:
- Limited offensive versatility with little to no back-to-the-basket game; relies heavily on lobs, rim runs, and offensive rebounds.  
- Struggles with improvisation, poor footwork, and weak fundamentals despite athleticism; lacks basketball IQ.  
- Inconsistent shot-making, especially around the rim and from mid-range; takes excessive fadeaways and has poor free throw shooting (62% FT, down to 40% in key tournaments).  
- Tends to foul when defending against savvy bigs (2.7 FPG), and frequently commits moving screen fouls due to poor footwork.  
- Has potential as a defensive player but lacks polish, coaching, and developed post skills needed to thrive in modern basketball.
weaknesses.399:
- Green is a good shooter with inconsistent outside shooting, particularly from deep, showing streakiness in 3PT attempts.  
- He relies heavily on quickness and athleticism rather than technical skill, especially when driving, and favors his right hand for finishes.  
- While he shows improvement as a playmaker, he still has room to grow in making plays for others and expanding his offensive arsenal.  
- Green is not yet a strong defender or rebounder, lacks bulk, and struggles to attack the boards effectively for his size.  
- He has shown maturity and development over his senior year, particularly in the G-League, but must learn to prioritize team success over individual stats.
weaknesses.401:
- Lacks athleticism by NBA standards, with limited speed and quickness  
- Struggles as a transition player and rarely gets to the rim against set defense  
- Low efficiency as a finisher (45% FG inside the arc) and poor decision-making against help defense  
- Limited 3-point shooting (33% as a freshman), though shows flashes and has touch to improve  
- Underutilizes off-ball opportunities; lacks consistent spot-up shooting and doesn’t draw enough contact for his size
weaknesses.402:
- Inconsistent three-point shooting (20% 3PT% in EYBL, 25% in NBPA Top 100) and poor free-throw shooting (63.2% FT%) raise concerns for development and effectiveness at the next level.  
- High turnover rate (more turnovers than assists at season end, 3.6 per game in EYBL) and poor ball security hinder his playmaking and reliability.  
- Lack of experience and early opt-out from the season signal potential attitude issues and lack of team dedication.  
- Defense often sags off him, and he frequently passes up open shots, leading to missed opportunities and inefficient scoring.  
- Needs to develop a stronger scoring mentality and improve decision-making to score when his team needs it most.
weaknesses.403:
- Undisciplined defensively with careless fouls and poor footwork  
- Struggles with box-out ability, often watching the ball bounce off the rim  
- Body development has been lacking, raising concerns about dedication and desire  
- Weighs only 195 pounds and needs to build more strength and muscle  
- Inconsistent performance despite immense talent; underachieves from the three-point arc (32% success rate)
weaknesses.404:
- Standing at 6'2 barefoot with a 6'7.5 wingspan, Pickett lacks significant size or length for a guard.  
- Has below-average athleticism, with a 33-inch vertical that is below NBA guard standards.  
- Struggles to penetrate or drive to the rim due to limited explosiveness and pace.  
- His effectiveness in college relied on a five-second rule allowing him to stay patient and keep his back to the basket.  
- Unable to match the athleticism of peers, which limits his defensive ability and may hinder his performance at the next level.
weaknesses.405:
- Lacked true competition in the Southern Conference and faced minimal shot blockers, limiting development against elite defensive pressure.  
- Struggles with winning off the dribble at the next level due to poor ball-handling and difficulty staying on the floor.  
- Inconsistent foul trouble leads to extended stretches of inactivity and limits his ability to secure a role in professional play.  
- Right-handed dominant with a tendency to spin back to his right when driving left, making him vulnerable to smart defensive assignments on his weaker side.  
- High turnover rate (over two per game in final three seasons) and system-dependent assists, with limited offensive upside due to age, foul trouble, and poor left-hand driving execution.
weaknesses.406:
- Hips and post movements appear stiff and robotic, lacking fluidity and flexibility.  
- Legs are thin and underdeveloped despite off-season strength gains; body symmetry raises potential injury concerns.  
- Jump shot is effective but slow to execute; can become more concise and efficient with time.  
- Limited ball handling ability and poor offensive creation off the dribble; movements lack flair.  
- Good at offensive rebounding but averages low (5.6) in rebounding; turnover rate (2.1) is a concern that needs improvement.
weaknesses.407:
- Inconsistent three-point shooting (33.7% from three) and occasional free throw struggles (76.1% from the line) highlight a need for improvement in shooting efficiency.  
- Defensive awareness is strong, but he can be outmaneuvered by simple offensive moves like change of pace near the basket.  
- High turnover rate (almost three per game) as a freshman indicates inefficiency and a need to improve ball security and decision-making.  
- Played in a relatively easy schedule at Gonzaga, facing a weaker conference, which limits the level of competition and real-world testing of his skills.  
- While solid in passing and overall play, lacks advanced court vision and decision-making, and has inconsistent three-point shooting across key youth tournaments.
weaknesses.408:
- Has burst but lacks elite athleticism to match top opponents in the professional game  
- Lateral speed is a concern and struggles to defend smaller guards off the dribble  
- Needs to use his long arms more to strip guards and improve defensive effectiveness  
- Can be slow on closeouts and lacks strength in fighting through screens and in the post  
- Limited exposure to high-level competition and needs to gain weight to replicate collegiate playing style
weaknesses.409:
- Lacks NBA-ready size and athleticism, with a short standing reach (8'3.5") and limited leaping/explosiveness  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from deep (33.7% 3FG on over 5 attempts per game; career 31.6%)  
- Relies on volume scoring and scheme-driven production rather than natural ball-handling or creation  
- Right-hand dependent, often forces shots and has poor decision-making (2.2:2:3 A:TO ratio)  
- Limited defensive impact, with poor lateral quickness, low turnover generation (0.4 BPG), and questions about defensive positioning at the NBA level
weaknesses.410:
- Franklin's game is questioned for NBA translation, with critics calling him a wild, undersized (6'5) combo forward who overpowers opponents primarily in garbage baskets.  
- He struggles to utilize explosiveness in contact situations and has poor dribbling mechanics, often dribbling upright and lacking corner turn ability under pressure.  
- His shot selection is inefficient, with 33% of isolation attempts resulting in jumpers and a low three-point shooting percentage (28%), despite attempting 4.5 per game.  
- He is ineffective in transition (0.9 PPP) and lacks playmaking instincts, committing 3.4 turnovers per game and showing poor breakdown skills.  
- Off-court incidents, including flipping off a fan and a reported flip-off to a referee, have damaged his character image and will be crucial in draft evaluations.
weaknesses.411:
- Limited playing time and lack of PG experience in college hinder skill development at the point guard position.  
- Appears timid with the ball in non-scoring situations and makes poor defensive decisions, often losing focus off the ball.  
- Struggles as a playmaker, averaging only 2.3 assists per game, and relies heavily on outside shooting (15 shots per game).  
- High turnover rate (2.6 per game, 13.6%) and inconsistent shot selection, including poor shooting in key games.  
- Lacks elite foot speed and length (6'6.5" wingspan), limiting his ability to defend NBA-level athletes effectively.
weaknesses.412:
- Undersized for a point guard at 6'0" barefoot with a 6'3" wingspan  
- Lacks range on his shot and needs to improve efficiency from NBA range  
- Not a strong scorer, averaging only 12.9 ppg as a senior  
- Struggles to create shots in isolation, performs better in catch-and-shoot situations  
- Tends to be gamble-heavy, often jumping pass lanes and leaving his defender open
weaknesses.414:
- Lacks ball handling skills and is hesitant with the ball, showing limited ingenuity in creating offense.  
- Drives predominantly to his left and struggles with directional changes and balance.  
- Dribbles with his head down and commits frequent turnovers and offensive fouls.  
- Poor passing ability (1.3 assists per game) and limited basketball IQ.  
- Needs to improve mid-range game and add bulk to enhance ball handling and overall effectiveness.
weaknesses.415:
- Athleticism is stronger in half-court settings, with limited effectiveness in open floor scenarios.  
- Regressed as a three-point shooter from 35% (freshman) to 29% (sophomore), despite doubling his shot attempts.  
- Overconfidence leads to poor shot selection and suboptimal passing decisions.  
- Limited playmaking ability and average passing (1.8 assists per game, nearly 3 turnovers) hinder offensive execution.  
- Needs improvement in shooting and passing; potential as a spark-plug scorer off the bench, but high turnover rate limits offensive impact.
weaknesses.416:
- Lacks a quick first step, reducing threat in one-on-one situations  
- Gets sloppy, forces passes, and averages 3.0 turnovers per game  
- Limited high-level experience, having played only two years at Long Beach State  
- Competed in the Big West Conference, which is not among the top competitive levels  
- Lacks upper body strength and lateral quickness, making him vulnerable in the paint
weaknesses.417:
- Struggled with two poor NCAA Tournament performances due to facing zone defenses, forcing him out of his comfort zone.  
- Lacks the size and leaping ability typical for a guard, limiting his effectiveness in contested drives.  
- Inconsistent driving, especially going right, with defenders easily cutting off his lanes.  
- Needs to improve midrange shooting and shot-making off the dribble before help defense rotates in.  
- Turnover-prone (3.4 per game) due to being asked to do too much; body type and lack of explosiveness limit his potential at the next level.
weaknesses.418:
- Inconsistent performance throughout career, with frequent absences and poor streakiness in scoring and playmaking  
- Two years older than his class, limiting growth potential and development timeline  
- Limited first-step explosiveness due to heavy legs and poor lateral movement  
- Struggles with shot selection, taking poor shots early in the shot clock and forcing high-risk plays  
- Needs improvement in free throw shooting (currently at 69%), upper body strength, and game tempo control
weaknesses.419:
- Has an average feel for the game and relies heavily on instincts due to the game being raw.  
- Limited playing time at an established club may have hindered his development.  
- Offensively, he has a narrow skillset, depends on teammates, and struggles with post-up mechanics and left-handed finishing.  
- Poor shooting range, weak free throw shooting, and inconsistent performance in pick-and-roll short rolls.  
- Defensive issues including foul-prone tendencies, poor rebounding, lack of focus, and difficulty in defending pick-and-rolls.
weaknesses.420:
- Limited college playing time and a suspended season raise concerns about his development and overall game consistency.  
- Shows promise as a defensive stretch big with strong potential, but lacks refined basketball IQ and is often rushed or mechanical in the low post.  
- Scoring ability is currently limited; struggles with shot selection, frequently settling for fadeaways and jumpers, with only a 10% three-point shooting rate in EYBL.  
- Needs significant improvement in physicality, strength (especially lower body), and shot mechanics, particularly in handling contact and finishing in the paint.  
- Averaged solid rebounding numbers but remains active on the boards; has potential to improve as a shot creator, relying heavily on others to create offense.
weaknesses.421:
- Lacks a standout go-to skill; generally good across areas but not exceptional  
- Jumper is inconsistent and lacks quickness, suggesting a better fit as a small forward  
- Tends to take poor shots early in possessions and has limited driving ability  
- Ball handling is limited, relying on few dribbles and needing improvement in right-hand usage  
- Limited experience shows in defensive gambling and poor decision-making; basketball IQ remains unclear
weaknesses.423:
- Vesely needs to improve his offensive efficiency and consistency, particularly in shooting (FG, FT, and 3PT) and ball-handling.  
- He is limited to one or two dribbles off the dribble and lacks a developed 1-on-1 game or go-to scoring ability.  
- His rebounding production is low (2.9–4.4 rpg), and he struggles to finish plays under pressure or in contact.  
- He lacks the strength, toughness, and length to effectively play in the NBA, especially in the paint or half-court set.  
- He makes frequent defensive mistakes and shoots off-balance, particularly when contested, requiring more consistency and better shot mechanics.
weaknesses.424:
- Tends to disappear during games when opportunities aren't available.  
- Has limited impact on the game when plays don't come easily to him.  
- His physical presence should have a greater influence on rebounding.  
- Needs to improve his ball-handling to effectively attack long close-outs and use his athleticism.  
- Should work on lateral quickness to better guard faster small forwards.
weaknesses.425:
- Lacks great lateral quickness when defending on the perimeter.  
- Has stiff hips that limit his defensive agility.  
- Can improve as a ball-handler by tightening up loose moves.  
- Needs to develop consistency in his jump shot and speed up shot release.  
- Currently not a consistent threat stretching the floor, with a 30.8% 3PT% and only 1.3 attempts per game in 2021-22.
weaknesses.426:
- Limited athleticism and lack of explosiveness make Butler below average in transition and hinder his ability to create space or finish at the rim.  
- Struggles with ball handling and poor passing decisions, averaging three turnovers per game and lacking as a facilitator despite being a scorer.  
- Ineffective as a rebounder, with only 10 offensive rebounds this season and declining numbers over the past two seasons.  
- Undersized for a shooting guard, lacking vertical, length, and defensive ability to contain taller or faster opponents, especially in transition.  
- Needs to improve isolation scoring, reduce turnovers, and develop better shot creation and defensive versatility to succeed at the NBA level.
weaknesses.427:
- Thin build limits durability against larger NBA defenders; needs to gain weight.  
- Inconsistent shot selection, especially with off-the-bounce jumpers; lacks confidence from deep.  
- Lacks a strong mid-range game and is not a reliable 1-on-1 scorer.  
- Gets to the rim well but is loose with his handling and struggles against shot-blockers.  
- Raw game understanding, prone to poor decisions and overreactions (e.g., steals, stagnation on screens).
weaknesses.428:
- Limited size and athleticism, making him more of a two-guard with difficulty generating explosive plays  
- Tends to rely on pull-ups and struggles to score efficiently at the rim, especially against longer defenders  
- Scoring efficiency varies: 11.3 PPG, 37.5% FG% in 2021, improved to 15.2 PPG, 39.4% FG% in 2022, but still below elite levels  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting: 22.2% to 34% from deep, with limited attempts and poor consistency in key tournaments  
- Lacks diverse scoring tools and significant contributions beyond scoring, limiting NBA off-ball guard potential
weaknesses.429:
- Lacks elite athleticism, with poor explosion, elevation, and lateral quickness, making him vulnerable to longer, athletic defenders.  
- Struggles to create space or operate effectively against mobile, athletic opponents due to limited size, length, and agility.  
- Plays beneath the rim, runs the floor laboriously, and has a weak mid-range jumper with inconsistent shooting.  
- Defensively, he is not dominant, with limited ability to block shots or alter attempts, and is overwhelmed by modern, mobile front-line players.  
- Weight and conditioning are concerns; his 262-pound frame is not solid, and he must improve agility, consistency, and dominance at the college level to remain a lottery pick.
weaknesses.430:
- Poor three-point shooting, with a 27% success rate from distance despite a 33% freshman campaign  
- Struggles with consistency stepping out and lacks reliable outside shooting at the next level  
- Questions about his height and length due to inconsistent measurements across camps  
- Prone to foul trouble, which limited minutes and requires better footwork and body control to stay ahead of smaller defenders  
- Inconsistent defensive effort, lacking intensity, shot-blocking ability, and the ability to lock down opponents
weaknesses.431:
- Still developing physically, with a thin frame and limited muscle/strength, especially in the upper body  
- Offensive game is unpolished; lacks explosiveness, balance, and consistent finishing, particularly in traffic  
- Prone to foul trouble due to defensive undiscipline, limiting playing time  
- Shooting mechanics need improvement: low release, rushed shots, poor balance, and inconsistent form  
- Lacks ball-handling maturity, passing awareness, and decision-making; not a reliable playmaker or low-post threat
weaknesses.432:
- Undersized for an NBA post player, measuring around 6'7 to 6'7.5 in shoes  
- Wingspan, strength, and motor help offset his height disadvantage  
- Struggles to defend face-up guards due to below-average lateral quickness  
- Has a limited offensive game but contributes significantly in other areas  
- Tennessee teams underperformed in the postseason until this year
weaknesses.434:
- Struggles with frequent foot, ankle, and leg injuries, limiting his availability and impacting overall performance.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from three-point range (19% in EYBL) and free throw line (38.6% in EYBL), with difficulty connecting on jumpers.  
- Poor decision-making and turnover-prone play, often forcing passes and overdribbing, leading to poor ball control.  
- Limited scoring efficiency and struggles to finish shots, particularly under contact or with his right hand.  
- Shows defensive potential but needs to improve commitment, leadership, and ability to play under control.
weaknesses.435:
- Standing 6'7", he is small for a power forward at the NBA level  
- Has a streaky three-point shooting style, with a drop in efficiency from 39% to just under 33% as a senior  
- Lacks an explosive first step, making it difficult to blow by defenders  
- Struggles with defensive coverage, particularly on pick-and-pop plays due to limited footspeed and quickness  
- Needs improvement in setting up teammates with accurate passes and in defensive awareness when his man doesn't have the ball
weaknesses.436:
- Jarrett Allen has good end-to-end speed but struggles laterally and exhibits an awkward running gate.  
- He brings the ball down too much in the lane, leading to strip risks, and his post offense is slow, robotic, and inefficient.  
- Allen handles double teams and heavy pressure poorly and turns the ball over when defenders intensify.  
- He has solid footwork and passing awareness but lacks boxing out ability and a strong low-post game.  
- His free throw form is awkward with inconsistent results; he relies on dunks and putbacks and needs improvement in close-range touch and jump hook execution.
weaknesses.437:
- Culver’s 3-point shot is currently effective at the college level but needs continued development to become a high-level shooter in the NBA.  
- He should tighten his handle to improve his play at the next level.  
- His 195-pound frame is vulnerable to larger NBA guards and forwards, requiring strength development to counteract size disadvantages.  
- While his 6’6” height is solid for a swingman, it is not elite, limiting his ceiling in the draft.  
- Culver needs to maintain his current development pace to reach his full potential.
weaknesses.438:
- Ineffective outside shooting, especially to his right, with awkward shot mechanics and lack of comfort turning that way  
- Lacks body strength and frame strength, making it difficult to establish position and finish in the low post  
- Poor foul shooter (only.654 from the free throw line) and prone to fouling due to poor footwork and pump-fake habits  
- Relies heavily on long arms and shot-blocking rather than scoring, lacking a left hand and mid-range jump shot  
- Needs to improve fundamentals, footwork, lateral quickness, and offensive skills in the low post under pressure
weaknesses.439:
- Lacks physicality and size to match bigger, faster defenders, limiting his ability to handle elite guards.  
- Struggles to draw fouls and convert at the line, especially given his frequent rim contact.  
- Average passing and undersized for the point guard role, raising concerns about decision-making under pressure.  
- Shows complacency at times, needing to remain aggressive and involved throughout the game.  
- Limited offensive usage and low shot attempt volume raise doubts about sustained production in a larger role.
weaknesses.440:
- Lacks explosiveness, second gear, and lateral quickness, limiting his ability to penetrate defense without screens.  
- Reliant on pick-and-roll plays; struggles to create separation or operate independently in defense.  
- Tends to settle for low-percentage fadeaways and runners due to inconsistent dribble separation.  
- Ball-dominant but prone to turnovers, overdribbling, and leaving his feet when making passes.  
- Fundamental flaws (e.g., high defensive stance, sluggish closeouts) become more problematic at higher speeds and against elite competition.
weaknesses.441:
- Average speed and burst for an NBA guard, with struggles in transition due to poor quick reads and loss of control.  
- Inconsistent finisher with limited touch on runners or floaters and reduced efficiency at the rim in traffic.  
- Tends to get tunnel vision, failing to recognize help defenders or secondary rotations.  
- Still prone to forcing passes or driving into congested areas despite improved playmaking.  
- Limited size, poor off-ball shooting, slow foot speed, and defensive vulnerabilities expose him to quicker ball-handlers.
weaknesses.442:
- Evans has defensive limitations at the next level despite a decent wingspan and effort.  
- He lacks strength and elite athleticism for his size, making him easy to shoot over in the NBA.  
- He struggles in the paint, with only a 50.4% field goal percentage against bigger defenders in college.  
- His sophomore season was inefficient, with a 43% shooting clip, which is not sustainable in the NBA.  
- A stronger freshman shooting performance could improve his outlook, especially if he shares offensive responsibilities.
weaknesses.443:
- Hayes has a strong frame but needs to add muscle and improve toughness to compete at the NBA level.  
- He struggles against more physical players, often losing effectiveness when pressured on offense and defense.  
- His defensive potential hinges on gaining physicality and consistency, as he currently lacks edge against stronger opponents.  
- Hayes is prone to fouls, and the pace of the game currently challenges his ability to stay disciplined.  
- He has limited passing ability, averaging only 9 assists all season, and may not be an effective offensive contributor yet.
weaknesses.444:
- Shows progression in the second half of the season but struggles with tunnel vision and over-dribbling, often entering multiple defenders in traffic.  
- Relies on talent rather than a game plan on drives, settling for contested shots in pick-and-roll and taking early 3-pointers too quickly.  
- 3-point shooting declined from 46.4% (freshman) to 33.3% (sophomore), and free throw percentage dropped from 79.1% to 72.7%.  
- Unselfish passer with limited playmaking, averaging 2.7 assists to 3.1 turnovers per game (0.9 A/TO), and tends to prioritize scoring over ball movement.  
- Defensive effort is inconsistent—struggles to stay with his man off the ball, commits fouls on aggressive plays, and lacks leadership in elevating teammates.
weaknesses.445:
- Struggles with consistency in shot selection, particularly from long range and spot-up shooting, with inefficient and unreliable jump shots.  
- Lacks ball-handling and ball security, prone to turnovers, losing the ball in traffic, and bringing the ball low too often.  
- Needs to develop a post game, back-to-the-basket moves, and better off-the-dribble skills to utilize physical tools effectively.  
- Inconsistent focus on both ends of the floor, with defensive lapses and poor offensive rebounding despite size and athleticism.  
- Must improve shooting mechanics, left-hand usage, and game awareness by looking for teammates rather than forcing plays.
weaknesses.446:
- Funky shooting motion and low shooting percentage suggest early career unreliability as a floor spacer.  
- Improved to 33% from three-point range this season, but poor mechanics and frequent misses indicate inconsistent perimeter threat.  
- Requires a complete overhaul of shooting mechanics to become a reliable 3-point shooter.  
- Lacks speed and ball-handling intelligence, making him ineffective as a slasher against top-tier defenders.  
- Injured Achilles required surgery, and even if available, his limited offensive contribution makes consistent minutes difficult.
weaknesses.447:
- Defensive alertness can improve; prone to losing his man and needs better contact handling and leaping ability.  
- Ball handling is solid but inconsistent, with sloppy attacks and frequent slips when attacking the rim.  
- Needs to tighten his handle, improve decision-making, and reduce turnovers (currently 1.8 per game).  
- Poor three-point shooting (only 1 made over 6 games) and limited perimeter threat; free throw shooting requires improvement.  
- Tends to shoot too early, show tunnel vision, and forces the offense; plays with passion but wears emotions on his sleeve.
weaknesses.448:
- Undersized for a shooting guard at 6'4", 200 pounds, requiring proof of effectiveness despite size limitations  
- Struggles with shot selection, often taking forced or inefficient shots  
- Needs to improve ball security, currently averaging 2.9 turnovers per game  
- Provides only modest playmaking, averaging 3.1 assists per game; would benefit from greater facilitation  
- Free throw shooting regressed from 80.0% to 77.9% and would improve if returned to 80%+
weaknesses.449:
- Has middling athleticism with average quickness, burst, and explosiveness  
- Struggles to get to the rim frequently; average first-step defense and limited finishing opportunities  
- Relies on pull-up shots due to difficulty separating from defenders, resulting in low inside shooting efficiency (46% FG inside arc)  
- Limited offensive versatility; primarily a straight-line dribbler with few moves or counters as a shot creator  
- Adequate but unimpactful on defense; lacks consistent fundamental execution and struggles with positioning and screen navigation
weaknesses.450:
- Comfortable shooting from the perimeter with potential to become a capable threat over time, though development will take several years.  
- Extremely hesitant to shoot outside the paint, limiting his offensive versatility.  
- Averaged nearly two turnovers per game, indicating poor ball security.  
- May not produce the same shot-blocking ability at the next level due to reduced paint presence and the physicality of higher-level defenders.  
- Expected to be a defensive asset rather than a high-scoring player throughout his career.
weaknesses.451:
- Not the fastest wing with limited blow-by speed  
- Methodical and somewhat predictable in dribble driving  
- Effective off the dribble despite lack of speed  
- Limited vertical leap and foot speed affect defensive potential  
- Has upside as a feature offensive player, but overall potential is somewhat limited
weaknesses.452:
- Inconsistent defensive intensity and lack of physicality, especially against bigger players or in transition  
- Struggles with closeouts and perimeter defense, lacking stance and resistance against dribble penetration  
- Streaky spot-up shooting (34.3% from three) with limited range and consistency; better as a mid-range shooter  
- Inefficient scoring in traffic due to lack of burst, poor first-step speed, and reliance on floaters and two-pointers  
- Needs to improve as a playmaker and scoring threat off the pick and roll, with questions about effectiveness against elite wing defenders
weaknesses.453:
- Davison struggles with consistent shooting, making only 30% of threes and 33% of mid-range jumpers.  
- He turns the ball over frequently (2.9 turnovers per game) and lacks reliable decision-making.  
- His size and lack of versatility limit his ability to play off the ball or guard multiple positions.  
- He has difficulty navigating picks, leading to easy layups or over-rotations, and lacks strength and frame development.  
- While he shows potential as an attacking bench player, his inconsistency and lack of half-court scoring ability suggest a long development timeline.
weaknesses.454:
- A bit undersized for a traditional power forward but compensated by strong physical tools  
- Limited post repertoire leads to minimal defensive pressure from opponents  
- Struggles to score against taller defenders in the post  
- Overreliance on the left shoulder makes his post movement predictable  
- Defensively undisciplined, prone to foul trouble, and often relies on teammates for scoring opportunities
weaknesses.455:
- Slight frame allows strong guards to body him and limit his driving angles.  
- Not a pure point guard; lacks the mentality and playmaking instincts of a true playmaker.  
- Defensively, he is a walking mismatch at 6-2 but lacks focus and consistent effort.  
- Plays erratically, often overpenetrates, and struggles to read the game, evidenced by a near 1:1 assist-to-turnover ratio.  
- While improving as a shooter and showing potential, his shot mechanics, midrange touch, and decision-making remain inconsistent.
weaknesses.456:
- He is one year older than the graduating senior class (23) and plays alongside players 2–3 years younger on average.  
- Took several years out of high school before becoming an effective college player, delaying his development.  
- Critics question his offensive abilities and effectiveness in pick-and-roll situations due to lack of lateral quickness.  
- Appears rigid and uncoordinated, especially in open-floor scenarios, with limited agility and foot speed.  
- His upperbody strength is average by NBA standards, raising doubts about his long-term effectiveness in today’s NBA.
weaknesses.457:
- Struggles with change of direction dribbling and is overly reliant on 2–3 dribbles offensively, which magnifies his ball-handling weaknesses.  
- Very dependent on his strong right hand; mechanical ball-handling style limits his efficiency and versatility.  
- Mediocre mid-range game with inconsistent shooting, lack of confidence, and tendency to be dared into shooting.  
- No college or NBA three-point range; limited perimeter skills, poor feel for the game, and poor confidence in offensive situations.  
- Tends to disappear during games, becomes passive when not handling the ball, and often makes flashy plays instead of simple, effective ones.
weaknesses.458:
- Struggles with body composition; needs to gain weight and build muscle to improve NBA readiness  
- Underdeveloped perimeter skills, poor ball handling, and no effective three-point shooting (0 made from deep as a sophomore)  
- Awkward shot release and inconsistent shooting, especially from beyond the arc and at the free-throw line (56% FT)  
- Lacks offensive versatility, with no post game and minimal ball movement or playmaking ability  
- Defensive potential exists but requires adjustment from zone to man-to-man defense; limited shot-blocking and playmaking despite physical tools
weaknesses.459:
- Shot mechanics are sound but inconsistent from three, especially off the dribble; needs greater consistency to unlock offensive potential.  
- Tends to settle for contested mid-range shots instead of seeking better looks or drawing contact.  
- Averages only 4.1 assists and 3.4 turnovers per game; needs to improve decision-making and reduce turnovers.  
- More of a scoring guard than a natural facilitator; still transitioning into a full-time point guard role.  
- Needs to develop as a spot-up shooter and improve screen navigation, defensive focus, and rotation discipline.
weaknesses.460:
- Limited physical profile with average length and lack of explosiveness for an NBA frontcourt player  
- Struggles with shot-blocking and is easily outmatched by quicker point forwards or larger players  
- Inconsistent shooting, with declining 3-point (28%) and free throw (71%) efficiency despite solid mechanics  
- Relies on fundamentals and body positioning; poor recovery when defensively beaten or misdirected  
- Most effective when open; lacks elite size or athleticism to dominate in the paint or against bigger defenders
weaknesses.461:
- Very raw and unpolished gameplay  
- Lacks skill, repertoire, and effective footwork to score from behind the basket  
- Has an extremely light frame  
- Easily pushed around in the paint  
- Struggles to establish post position or make strong moves to the basket
weaknesses.462:
- Very thin with limited strength and narrow shoulders, making it difficult to gain bulk and perform effectively against contact at the rim or on defense.  
- Struggles to finish at the rim against contact despite having good length and athleticism; lacks the strength to consistently convert offensive opportunities.  
- Low free-throw attempt rate (3.6 per 37.2 minutes) due to lack of confidence and a tendency to settle for pull-ups and floaters instead of challenging defenders.  
- Inconsistent offensive creation, poor ball-handling against top defenses, and a lack of moves to shake off defenders, especially off the dribble.  
- Three-point percentage declined from 36.8% to 33.6% in 2011–2012 due to increased defensive attention and a shift in team dynamics after Kemba Walker left.
weaknesses.463:
- Poor shooting efficiency: 47% from the floor, 30% from deep, and 60% from the free throw line, with limited improvement potential.  
- Struggles with turnovers when creating offense, turning the ball over four times in five games.  
- Ineffective defensive habits: fouls attackers too often and fails to stay near the rim to contest shots.  
- Lacks consistent offensive production and advanced stats, with limited threat in the post against smaller defenders.  
- Requires a quality development organization to unlock his elite athleticism and potential.
weaknesses.464:
- Lacks decisiveness and polish in his post moves, making him easy to defend and inconsistent offensively.  
- Relies too heavily on his size, leading to poor efficiency and shaky defensive performance.  
- Struggles with basketball IQ, decision-making, and ball handling, often settling for jumpers and committing turnovers.  
- Shows poor work ethic, lacks defensive intensity, and has a history of quitting teams and resisting adjustments to play styles.  
- Has significant character and maturity concerns, with inconsistent performance and inflated self-perception despite overseas struggles.
weaknesses.465:
- Needs to improve shot selection, avoiding forced shots, especially with defenders in his face.  
- Shot efficiency declined from a strong freshman.408% to a.316% from three-point range as a senior.  
- Should focus on quality shot selection and increase deep shooting reps to improve three-point performance.  
- Has potential as a combo guard but lacks elite lateral quickness and defensive intensity to be a lockdown defender.  
- Can improve by being more aggressive in the paint, increasing rebounding, and incorporating a floater into his offensive game.
weaknesses.466:
- Limited offensive versatility, primarily functioning as an at-the-rim finisher with no perimeter skills or floor spacing.  
- Rarely attempted three-pointers in college (only 1 attempt) and showed minimal mid-range shooting ability.  
- Below-average free throw shooting (52% career, declining over three seasons) with no sign of improvement.  
- Lacks patience and decision-making, often rushing when setting screens or making plays, leading to turnovers and offensive fouls.  
- Struggles under defensive pressure, lacks game awareness, and has underdeveloped back-to-the-basket game despite being an experienced player.
weaknesses.467:
- Undersized at 6'4, lacks strong first step and is ineffective with more than 2–3 bounces, especially to his left.  
- Struggles with ball handling under pressure or in transition, limiting his offensive versatility.  
- Relies heavily on spot shooting and fails to exploit closeouts, often settling for contested shots.  
- Good vision and passing ability, but limited by poor space creation and inability to draw help, resulting in few open shots for teammates.  
- Consistently averages more turnovers than assists; defensive effort lacks desired intensity despite physical tools.
weaknesses.468:
- Jordan shows steady improvement in college but has struggled with contact and lacks the toughness expected of a post player.  
- He lacks natural strength, especially in his legs, and appears to have limited ability to build strength due to his age and body composition.  
- Despite being a fluid athlete, he lacks explosiveness, quickness, and strong reaction speed, with average hand and ball-handling skills.  
- He often defers to teammates, lacks intensity, and fails to capitalize on his size and talent by not asserting himself during games.  
- He is right-handed even when moving left, making his offensive moves predictable, and lacks vision and passing ability.
weaknesses.469:
- Turned 21 in February, making him slightly older than average for a first-round prospect.  
- Lacks explosive off-the-dribble ability and is not a strong isolator or ISO player.  
- First step is average, limiting his ability to beat quick defenders and create scoring opportunities.  
- Solid playmaking within team offense, but limited ability to create open looks independently; struggles with length and speed for blow-by situations.  
- 8’2” standing reach is below average for a guard; needs to add strength to improve defensive presence and rebounding.
weaknesses.470:
- Lacks athleticism, explosiveness, and length, with limited penetration when isolated.  
- Struggles to get to the paint and finishes poorly inside (42% FG% inside the arc).  
- Prefers minimal dribbling and is less effective when handling the ball aggressively.
