- Exhibits poor defensive performance with low steal and block totals (0.4 spg, 0.7 bpg) and weak rebounding (2.8 rpg).  
- Shows poor lateral quickness, is vulnerable to switching matchups, and has a tendency to leak out in transition.
weaknesses.471:
- Jevon Carter has a fearless demeanor and strong defensive grit, which will serve him well in the NBA.  
- Despite a good wingspan and leaping ability, his 7’11” standing reach was among the lowest at the NBA Combine.  
- Larger NBA players will be able to shoot over him, making it difficult to finish around the rim.  
- At the collegiate level, he shot only 50% at the rim, a statistic that will become even harder to replicate in the NBA.  
- Carter will turn 23 before his first NBA game, limiting his perceived upside due to age and lack of size advantage.
weaknesses.472:
- Good all-around player but lacks a standout individual skill  
- Fits the "jack of all trades, master of none" profile  
- Not a stand-out athlete or consistent offensive creator  
- Relies on teammates and is not an isolation player  
- Needs to add range to his jump shot and show consistent three-point threat
weaknesses.473:
- Limited half-court offensive skillset with difficulty creating his own shot  
- Underdeveloped low-post game, especially with left hand; lacks touch and consistency around the rim  
- Poor shooting form, mechanical mechanics, and inability to stretch the floor or hit jumpers in games  
- Inconsistent free throw shooting (53%) and poor footwork/scoring moves on the block  
- Overaggressive defense leading to foul trouble, poor post defense, and lack of rebounding technique and team communication
weaknesses.474:
- Joan Tomas has improved annually through training with Josep Maria Margall.  
- He needs to play at a higher level (e.g., ACB) to showcase his true potential.  
- He must improve consistency on his three-point shooting.  
- He needs to strengthen his physical condition, quickness, and lateral movement.  
- He requires improvement in ball-handling, particularly with his left hand, and lacks confidence in this area.
weaknesses.475:
- Meeks lacks size and length for the SG position in the NBA.  
- He is a rhythm shooter who struggles when out of rhythm or disrupted.  
- Needs to improve shot selection and become more aggressive in attacking the basket.  
- Has limited ball-handling, commits too many turnovers, and struggles in traffic.  
- Defensively, he needs better lateral quickness and help defense to compete at a higher level.
weaknesses.476:
- Below-average speed and athleticism limit defensive effectiveness and ability to compete against faster opponents.  
- Struggles with shot creation, poor footspeed, and limited vertical explosion due to short wingspan (6'6").  
- Ineffective at the rim, making only 56.3% of shots at the basket and struggling from the free-throw line (64% as a senior).  
- Strong shooting skills, particularly from distance (41.7% three-pointers as a freshman), and solid defensive effort with unselfish play.  
- Likely suited as a spot-up shooter in a system like the Spurs’ motion offense; if undrafted, a strong fit in Europe due to shooting and passing abilities.
weaknesses.477:
- Wieskamp is one of the best three-point shooters in his class but struggles with two-point jumpers (38.5% success rate).  
- He has a low free-throw percentage (68%) compared to his three-point performance.  
- His passing ability is limited, averaging less than two assists per game throughout his career.  
- He is not an offensive initiator and lacks the playmaking skills to distribute the ball effectively.  
- Due to Garza’s offensive presence, Wieskamp was rarely the focal point for opponents in college and won’t face double teams in the NBA.
weaknesses.478:
- Limited offensively with a lack of variety in post moves beyond his hook  
- Body appears rigid, with a notably upright playing stance, especially defensively  
- Turned 22 in January, reducing perceived upside compared to underclassmen  
- Has a solid frame for a 225 lb player but could benefit from adding weight  
- Lacks shot-blocking and rim protection despite length and athleticism; could improve ball handling to complement midrange shooting
weaknesses.479:
- Embiid struggled with durability, missing 20% of KU games due to knee and lower back injuries, and averaged less than 24 minutes per game when active.  
- He committed an average of 5.9 fouls and 4.2 turnovers per 40 minutes, showing poor offensive and defensive awareness.  
- Defensively, he often focused too much on the ball, lost track of his man, and was frequently pushed off the block by bigger centers.  
- Despite strong per-40 stats and freshman records in blocks, rebounds, and field goal percentage, his performance against top front lines was subpar.  
- He has physical tools for a dominant center but needs to improve vertical explosiveness, hand positioning, post moves, and defensive intensity to become a true impact big.
weaknesses.480:
- Lacks great length and lower body strength, limiting post presence and ability to hold position.  
- Poor shooting outside the painted area in catch-and-shoot situations with limited range and weak release mechanics.  
- Ineffective in pick-and-pop scenarios due to inability to exploit space when defenders double-team guards.  
- No reliable post game; struggles with pivot foot usage and positioning, especially against taller opponents.  
- Weak ball handling for a perimeter player, limiting dribble exploitation against bigger defenders; defensive struggles against strong big men due to limited wingspan and strength.
weaknesses.481:
- Lacks an outside game, with only one three-pointer attempted in two seasons at Wake Forest  
- Shows limited face-up ability and needs to improve range and consistency  
- Frequently leaves his feet, plays defense standing too tall, and lacks defensive awareness  
- Poor pick-and-roll defense: hedges too high, leaves man open, and allows defenders to load up  
- Needs improvement in footwork, post movement, free throw form, floor movement, and passing fundamentals
weaknesses.482:
- Henson has a thin, narrow build with weak strength and inadequate muscle mass for an NBA power forward, limiting his ability to compete in post-up situations.  
- His upright stance and lack of core strength make him vulnerable to being sealed or bumped off balance, especially on drives.  
- Poor footwork and unorthodox lateral movements, combined with pronated legs, result in clumsy movements and a lack of grace under pressure.  
- Offensively, he lacks consistency in shooting, particularly from the free throw line (career sub-50%) and mid-range, with limited range and no established scoring threat.  
- He relies heavily on length and finesse rather than physicality or fundamentals, struggles to finish shots with contact, and does not project confidence or effectiveness in physical games.
weaknesses.483:
- Jenkins lacks elite athleticism, being slow, quick, and explosive, especially off the dribble.  
- He struggles to create offense off the ball, relying mostly on sporadic slasher moves and mid-range pull-ups.  
- Limited ball-handling and poor transition game due to lack of speed and length; often forced to make ill-advised plays.  
- Needs to develop a reliable floater to improve scoring in tight spaces and increase shot efficiency.  
- Defensively, he lacks size and foot speed, resulting in few steals and limited impact, relying on teammates to mask his shortcomings.
weaknesses.484:
- Wall’s jumpshot is a work in progress with decent form but lacks trajectory, lift, and consistency, making him a non-threat from the outside.  
- He struggles in half-court play, particularly in pick-and-roll situations and set shots, and is more effective in transition and open-floor scenarios.  
- His ball-handling and passing are inconsistent, with 4 turnovers per game and a 1.6 assist-to-turnover ratio that reflects raw point guard skills.  
- He lacks core strength and body control, limiting his ability to finish around the rim and absorb contact, especially in traffic.  
- Wall is athletically gifted but still raw; his development depends on discipline, work ethic, and growth in shot creation, defense, and game-ending intensity.
weaknesses.485:
- Broome is a below-the-rim player with limited ability to finish over length in the NBA.  
- He has below-average foot speed and lateral mobility, making him vulnerable in switch-heavy schemes and against quicker defenders.  
- His three-point shooting is inconsistent, shooting only 27.8% from deep in 2024–25 with poor reliability and limited range.  
- Free throw percentage (58.7%) raises concerns about shooting consistency and long-term touch.  
- Lacks a face-up game, offensive creativity, or ability to operate off the dribble, relying heavily on set plays and post entries.
weaknesses.486:
- Struggles with shot selection, particularly from inside the arc (46% FG from inside, 42% overall FG) and against athletic defenders, often forcing contested shots.  
- Lacks elite offensive instincts, court vision, and decision-making, with an average A/TO ratio (2.1/2.3) and limited passing ability despite being a primary ball handler in college.  
- Relies on angles and off-balanced defenders to create separation; lacks quickness and lateral speed, making it difficult to generate space against top-level athletes.  
- Inconsistent 3-point shooting (30% as a sophomore, long slump), with average release speed limiting opportunities as a deep off-ball shooter.  
- Needs to improve offensive efficiency, playmaking, and shot selection while using defense, athleticism, and hustle to earn minutes early in his career.
weaknesses.487:
- Seen as a development project rather than a big-time athlete  
- Has a good feel for the game but is still raw and needs time to develop  
- Primarily functions as a catch-and-shoot or finisher, limited off-the-dribble creation  
- Decent passer with room to improve decision-making and ball-handling mechanics  
- Defensively slow-footed and upright; needs work on focus, body control, and strength (currently 22 lbs)
weaknesses.488:
- Averaged 4.3 turnovers per 40 minutes due to slow reactions to double teams, poor dribbling, and overestimating his skills.  
- Lacks explosiveness, speed, and defensive aggression, making it difficult to defend against elite perimeter players or block shots effectively.  
- Has a strong scoring ability inside and can finish with authority, but struggles in transition and beyond 15 feet due to inconsistent shooting.  
- Possesses solid offensive production (15.4 PPG, 7.7 RPG) and All-SEC honors, with notable performance against top defenses, but lacks elite athleticism and conditioning.  
- Has potential to become a reliable scoring power forward in the NBA, with a second-round draft outlook and possible first-round chance if workouts impress.
weaknesses.489:
- A 6’7 power forward with limited athleticism and offensive skills (50% FT shooter) has limited effectiveness at the next level.  
- Most of his scoring comes from rebounding and exploiting broken plays, not from strong offensive production.  
- He lacks consistent low-post moves and offensive versatility.  
- To have value, he must prove he can be a reliable midrange spot-up shooter.  
- In the NBA’s pick-and-roll system, his value would significantly increase if he can score consistently from 15–17 feet.
weaknesses.490:
- Fairly one-dimensional player with limited offensive and defensive versatility  
- Thin frame leads to being easily pushed around on defense  
- Lacks speed, quickness, and athleticism comparable to an NBA guard  
- Streaky shooter who struggles when going cold and fails to impact the game consistently  
- Limited physicality, infrequent free throw attempts despite being an 82% shooter, and no star potential
weaknesses.491:
- Lacks strength and explosiveness, limiting his effectiveness against higher-level opponents.  
- Inability to body up and maintain ground, creating issues on both offensive and defensive post play.  
- Mediocre rebounding (5.8 rebounds per game) due to insufficient strength and positioning.  
- Not laterally quick enough to keep up with perimeter and stretch 4s.  
- Insufficient height and strength to guard taller, more powerful centers at the next level.
weaknesses.492:
- Needs significant upper body strength and improvement in post game, with average post skills and limited counter moves  
- Struggles to create his own shot against athletic defenders and often settles for mid-range or long-range shots  
- Tends to pop out after screens rather than roll hard to the basket and lacks strong positioning in the post  
- Poor free throw shooting (below 60% in 2016/17) and infrequent shot attempts at the line despite skill level  
- Defensive issues including losing focus, poor shot-blocking technique, and inability to contain physical defenders
weaknesses.493:
- Lacks fluidity and finesse compared to many European prospects  
- Slow shot release; ineffective at shooting on the move or off the dribble  
- Limited perimeter game, especially when off the dribble  
- Prefers to turn his back to defenders under pressure and uses body positioning to continue toward the basket  
- This strategy works against smaller defenders but becomes a weakness against taller, more athletic opponents
weaknesses.494:
- Lacks a developed perimeter game and relies almost entirely on post offense near the hoop.  
- Has budding post skills but needs teammates to set him up and struggles with passing, especially out of double teams.  
- Plays aggressively, which leads to frequent foul trouble and requires better defensive discipline.  
- Needs to improve defensive habits, including staying stationary when setting screens and guarding stronger players.  
- Still developing physically, with room for growth in strength, explosiveness, and move diversity—especially in avoiding predictability.
weaknesses.495:
- Isaac is thin for his size (205 pounds) and lacks strength in both upper and lower body, limiting his physicality.  
- He struggles on the interior due to poor core strength, difficulty maintaining position, and being easily pushed off the spot.  
- He avoids contact, fails to explode to the rim, and often settles for fall-away jumpers instead of attacking the basket.  
- On defense, he is easily screened, lacks post-guarding consistency, and has poor box-out ability despite good length.  
- He needs to improve his shooting consistency, ball handling under pressure, defensive discipline, and game focus to reach his full potential.
weaknesses.496:
- Lacks shooting consistency, averaging only 24.6% from deep and 62.5% from the free throw line, which limits his minutes and impact at the next level.  
- Struggles with diverse post moves, relying on the same spin move and often failing to score against bigger, stronger defenders.  
- Makes difficult finishes but botches easy ones, indicating a need to develop more reliable offensive skills despite strong athleticism.  
- Turned the ball over as frequently as he assisted (3.5 turnovers per game), showing poor decision-making and a need to improve ball control.  
- Lacks defensive intensity, tends to coast on defense and can be caught off guard, while also not standing out as a rebounder for his size.
weaknesses.497:
- Short and lightweight at 6'7" and 220 lbs, lacking size and strength for a traditional frontcourt player  
- Has wiry upper-body strength and is willing to engage in physical play but faces size disadvantages against NBA centers  
- No threat as a shooter; relies solely on the paint and lacks a mid-range game, limiting offensive versatility  
- Mediocre post moves and footwork, especially with back-to-basket, making post scoring unlikely to translate to the NBA  
- Older prospect (23 at rookie level), with limited development time and significant challenges in improving perimeter skills
weaknesses.498:
- Flynn is 6-feet tall and undersized for the NBA, making it difficult to compete with big men against the rim.  
- He needs to develop a more consistent jump shot, particularly from beyond the arc and at 12–15 feet, to become unguardable.  
- Despite being a strong athlete, his lack of size and occasional over-dribbling raise concerns about his readiness and effectiveness as a point guard.  
- He tends to settle for contested jump shots and plays out of the offense, often taking ill-advised shots and making poor transition decisions.  
- While he has decent court vision and assist numbers, he needs to improve as a decision-maker and reduce turnovers (averaged 3.4 per game as a sophomore).
weaknesses.499:
- Adams is not a great athlete, making it harder for him to score in the paint against NBA bigs.  
- He must significantly elevate his mid-range game to compensate for limited scoring ability in the paint.  
- His defensive performance will be challenged due to lack of lateral and foot speed, though upper body strength may help with contact.  
- He is inconsistent as a three-point shooter, especially in close-out situations, with a low success rate (28%) when defenders recover.  
- Adams needs to become a more consistent shooter and decision-maker in close-out scenarios to establish value in the NBA.
weaknesses.500:
- Plays center at Oregon and is undersized at 6'8" with a 6'11"–7'0" wingspan, lacking size to defend bigger, stronger players.  
- Has strength but can benefit from adding weight to improve defensive performance against larger opponents.  
- Low usage leads to frequent turnovers; struggles with dribbling and forces poor passes.  
- Limited offensive threat, lacks mid-range shooting ability, and has minimal upside as a scorer; post moves are stiff and not effective offensively.  
- Defensive issues include poor boxing out, poor rebounding due to positioning, and difficulty in pick-and-roll scenarios, especially against stronger players.
weaknesses.501:
- Needs to improve strength to compete with NBA point guards and switch effectively against bigger players  
- Struggles with consistency as a shooter, especially from NBA range despite being a streaky scorer  
- Shows elite athleticism in workouts, including top vertical jump (42.5 inches), but translation to game performance is uncertain  
- Lacks sufficient length (6’3.25 wingspan, 7’11 standing reach) for defensive impact and rim protection  
- Has potential to develop into a second-round sleeper or even a starter if he improves consistency and offensive decision-making
weaknesses.502:
- Struggled from beyond the arc, shooting only 28.1% from 3-point range.  
- Inefficient shot selection and streakiness hinder consistency, especially in critical games.  
- Poor offensive decision-making with a low assist-to-turnover ratio, indicating issues as a distributor.  
- Defensive awareness is underdeveloped despite physical ability, with limited statistical efficiency.  
- Needs improvement in strength, shot mechanics, and left-hand shot production, particularly under pressure.
weaknesses.503:
- Inconsistent shot selection, often taking "heat checks" after scoring, with poor form when pulling up off the dribble  
- Struggles with shot form and finish around the basket; frequently fades his shots instead of going straight  
- Tends to drive aggressively with limited lane options, rarely kicking out in traffic and forcing plays  
- Over-reliant on offensive playmaking; lacks defensive stamina due to energy spent on offense, leading to poor defensive positioning  
- Physically undersized at 6’4 in shoes with a standard 6’5.5 wingspan, and exhibits a hunched body posture
weaknesses.504:
- Improved team play since freshman year, though still occasionally takes ill-advised shots.  
- Below-average lateral quickness and athleticism hinder defensive performance, especially against quicker opponents.  
- Prefers dribbling and pull-up jump shots over slashing, limiting his effectiveness as a slasher.  
- Low free-throw volume (3.39 FTA per game) affects consistency and efficiency; shows streak shooting tendencies.  
- Possesses solid all-around talent but lacks a defined niche, killer instinct, and defensive edge against stronger players.
weaknesses.505:
- Inefficient shooting (40% FG as a sophomore, career 39%) with limited rim contact despite athleticism and length  
- Relies heavily on jump shots, showing streakiness rather than consistency despite solid three-point numbers  
- Mediocre ball-handling and shot selection; negative assist-to-turnover ratio (career 1/1.2) and poor passing habits  
- Right-hand dependent; left-hand development is critical for improving as a shot-creator and finisher at the rim  
- Lacks physicality, strength, and defensive durability—prone to fouls (2.3 fpg as a sophomore) and struggles with screens and help defense
weaknesses.506:
- Struggles in traffic and against double teams due to inexperience, leading to frequent turnovers.  
- Poor ball-handling and inconsistent catches allow defenders to converge and disrupt his play.  
- Overreliance on athleticism leads to poor technique, such as improper boxing out and aggressive, uncontrolled finishes.  
- Inconsistent focus and intensity result in poor shooting (60% season average) and lapses in court awareness.  
- Lacks bulk and a killer instinct, with concerns about durability at the NBA level despite strong athleticism and length.
weaknesses.507:
- McRae is thin at 179 lbs with a narrow frame and thin shoulders, making it difficult to gain strength without losing athleticism.  
- He struggles with contact and lacks legitimate strength, raising concerns about his ability to handle physical play.  
- His shooting efficiency is inconsistent, particularly from deep, with poor 3-point performance and unconventional mechanics.  
- Ball-handling and decision-making under pressure are lacking; he doesn't handle tight defensive pressure well and is not a strong isolation player.  
- Despite maturity and improved basketball IQ, he is 23 years old, older than many prospects, and still has room to develop his game feel and fundamentals.
weaknesses.508:
- Lacks height and strength for the power forward position  
- Poor post defense due to insufficient size  
- Needs to improve strength to avoid being bullied in the post  
- Struggles with footwork and back-to-the-basket offense  
- Prone to turnovers, especially when doubled in the post; jump shot consistency and fluidity still developing
weaknesses.509:
- 23 years old (turns 24 in January), one of the oldest players in the class  
- Not an elite shooter, but comfortable taking shots to keep defenders honest  
- Shorter than expected, which limits defensive versatility  
- Limited potential due to age and role-player skill set  
- Lean build that may struggle against larger wings or in post-up situations in the NBA
weaknesses.510:
- Average athleticism and slow lateral movement limit offensive effectiveness  
- Lacks a strong first step and is outmatched by faster, more physical opponents  
- Tends to take questionable shots, especially in high-pressure situations  
- Low shooting efficiency from three-point range (37.4% as a sophomore) despite high attempt volume (6.1 per game)  
- Struggles with ball security, showing 1.3 assists to 2.4 turnovers due to poor ball protection
weaknesses.511:
- Needs to build core strength and muscle to improve athleticism and physicality  
- Struggles with ball security and consistency, often making careless turnovers  
- Lacks focus and can be lackadaisical or nonchalant during gameplay  
- Has average athleticism for a shooting guard and is not a naturally strong athlete  
- Needs to improve playmaking ability and emotional maturity to reach the next level
weaknesses.512:
- Struggles with shooting, particularly from deep (27.8% 3FG%, only 20 made 3s as a freshman), with poor release, touch, and footwork; needs significant improvement to become a viable floor spacer.  
- Limited mid-range scoring and often passes up open shots, making him easy to guard, especially in 1-on-1 situations.  
- Low scoring output (7.2 PPG, season-high 18 points) with minimal shot attempts (10+ in only 3 games), indicating poor scoring efficiency and reliance on shot selection.  
- Raw playmaking abilities with a 0.9:1 assist-to-turnover ratio; teams often disregard his shooting, leading to defensive gaps and open passing lanes.  
- Defensive issues include poor positioning, unnecessary fouls, and a tendency to be overly handsy; prone to turnovers and emotional outbursts, with room to improve decision-making and discipline.
weaknesses.513:
- Still out of shape and struggles with court movement, often lumbering due to constant running.  
- Plays hard but lacks smart decision-making, particularly in recognizing double teams.  
- Lacks ideal length and performs poorly when surrounded by taller, longer defenders in the post.  
- Post moves are developing but not yet refined, having improved since freshman year.  
- Jump shot has good mechanics and touch near the basket but lacks consistency, especially from the free-throw line (50%).
weaknesses.514:
- Still appears more like a shooting guard in a point guard's body, making him undersized by NBA standards.  
- Struggled to establish himself as a primary ball handler, with the team performing better when another player held that role.  
- Experienced a significant drop in three-point shooting (35.7% this season vs. 41.5% previously), showing streakiness.  
- Lacked intensity and proper footwork when guarding the ball, especially in one-on-one situations.  
- Needs to improve defensive intensity, learn to defend point guards, and become more comfortable shooting off screens and driving off the ball.
weaknesses.515:
- Struggles with consistent perimeter attack, leading teams to force deep shots and negate his strengths in driving and finishing at the rim.  
- Has a slow, awkward three-point release from the left hip to the top of the shot, though it is considered a minor issue.  
- Frequently puts his head down in transition, limiting teammate opportunities and contributing to poor ball movement and shot selection.  
- Inability to find open teammates in transition is evident in stats (1.4 APG, 1.7 TO per game) and limits team impact during fast breaks.  
- Needs to improve shot selection, reduce turnovers (averaged 3.8 per game in EYBL), and balance scoring with facilitation to develop as a well-rounded player.
weaknesses.516:
- Has a thin frame and needs to bulk up significantly to improve strength and athleticism.  
- Lacks elite burst on first step and is not a strong leaper, limiting his ability to create separation.  
- Relies heavily on screens to create shots or make plays due to limited offensive independence.  
- Struggles with shooting mechanics: slow release, tendency to lean forward in spot-up situations, and inconsistent shooting (streaky performance).  
- Limited defensive lateral quickness and low stance; best suited for guarding wings, not point guards, due to lack of build and defensive instincts.
weaknesses.517:
- Inconsistent three-point shooter (32% percentage) with poor pull-up shooting and reliance on catch-and-shoot attempts  
- Relies heavily on first-step driving to the right, lacks dribbling creativity and elusiveness in half-court  
- Underdeveloped midrange game and limited offensive versatility; struggles to attack the left side  
- Impulsive defensive style leads to frequent foul trouble; poor ball protection and decision-making  
- Poor free throw shooter (59.1%) and sometimes plays unselfishly, failing to maximize individual impact
weaknesses.518:
- Out of shape and considered one of the least athletic players in the NBA  
- Height is not ideal for the center position  
- Lacks offensive skills beyond rebounding  
- Defensive performance relies on physicality rather than skill or athleticism  
- Limited proven track record, even after a Final Four appearance
weaknesses.519:
- Lacks explosive speed and size, limiting his ability to create open looks against stronger competition.  
- Not a quick leaper, which may become a disadvantage against more athletic opponents.  
- Limited lateral speed hinders defensive efficiency and makes it difficult to stand out defensively.  
- Improved as a shooter but still lacks effectiveness in mid-range shooting.  
- Not a high-level ball handler or versatile enough to provide significant value in a competitive backcourt.
weaknesses.520:
- Limited shooting ability, especially from three-point range and free throws (62.7% FT as a senior)  
- Minimal offensive production with the ball; inefficient as an isolation player  
- Lacks lateral speed, which could be a concern at the NBA level  
- Poor ball-handling and playmaking skills; rarely a primary distributor  
- Needs improvement in footwork, offensive moves, and ball-handling fundamentals
weaknesses.521:
- Jackson's shooting is a major weakness, with only 28% from deep and 54% from the charity stripe, despite having touch and potential for improvement.  
- His shot has a consistent hitch, causing him to release the ball on the way down, limiting consistency and requiring time to correct.  
- His aggressiveness often leads to foul trouble, resulting in 3 technical fouls by Jan 7, 2017, and 4+ fouls in 5 of Kansas’ first 15 games.  
- He has room to improve as a ball handler, often getting sloppy, and should be more involved in every play rather than taking them off.  
- Jackson can become a more consistent scorer from outside and should use his athleticism to create mismatches near the basket, enhancing his overall game.
weaknesses.522:
- Shooting form is the primary issue, with shoulders often not squared to the basket and a starting point on the left side of the body.  
- Shot selection can be too quick; improved consistency and control are needed, especially on layups.  
- Lacks offensive technique and finishing ability when not dunking.  
- Struggles with lateral movement and changing direction when guarding quicker wings.  
- Not the fastest on the open floor, but has a solid frame with potential for growth and strength.
weaknesses.523:
- Game is ball-dominant; needs to improve productivity playing off the ball  
- Unorthodox playing style with poor decision-making and tendency to force plays  
- Not a strong passer or offensive facilitator; lacks natural point guard abilities  
- Limited shot elevation, effective primarily in space as a set shooter  
- Physically undersized for the wing at 6'4" with rigid movement and lack of fluidity
weaknesses.524:
- Lacks the strength to defend elite perimeter players effectively  
- Tends to avoid contact on both offense and defense  
- Inconsistent shooting form, especially under pressure, with low conversion rate (51%) and poor finish at the rim  
- High turnover rate, with a declining assist-to-turnover ratio from junior to senior year  
- Poor defensive awareness, weak stance, and frequent mistakes when guarding off the dribble
weaknesses.525:
- Inconsistent scoring, especially from inside the arc (38%), with poor shot selection  
- Below-average ball-handling and passing ability, often failing to notice open teammates  
- Poor defensive potential, especially against shooting guards at the next level  
- Limited game management; not a natural point guard, even in AAU play  
- Needs significant improvement to become a first-round NBA pick; long-term development required
weaknesses.526:
- Limited explosiveness and difficulty entering the paint in half-court situations, especially if initial moves fail.  
- Right-handed dominance leads to predictable play and struggles with left-side shot conversion.  
- Lacks strength, making him vulnerable when matched up against more physical or similarly sized defenders.  
- Has a solid jumper but inconsistent shooting, particularly in streaky form as a freshman; not yet a reliable marksman.  
- Prefers side-step playmaking over driving, limiting foul-line opportunities and passing instincts, though potential as a complementary playmaker exists.
weaknesses.527:
- Needs to rework his jump shot form from feet to release, with poor free throw shooting (54%) and deep shooting (26% career)  
- Must improve attacking the basket from the left side, as he is right-hand dominant  
- Needs to become more comfortable creating his own shot and reducing reliance on midrange jumpers  
- Committed the fourth-most turnovers (86) in conference play and tends to play in spurts; needs greater consistency  
- Should focus on weight room training to gain weight (196 lbs at draft combine) to become a more effective defender
weaknesses.528:
- Played out of position throughout the season, struggling to find his comfort zone  
- Inconsistent shot form and release, including shooting off the side of the head with an outstretched elbow  
- Defenses heavily challenged his outside shot and limited his driving lanes  
- Lacked elite athleticism and strength, leading to poor finishability on drives and offensive inefficiency  
- Struggled with decision-making, ball handling, and offensive flow, often wasting dribbles and making poor passes under pressure
weaknesses.529:
- Has an impressive highlight reel but struggles with consistency and fundamentals at the NBA level.  
- Poor ball handling with a predictable dribble, slow reaction time, and frequent turnovers, especially in half-court offense.  
- Inconsistent shooting mechanics, including hitches, body twisting, and a lack of strong form, leading to middling FG and 3FG percentages.  
- Lacks strength, agility, and footwork, making it difficult to handle contact or maintain balance under pressure.  
- Limited offensive versatility, relying on jump shots and poor finishing inside, with minimal post scoring and poor playmaking.
weaknesses.530:
- Lacks a solid traditional post game and struggles with positioning back to the basket using his pivot foot.  
- Does not have a reliable hook shot and needs to improve his ability to create space in the paint.  
- Requires work on shot mechanics and developing range beyond the three-point line to become a modern stretch forward.  
- Shows impressive energy but lacks focus and defensive discipline, especially in positioning.  
- Often plays aggressively for steals, which can lead to poor positioning or mismatched assignments.
weaknesses.531:
- Below average athlete with a quick but not explosive first step and average leaping ability  
- Struggles to finish at the rim against elite defenders and lacks shot creation from standstill  
- Relies heavily on ball screens to create opportunities; ineffective without the ball  
- Questionable jump shot and below-average 3-point shooting (especially in FIBA-style lineups); free throw shooting is low (under 60%)  
- Defensive weaknesses include lack of length, poor close-out, poor rotation, and vulnerability to explosive guards and drop defense
weaknesses.532:
- Lacks mobility suitable for a wing forward position  
- Athleticism and fluidity are more aligned with a power forward  
- Has slow shooting mechanics  
- Lacks speed required for the shooting guard position  
- Doesn’t have a quick first step and could struggle to defend quick players
weaknesses.533:
- Inconsistent performance with poor decision-making and a tendency to make hurried plays leading to turnovers  
- Poor assist-to-turnover ratio and limited court vision, with struggles as a passer and finisher at the rim  
- Inconsistent 3-point shooting and questionable shooting selection, often relying on mid-range pull-ups  
- Not a reliable free throw shooter (low 60s) and underutilizes his athleticism to draw fouls  
- Defensive issues including tunnel vision, lack of focus off the ball, missed box-outs, and excessive fouls from over-energetic play and overuse of pump fakes
weaknesses.534:
- Struggled offensively with limited scoring (8.3 ppg) and low efficiency (41.1 FG%)  
- Limited offensive involvement, often looked disengaged and lacked initiative  
- Inefficient from three-point range (24% success rate, one attempt per game)  
- Lacked specific offensive moves and was not a primary playmaker or shot creator  
- Experienced instability in transition from high school to college due to de-committing from LSU
weaknesses.535:
- Unorthodox jump shot with awkward base and knees tucked inside the toes  
- Lacks explosive athleticism, relying on lengthy strides and relentless motor to advance  
- Limited playmaking and shot creation, especially in half-court sets; assists rarely come off the dribble  
- Average free-throw shooter (74% career) with only 3.1 attempts per game in recent seasons  
- Performance declines against high-level opponents; success heavily dependent on system and floor spacing
weaknesses.536:
- Randle is undersized for a power forward with a sub-7 foot wingspan, limiting his effectiveness against taller, more athletic opponents.  
- He struggles to score in the post with low success rates on low-post blocks and limited ability to finish over the shoulder.  
- Randle is heavily left-handed and lacks proficiency with his right hand, a critical flaw for high-level play where both hands are essential.  
- His shooting is inconsistent, with poor three-point range and a reliance on a shaky jumpshot that lacks reliability.  
- His success is heavily dependent on finding the right team system and focusing on post development, as his game is currently built on overpowering weaker opponents.
weaknesses.537:
- Good at multiple aspects of the game but lacks specialization in any one area.  
- Ball-handling skills are below average, limiting his ability to create scoring opportunities independently.  
- Relies heavily on teammates to get him open instead of using advanced fakes or moves to draw defenders.  
- Needs to improve consistency in his jumper, pull-up game, and finishing at the rim, especially with his right hand.  
- Defensive lapses due to poor positioning and lack of engagement, along with below-average rebounding and limited free throw opportunities.
weaknesses.538:
- Lacks explosive athleticism and poor vertical leap  
- Plays below the rim and lacks strong finishing ability  
- No go-to move or reliable scoring against NBA-level talent  
- Solid all-around skills but not exceptional in any area  
- Limited potential as a starter; could provide value as a size-oriented bench player in mid-to-late second round draft pick
weaknesses.539:
- Lacks true position due to insufficient bulk to defend post players and lack of lateral speed to guard quick 3-point shooters.  
- Easily pushed off the block, indicating weak defensive positioning and stability.  
- Needs to bulk up to become a full-time power forward, though may lack the frame for significant weight gain.  
- Underutilizes back-to-the-basket skills; prefers isolating with dribbles over maintaining low post position and using post moves.  
- Limited rebounding potential due to insufficient leg and upperbody strength; playing at Richmond does not demonstrate consistent competitiveness against top-tier talent.
weaknesses.540:
- Coming off a serious shoulder injury that ended most of his sophomore season  
- Turnover-prone, averaging just under three giveaways per game last season  
- Shooting percentage declined in his second year, partly due to shoulder injury  
- Prefers to create scoring opportunities for himself rather than for teammates  
- Needs to improve free throw consistency, midrange shooting, and defensive positioning
weaknesses.541:
- Lacks elite explosiveness, athleticism, and physicality, with a thin, lanky frame that limits performance against NBA-level defenders  
- Struggles with ball-handling in traffic, lacks a strong first step, and has difficulty creating offense in isolation or pick-and-roll situations  
- Inconsistent shooting, with a streaky touch and limited effectiveness at the rim; prefers floaters over contact shots and takes too many long-range attempts  
- Defensive concerns include poor stance, limited lateral quickness, inability to match up with stronger wings, and difficulty containing NBA-level players  
- Needs to gain strength, mass, and explosiveness to reach his ceiling as a small forward or small-ball 4, particularly defensively against bigs like LeBron James and Kawhi Leonard
weaknesses.542:
- Poor three-point shooting, hitting only 30% last season and 33% over college career  
- Lack of fluidity in shooting form and suboptimal shot selection  
- High turnover rate (4.2 per game) that undermines team efficiency  
- Struggles to score in traffic and lacks experience against top competition  
- Needs to gain weight (currently at 185 lbs) to improve physical readiness
weaknesses.543:
- Redshirt freshman will be 20 by the draft and needs significant development time to reach NBA readiness.  
- Likely to spend much of his rookie year in the D-League due to lack of NBA-ready physicality and experience.  
- Still thin for a 7-foot center, lacking strength to compete effectively with NBA big men in physicality and rebounding.  
- Struggles with ball handling under pressure, dribble-happy tendencies, and overthinking during post moves, leading to turnovers and missed shots.  
- Free throw shooting is poor at 51%, and improved physicality and consistency are needed to reach his full potential.
weaknesses.544:
- Played in the Colonial Conference, where he received less recognition compared to stronger conferences.  
- Did not face the strongest competition consistently throughout his career.  
- At 22 as a senior, he is seen as lacking the perceived potential of other guards.  
- Positioned more as a scorer and shooting guard despite playing in a point guard’s body.  
- Had a modestly improved A/TO ratio as a junior (2.9 to 2.6), but his long-term success as a role player remains uncertain.
weaknesses.545:
- Jessup lacks athleticism and speed, particularly in a 6'7" frame, limiting his ability to compete with guards or wings.  
- He is not a strong defensive player and is more likely to be a liability defensively despite averaging over a steal per game.  
- Poor playmaking skills and limited offensive versatility, with only spot-up shooting capability.  
- Inefficient at finishing at the rim, with just 47% success on two-point shots.  
- His one-dimensional offensive style and delay in entering the NBA due to playing in Australia at age 23 raise significant development and team integration concerns.
weaknesses.546:
- Displayed tremendous talent during high school and AAU but struggled with consistency.  
- Shows improved focus and preparation compared to expectations.  
- Needs to demonstrate greater aggression and intensity to unlock his full potential.  
- Shot has high potential but requires refinement in mechanics to increase range and consistency.  
- Should improve from current 62% free throw shooting percentage.
weaknesses.547:
- Limited leaping ability and explosive power; relies on strength, making him vulnerable against taller, longer opponents.  
- Prone to fouls due to lack of discipline in fundamentals, positioning, and poor attitude.  
- Temper and frequent arguments with referees restrict court time to an average of 15 minutes per game.  
- Concerns about maturity and off-court behavior.  
- Game is instinctive and talent-driven; needs improvement in game comprehension, turnovers, and forced shots.
weaknesses.548:
- Allen is a good passer in transition but struggles with vision in half-court isolation due to putting his head down.  
- He is prone to turnovers, particularly due to poor dribbling in traffic.  
- He needs to improve as a finisher in the paint, despite having physical tools, and often avoids contact, leading to off-balance shots.  
- His shooting mechanics are inconsistent, with a line-drive style and limited touch on layups and floaters; volume and consistency remain concerns.  
- At 24, he will be one of the oldest players on the draft radar, raising questions about his offensive upside and ability to refine skills quickly.
weaknesses.549:
- Size remains a significant concern, limiting rotation opportunities and making him a target defensively.  
- Inconsistent jump shot and poor finishing in traffic will be major challenges at the NBA level.  
- Struggles with ball security and turnovers will be amplified by the faster pace of NBA play.  
- Needs to improve defensive versatility, particularly against shooters, and develop better off-ball movement.  
- Must refine his spot-up shooting, off-screen play, and decision-making when handling the ball frequently.
weaknesses.550:
- Jones' size (6’11”) and vertical are promising, but his 220 lbs lack the strength to compete effectively against powerful big men in the paint.  
- Limited to 18 minutes in the Big-12 Championship due to foul trouble and poor decision-making.  
- Averages just under 9 points and 22 minutes per game, significantly below expectations given his skillset.  
- Only averages five rebounds per game and less than 9 rebounds per 40 minutes, falling short compared to his running mate Jericho Sims (12 rebounds per 40 minutes).  
- Shows strong potential for steals and blocks (over 0.9 steals and 0.8 blocks per game) due to leaping ability and length, despite limited playing time and nearly three fouls per game.
weaknesses.551:
- Limited athleticism with below-average vertical explosiveness (24.5” no-step, 31.5” max vertical) raises concerns for guard prospects.  
- Overreliance on downhill drives leads to tough finishes or awkward floaters, especially against longer defenders.  
- Struggles to generate clean looks or finish efficiently in transition due to lack of burst and speed.  
- Shooting efficiency declined in senior year (48.3/31.1/64.8) as he shifted to a playmaking role, impacting consistency.  
- May struggle defensively against bigger, more athletic guards due to limited vertical leap and run-jump ability.
weaknesses.552:
- Struggles with post offense, footwork, and confidence on the block.  
- Movements are gangly; still growing into his body and lacks explosive leaping without momentum.  
- Leaps better off one foot than two, which suits wing play but limits full-court impact.  
- Huge feet weigh him down, hindering quick moves from a standstill and contributing to foul trouble.  
- Balance and lower body strength are weaknesses, though improvements are possible given age and work ethic.
weaknesses.553:
- Still raw with limited game development and lacks a diverse offensive arsenal  
- Lean build, needs to add weight, especially in the upper body, to improve strength and stability  
- Inconsistent shooter; needs to extend range to the 3-point line and improve perimeter shooting  
- Struggles with face-up plays, post-up against physical defenders, and finishing through traffic  
- Poor ball handling when posting up, prone to turnovers, foul-prone, and lacks lateral quickness and hip mobility
weaknesses.554:
- Lacks explosiveness and a quick first step, leading to frequent finishes through contact.  
- Struggles to create space or break away from pro-level defenders, limiting dribbling creativity.  
- Shooting consistency is poor, with a decline from 34% to under 32% from three-point range.  
- High turnover rate (3.7 per game) due to high dribbling and poor decision-making under pressure.  
- Average lateral quickness and limited ability to keep up with fast-paced, athletic guards.
weaknesses.555:
- Lacks consistent offensive skills and standout abilities, making it difficult to score reliably.  
- Will likely play as a perimeter player due to size, but lacks necessary polish and skill for consistent wing play.  
- Struggles with ball handling, especially with his left hand, and has poor dribble-breaking ability.  
- Jumpshot is inconsistent, with a high, sling-like release that results in a flat, unpredictable shot.  
- Poor touch around the basket, lack of defensive discipline, and tendency to take off-balanced shots with low conversion rates.
weaknesses.556:
- At 6'8", Murray may be better suited as a power forward or "small ball" center than a combo forward due to his length and frame development.  
- He is effective in the mid to high post but struggles as a consistent perimeter shot creator, especially facing down NBA-level defenders.  
- While efficient and comfortable handling the ball, he shows inconsistency from deep and is still improving in shooting range.  
- Prefers driving with his right hand, which can lead to awkward possessions when that side of the rim is blocked.  
- Defensively, he relies too heavily on length for steals, lacks lateral speed, and can struggle against physical defenders or perimeter threats.
weaknesses.557:
- His ball handling, especially his first step off the dribble, lacks refinement and was not well-developed at the college level.  
- He is currently ranked among the third or fourth best options for ball handling and scoring, not a standout.  
- He had inconsistent shooting performance in college, frequently making big shots but often missing them.  
- His usage rate is expected to drop significantly from his 29.4% at Ohio State, raising questions about his adjustment to a limited role.  
- Despite maturity and a strong senior year (672 points, surpassing previous three seasons combined), some scouts question his true potential due to a disappointing early career and reliance on less developed opponents.
weaknesses.558:
- Benson lacks strength and is currently too thin, making him vulnerable to being pushed out of the post by stronger players.  
- He struggles to establish consistent positioning, especially against physically dominant opponents of similar size.  
- His lower body strength and motor skills are significant areas for improvement; he often takes too long to enter his moves, making him easier to defend.  
- The competition in the Summit League is not highly challenging, limiting his development against top-level opponents.  
- He shows promise in face-up situations but has not utilized them effectively in college; he is a decent free throw shooter (67%) and performs better on the right block.
weaknesses.559:
- Struggles to establish himself as a go-to offensive threat, relying mainly on putbacks, blocks, and rim finishes  
- Limited offensive creation, especially off the bounce, with a laid-back demeanor and low energy level  
- Inconsistent decision-making, 1.6 turnovers per game, poor anticipation on defense, and slow reaction to attacks  
- Free throw shooting declined from 71% to 63%, and defensive effectiveness diminishes away from the basket  
- Lacks drive, leadership, and quick feet; struggles with double-teams, pass execution, and fouls from aggressive blocking
weaknesses.560:
- Needs to improve free throw shooting, which averaged only 70% and is below guard standards.  
- Inconsistent three-point shooting, with low percentages in EYBL (26.3%) and NBPA Top 100 (21.4%).  
- Overly aggressive driving style leads to unnecessary fouls and missed layups due to lack of control.  
- Struggles with catch-and-shoot situations due to slow shooting motion and poor footwork.  
- Needs to improve playmaking, reduce turnovers (averaged 2.7 per game in EYBL, 1.8 in NBPA Top 100), and increase defensive turnovers.
weaknesses.561:
- Below-average foot speed and athleticism for the NBA  
- Naturally thick legs reduce quickness and explosiveness  
- Lacks leaping ability, making him an ineffective shot blocker  
- Struggles to penetrate against elite big men due to inability to fake and drive effectively  
- Age (born in 1991) is seen as a negative by scouts in terms of long-term upside despite experience
weaknesses.562:
- Kelly is thin despite weighing around 200 pounds and could benefit from strength gains, though significant bulk is not necessary.  
- Physical maturation and strength improvements will greatly enhance his performance in the NBA.  
- His shot can appear flat when driving, but his in-the-lane floater is a major threat to shot blockers.  
- Improving finesse and touch on his in-the-lane shots will strengthen his offensive game.  
- He has strong defensive potential but must maintain focus to avoid questionable fouls and stay disciplined on defense.
weaknesses.563:
- Walker is undersized at 6'1 (likely closer to 6'0), with a standing reach of 7'7.5, comparable to 5'10" PG Isaiah Thomas, limiting his defensive and offensive effectiveness.  
- He is a dynamic playmaker and unselfish distributor but lacks the vision and leadership to serve as a true lead guard or orchestrator.  
- His decision-making is inconsistent, with frequent turnovers (2.7 per game), forced shots, and out-of-control plays due to overaggression and lack of situational awareness.  
- Poor shooting efficiency (43% FG) and a flawed jumper arc limit his scoring, especially from range, with a tendency to waste possessions and get blocked.  
- Defensively, his lack of size and strength make him vulnerable to taller, stronger guards, and he struggles to contain drives or block shots in the paint.
weaknesses.564:
- Scored 9.7 points per game with low usage and declining productivity late in the season  
- Lacks effective shooting form, takes time to shoot, and has poor mid-range shooting (33%)  
- Struggles as a scorer and distributor, with only 1.9 assists and 1.7 turnovers per game  
- Inconsistent free throw shooting (68.9%) and limited three-point shooting (34% on low volume)  
- Best offensive contributions come on fast breaks; needs to improve half-court usage, motor, and rebounding
weaknesses.565:
- Lacks strong scoring ability and relies more on assists than points.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from the perimeter and free throw line.  
- Limited confidence in creating his own shots compared to creating for teammates.  
- Average footspeed and lack of explosiveness restrict defensive potential.  
- Has potential to become a quality starter with a consistent jump shot.
weaknesses.566:
- Standing at 6'1" in shoes with a 6'5" wingspan, Chandler lacks size, making him a defensive liability.  
- Struggles with free throw shooting (60% per game), indicating potential inconsistency in shooting, especially from three-point range.  
- Slow shooting motion forces him to create more separation, limiting efficiency in isolation plays.  
- Requires time to adjust to the professional game, with limited experience in college-level competition.  
- Shows solid ball-handling and passing, but lacks creativity and advanced defensive reads as a playmaker.
weaknesses.567:
- Undersized for the NBA 4-position (6'8", 225 lbs) and plays in a smaller conference (Ohio Valley) that limits exposure.  
- Scouts question standout stats due to competition level and lack of high-impact games.  
- Post skills are underdeveloped; moves are predictable and lack face-the-basket ability.  
- Relies heavily on putbacks, deep positioning, and athleticism for scoring; struggles with consistent finishes.  
- Poor free throw shooting (below 60%); limited passing, vision, and high turnover rate with excessive giveaways.
weaknesses.568:
- Highly dependent on perimeter accuracy; 52% of shots come from behind the arc.  
- Lacks short-range quickness, explosiveness, and offensive handles (no blow-by or shake-up ability).  
- Struggles with shot selection, often takes contested shots with hand in face and fails to react to tight defense.  
- Poor free-throw efficiency (2.5 ft per game, 65% success rate) and limited ball-handling; basic dribble and poor decision-making under pressure.  
- Minimal ball movement; negative assist-to-turnover ratio (1.8:2) and limited defensive awareness, prone to lapses and overreaching.
weaknesses.569:
- Size: Just under 6-9 inches  
- Limited upper body strength  
- Prone to being pushed around  
- Injury concerns require attention
weaknesses.570:
- Shot only 27% from three-point range and needs to improve from the free-throw line (70%).  
- Averaged just 11 points per game and lacks scoring versatility, relying heavily on post scoring.  
- Struggles with decision-making, often over-dribbling and forcing bad shots or turnovers (2.6 turnovers per game).  
- Has more turnovers than assists and tends to get into foul trouble.  
- Has potential to improve defensively and rebounding, but is currently not a plus defender.
weaknesses.571:
- Lacks perimeter skills to be a full-time wing, with limited shot creation despite occasional necessity in college.  
- Struggles with dribble-driven play and has a slow, unorthodox lower body motion that hinders his ability to finish off the dribble.  
- Average low post scoring; more effective facing up or moving without the ball.  
- Good defensive potential but has fundamental issues like an upright stance and out-of-control closeouts that may not translate to NBA-level competition.  
- Limited size at 6'7" barefoot with a 203 lb weight, which is not ideal for a player expected to compete for rebounds at the next level.
weaknesses.572:
- Has experienced two major knee injuries, raising long-term concerns.  
- Will turn 22 in July, older than many players in the draft pool.  
- Questions about consistency and competitiveness at a high level.  
- Limited experience against elite competition, impacting readiness.  
- May struggle transitioning from a starting role to a role player and could end up not thriving.
weaknesses.573:
- Huerter has limited defensive length (6'7 wingspan), which restricts his upside and ability to guard taller players.  
- He lacks the strength to handle NBA-level physicality and needs significant development.  
- He is not a strong finisher at the rim and struggles to get to the basket effectively.  
- Offensively, he is one-dimensional, with poor ball-handling and limited free-throw attempts.  
- He struggles with extended dribbling, is prone to turnovers, and lacks an explosive first step, making him vulnerable against athletic opponents.
weaknesses.574:
- Struggles with court vision due to keeping his head down, leading to turnovers and offensive fouls.  
- Lacks offensive versatility, with only one move in the post and poor ability to create his own offense.  
- Tends to settle for long, contested shots and fails to get to the rim efficiently, especially in traffic.  
- Shows inconsistent performance when not the focal point of the offense, particularly in high-pressure or team-oriented settings.  
- Needs to improve ball handling, perimeter movement, strength, and physical maturity to succeed at the next level.
weaknesses.575:
- Struggles with quick wing players and has a heavy-footed playing style.  
- Frequently fouled, with multiple seasons exceeding 3 fouls per game and being fouled out in several games.  
- Limited offensive potential at the NBA level; primarily a last-resort scoring option.  
- Improved as a floor spacer but remains a poor shooter with a hitched release and inconsistent 3-point accuracy (33% career best).  
- Lacks natural scoring ability, poor first step, and basic offensive fundamentals; playmaking role is limited by low ball-handling and usage.
weaknesses.576:
- Limited rim finishing due to a slight frame, leading to reliance on outside scoring.  
- Most of his scoring comes from beyond the arc, making scoring rhythm inconsistent if shots don’t fall.  
- Only shot 72.1% from the free-throw line, a concerning statistic given his strong field shooting.  
- Tends to prioritize scoring over passing, risking a score-first mindset.  
- Needs defensive improvement and could thrive as a spot-up shooter, offering value as a rookie or second-round pick.
weaknesses.577:
- Needs to improve shot selection and decision-making  
- Tends to get out of control, resulting in 1.4 assists and 1.9 turnovers per game  
- Foul shooting accuracy is low at 52.2%  
- Limited minutes as a freshman (22.1) suggest a small sample size for evaluation  
- Off-court concerns include lack of maturity, team focus, discipline, and focus structure
weaknesses.578:
- Late season knee injury has limited workout opportunities and ruled him out of Summer League  
- Lacks offensive polish; shot quality is average, with limited versatility in ball handling, vision, and passing  
- Offensive game relies heavily on putbacks and close-range shots; development of a legitimate post game is uncertain  
- Weight gain to 263 lbs has reduced mobility and foot speed, impacting overall agility and performance  
- Averaged 52% from the floor and 55% from the free throw line; struggles with shot creation and efficiency near the basket
weaknesses.579:
- Needs to improve upper body strength to enhance offensive rebounding and post play.  
- Relies heavily on offensive rebounds; must develop consistent scoring alternatives.  
- Slow, methodical shooting release allows defenders to close out, leading to inconsistency.  
- Limited lateral quickness makes it difficult to guard perimeter players effectively.  
- Requires improvement in left-hand usage, transition efficiency, and defensive focus to reduce foul trouble.
weaknesses.580:
- Undersized for an NBA small forward, with defensive vulnerabilities due to limited height and poor shot-blocking (0.3 bpg career).  
- Prone to fouls and handsy play on defense, particularly when guarding perimeter players.  
- Relies heavily on his right hand for finishes and often attempts high-risk, low-elevation shots near the rim.  
- Limited shooting range and poor elevation on his jumper, restricting effectiveness against set defenses.  
- Struggles with ball-handling, often drives into no-land, leading to turnovers and offensive fouls (3.1 TOPG as a senior); career free throw percentage (72%) remains a concern.
weaknesses.581:
- Mediocre physical profile and athleticism for an NBA guard prospect, lacking explosive quickness and above-the-rim finishing ability.  
- Struggles with shot selection, relying on low-percentage, contested shots; 33.8% 3PT accuracy with inconsistency and streakiness.  
- Limited playmaking instincts, negative A:TO ratio (2.8:2.9), and tendency to telegraph passes or over-dribble without creating opportunities.  
- Inadequate performance under pressure, especially in pick-and-roll coverage, due to middling speed and poor corner turning ability.  
- Needs significant development in basketball IQ, defensive fundamentals, weight management, and consistency—still green and requires polish and experience.
weaknesses.582:
- Not an elite athlete with raw skills and lacks polish in his game  
- Inexperienced with limited high-level playing time and struggles with body control  
- Poor ball handling, especially in pick-and-roll situations, leading to turnovers  
- Predictable playing style due to strong right-hand dominance and preference to post up on the left shoulder  
- Inconsistent decision-making, shooting, and finishing, along with a tendency to commit fouls and lack confidence in face-up situations
weaknesses.583:
- Shows potential with shooting form and 76% free throw percentage, but lacks proven perimeter scoring and offensive creation ability.  
- Limited ability to create offense from the low post and lacks ball handling skills.  
- Improved stamina but still struggles with cardio, gets winded quickly, and has had in-game vomiting incidents.  
- Foot speed is a weakness, hindering his ability to guard quick perimeter players and switch effectively.  
- Strong defensive rim protection with 1.3 blocks per game, but defensive inconsistencies, poor spacing, and inexperience limit his overall impact.
weaknesses.584:
- Struggles with consistency in ball-handling and lacks effectiveness in isolation sets.  
- Needs to improve strength and explosiveness to better withstand physical defense.  
- Inconsistent in finishing at the rim due to limited power and agility.  
- Struggles with double-teams and on-ball defense, showing middling defensive skills.  
- Requires greater aggression and urgency in gameplay to elevate performance.
weaknesses.585:
- Turns 22 in May, limiting draft upside compared to other prospects  
- Struggles as a facilitator and ball handler in pick-and-roll situations  
- Solid athlete with efficiency in transition but lacks elite explosiveness  
- Could benefit from greater aggression in taking the ball to the basket and drawing contact  
- Limited first step and 2-foot jumper hinder effectiveness against bigger defenders
weaknesses.586:
- Lacks an explosive first step and is not an elite athlete, limiting performance in isolation situations  
- Struggles with body composition; needs to bulk up to improve finishing through contact and overall strength  
- Tends to force the ball, play too fast, and overcommit on offense, leading to poor decision-making  
- Left-hand dominant and predictable, making him easy to read; limited threat with right-hand drives and basket attacks  
- Inconsistent defensively and offensively, with issues in lateral quickness, ball handling, passing, and finishing at the rim
weaknesses.587:
- Has a skinny frame and lacks strength and explosiveness for a shooting guard  
- Lacks length, making it difficult to finish at the rim when defended  
- Limited shot-creating ability and not a dynamic dribble driver  
- Ineffective off the dribble; cannot separate from defenders east/west  
- Low ceiling prospect, primarily viable as an 8th man or spot-up shooter
weaknesses.588:
- Struggles to score from the perimeter in isolation; most success comes from catch-and-shoot opportunities.  
- Lacks polish in pick-and-roll situations and needs to improve his game intelligence and tempo control.  
- Needs to add bulk to his frame (currently 167 lbs) to improve strength, especially in finishing after contact and defensive intensity.  
- Defense remains a work in progress despite strong potential; must focus more on consistent defensive effort and attention.  
- Tends to make turnovers and get sloppy on drives, with limited ability to see plays ahead or elevate teammates' scoring.
weaknesses.589:
- Struggles with pull-up shooting when defenders run him off the line and has poor decision-making in offensive situations.  
- Needs to develop a reliable mid-range game and improve his ability to finish around the rim, especially with his left hand.  
- Suffers when defenders engage his body, raising concerns about his competitiveness and physicality in physical games.  
- Ball handling is weak, particularly in one-on-one situations, where he often turns the ball over due to poor dribble control and poor reading of help defenders.  
- While not a high-scoring threat, he has potential as a defensive stopper and athletic contributor on a strong team, though he must improve perimeter skills and game awareness to succeed in the NBA.
weaknesses.590:
- Very small stature (6'0 barefoot, 7'9 standing reach) creates challenges on both offense and defense.  
- Limited effectiveness in rebounding and defensive disruption, requiring the offense to compensate.  
- Offense must be highly efficient to offset weaknesses when he's not involved in scoring or assists.  
- At 187 lbs, physically close to his maximum capacity, though has decent strength relative to guards of his size.  
- Faces growing difficulty playing against taller, more athletic opponents due to size and lack of physical advantages.
weaknesses.591:
- Performs best with momentum toward the rim, showing solid athleticism in those situations.  
- More effective in half-court scenarios than in open-floor, transition play.  
- Lacks elite foot speed, limiting his ability to take the ball off the dribble against quick defenders.  
- Suspected marijuana possession led to a one-game suspension, but scouts remain unconcerned and his recruiting stock is largely unaffected.  
- Long, athletic defenders can pose a significant challenge to his effectiveness.
weaknesses.592:
- Has a modest vertical leap (32") and lacks elite lateral or explosive athleticism.  
- Free-throw shooting improved significantly (20.6% to 45.5%), but this dramatic increase raises concerns about consistent NBA-range shooting.  
- Struggles with mid-range scoring, often leading to poor shot selection, turnovers, or forced shots.  
- Inflexible positional fit: too slow for the four and too short for a small-ball five without strong rim protection.  
- Effective perimeter defender due to instincts and intelligence, but lacks foot speed and quickness for strong perimeter defense.
weaknesses.593:
- Inconsistent long-range shooting, especially from beyond the arc, with improvement that needs to be proven sustainable.  
- Struggles with shot selection, often dribbling excessively or forcing contested jumpers.  
- Lacks dynamic athleticism and physical presence, making rim finishing difficult against NBA frontcourt players.  
- Overly aggressive defensively, leading to frequent turnovers and foul trouble (2.4 fouls per game, fouled out in 4 games as a sophomore).  
- Shows inexperience at times, lacking cohesion and consistency on both offense and defense.
weaknesses.594:
- Sanders lacks elite athleticism and length, resulting in a near-even wingspan to height ratio that limits defensive versatility and rebounding impact.  
- His lateral quickness is average, making it difficult to stay in front of quicker guards.  
- In transition, he is only average, lacking the speed to consistently outpace defenders down the court.  
- Defensively, he struggles against more athletic opponents due to a lack of explosiveness and length.  
- His rebounding is modest for his size, and his shooting, while solid, is not elite due to a slightly slow release that allows defenders to contest effectively.
weaknesses.595:
- Struggles to generate offense independently or against aggressive defense.  
- Limited ball-handling skills; lacks creativity in breaking down defenders or attacking downhill.  
- Relies on straight-line driving without explosiveness or shiftiness off the bounce.  
- Defensively competent but lacks lateral quickness or physicality to consistently guard NBA wings or twos.  
- Limited rebounding and playmaking; averages only 2.2 rebounds and minimal secondary creation.
weaknesses.596:
- Lacks explosiveness and vertical pop, limiting his ability to finish above the rim or create separation.  
- Poor foot speed and lateral quickness make it difficult to defend quicker guards or switch effectively.  
- Struggles to beat defenders in isolation without screens or misdirection due to a weak first step.  
- Below-average transition skills; slow to change ends and limited ability to create highlight plays in open court.  
- Reliant on shooting (57.1% from 3) with limited offensive tools, raising concerns about long-term effectiveness without strong shot creation.
weaknesses.598:
- Lacks fluidity and quick start/stop ability, particularly laterally, making it difficult to defend athletic NBA wings.  
- Has an average first step, allowing defenders to easily crowd him.  
- Not quick enough to guard perimeter players (3s) and not tall or athletic enough to guard power forwards (4s).  
- Relies on spot-up shooting; has not demonstrated ability to create or score at the NBA pace.  
- Lacks a reliable right-hand dribble and struggles to adapt to the faster tempo of NBA play.
weaknesses.599:
- Turnover-prone with a 1.77:1 assist-to-turnover ratio, inconsistent ball-handling and decision-making  
- Struggled significantly with jump shooting across all levels, lacking efficiency in catch-and-shoot situations  
- Inconsistent offensive output, underperforming as a high-volume scorer despite athleticism  
- Suffered multiple injuries, including two shoulder injuries that sidelined him for much of the 2013–14 season  
- Limited floor time in early seasons due to injuries and questions about long-term durability and development
weaknesses.600:
- Limited shot creation and poor efficiency (42% FG rate) when carrying more offensive load, despite leading Syracuse in scoring.  
- High percentage of field goal attempts from distance (38%) inconsistent with his physical attributes.  
- Predictable off-the-dribble movements and lack of fluidity, with a tendency to tighten up and lack first-step explosiveness.  
- Physical stiffness due to added bulk, reducing quickness and leaping ability; weak wrist strength limits off-balance shot conversion.  
- Draft projection is speculative; lack of growth potential due to age (23) and inconsistent performance post-sophomore season.
weaknesses.601:
- Murray is more finesse-oriented than physical, with adequate contact coverage but limited strength.  
- Less consistent from deep compared to his brother, with a drop in 3FG% from 38% (Sophomore) to 34% (Junior) and multiple games shooting 0-5 from deep.  
- Struggles to break down defenses when guarded by wings or physically similar power forwards.  
- Limited ball-handling ability on the perimeter; moves can be predicted, and he relies heavily on his left hand, which may be a disadvantage against NBA defenders.  
- Has solid rebounding moments but is primarily an area rebounder with only average defensive rebounding production (5.1 DRPG) and a late-career age of 23, raising questions about long-term upside.
weaknesses.602:
- Porzingis has a narrow frame with thin shoulders and lacks significant upper and lower body strength, limiting his defensive post presence and offensive finishing.  
- He struggles to finish at the rim, often settling for mid-range shots due to balance issues after contact and poor post positioning.  
- His inside game is underdeveloped, with most points in the paint coming from offensive rebounds and putbacks rather than effective offensive moves.  
- He lacks rebounding ability, which raises concerns about his role as a traditional center and has led some to compare him to Bargnani.  
- Porzingis needs substantial physical development—especially in strength and frame growth—to transition from a shooting guard in a center’s body into a reliable rim protector and key contributor.
weaknesses.603:
- Lacks elite-level athleticism with a below-average first step and lateral quickness  
- Struggles to defend smaller players when playing guard  
- Difficulty creating shots for himself at times  
- Needs to build strength due to a skinny frame that hinders finishing through contact  
- Improved shooting ability, but still inconsistent from the field
weaknesses.604:
- Has a solid floor presence but a low ceiling and limited upside, described as "below the rim"  
- Limited speed, explosiveness, and length; wingspan equals height, making him ineffective against athletic big men  
- Improved three-point shooting (35% as sophomore, up from 28% as freshman), but no dramatic offensive breakthrough  
- Struggles defensively: lacks lateral speed, shot-blocking feel, and rim protection; defense is inconsistent and relies on offense to compensate  
- Shows signs of peak performance despite not turning 21 until November, with mechanical movement and modest statistical gains
weaknesses.605:
- Kyle Guy is undersized for an NBA shooting guard at 6'0.75 barefoot and 168.7 pounds, making him one of the lightest players in the NBA.  
- He lacks height and muscle, which will hinder his ability to defend NBA guards and defend against shots.  
- His 7'11.5" standing reach makes him easy to shoot over, limiting his effectiveness on defense.  
- He needs to improve his ball handling from above average to good or better to secure regular playing time.  
- Transitioning to a combo guard role, focusing more on facilitation and less on traditional shooting guard duties, would improve his chances of success in the NBA.
weaknesses.606:
- Kuzma is a talented stretch four with promising mechanics but struggles with consistency, intensity, and focus.  
- He is a streaky shooter with only a 29% career three-point percentage and 59% free throw rate, lacking a reliable mid-range or off-the-dribble game.  
- His ball handling is loose, often leading to forced plays, turnovers, and poor shot selection; he lacks fluidity in creating multiple moves or changing direction.  
- Offensively, he struggles against NBA-level defenders, has limited explosiveness, and is ineffective in traffic or against long defenders despite decent length and mobility.  
- Defensively, he plays too upright, fails to close out effectively, and lacks toughness, shot-blocking ability, and consistency in stopping offensive first moves.
weaknesses.608:
- Singler struggles with below-average athleticism, lacking quickness, explosiveness, and lateral quickness, limiting his defensive and offensive potential.  
- He is ineffective on defense, particularly against quicker opponents, and lacks the strength and size to guard bigger forwards or compete for rebounds.  
- His shooting is inconsistent, with poor three-point accuracy and long stretches of inefficient shooting, especially as a senior.  
- He has difficulty finishing at the rim and creating shots off the dribble, relying heavily on the jump shot and struggling to generate scoring opportunities.  
- Singler’s age (23) and limited upside as a "tweener" restrict his potential, as he is not quick enough to guard guards or strong enough to guard bigger 4s.
weaknesses.609:
- Missed all but 8 games of freshman season due to a turf toe injury, raising concerns about durability and whether it was a fluke or a sign of a larger issue.  
- Limited college experience (only 8 games) raises questions about his ability to transition successfully to the NBA.  
- While athletic and advanced for a freshman point guard, lacks elite physicality and does not dominate with highlight-reel dunks or defensive presence.  
- Tendency to over-dribble and lose control when attacking the basket, impacting offensive efficiency and team possession quality.  
- At 6'2", his size is adequate at the college level but offers little advantage in the NBA, and his low shot release point and shot selection need improvement.
weaknesses.610:
- Limited productivity (7.4 ppg) with inconsistent impact and development trajectory  
- Shows promising upside but lacks proven ability to isolate and create against top talent  
- Struggles with first step, foot speed, and offensive urgency, leading to stagnation outside the three-point line  
- Highly effective from beyond the arc (68.4% of shots from 3-point range), but needs to diversify scoring and improve inside game  
- Requires significant improvement in conditioning, defensive intensity, and shot release timing to reach full potential
weaknesses.611:
- Defense needs improvement, especially against quicker ball handlers, with limited shot-blocking (0.7 per game) and steals (0.9 per game).  
- Must add weight to compete with NBA wings and forwards and improve physicality to hang with bigger players.  
- Struggles with screen defense, often loses his man, and needs to fight through screens more effectively.  
- Committed to 2.6 fouls per game; too afraid to pick up fouls, leading to blown opportunities and poor finishing in contact.  
- Needs to extend his 3-point range and improve free throw percentage (currently 69.4%) to meet NBA standards.
weaknesses.612:
- Olbrich lacks elite length and vertical pop, standing under 6’9” with a 6’11.25” wingspan, making him undersized at the 5 and lacking perimeter agility.  
- Averages only 0.6 blocks per game in the NBL, indicating limited rim protection capabilities at the NBA level.  
- Has average lateral quickness and may struggle defending guards or playing in space due to poor switching ability.  
- Minimal three-point threat, with only 0.4 attempts per game and a 9.1% shooting percentage from deep in 2024–25.
