- Lacks a consistent go-to offensive move despite being versatile  
- Inconsistent motor and tendency to float around the perimeter  
- Prone to turnovers when attempting offensive plays  
- Requires continued development of confidence and consistency  
- Shows strong potential as a center with growth evident in sophomore year  
- Has few significant weaknesses and is expected to receive lottery consideration
weaknesses.685:
- Averaged only 0.3 assists per game, indicating limited passing ability.  
- Lacks speed and shows a gingerly playing style, with weak knee strength evident from his large knee pads.  
- Needs to improve ball-handling skills and consistency in jump shot execution.  
- Frequently fouls, committing nearly three fouls per game despite limited playing time.  
- Projected as a late first-round or early second-round NBA draft pick due to strong tournament performance and desirable size, athleticism, and skill set.
weaknesses.686:
- Shot 40.0% from three this season, but career three-point percentage is only 32.3%, raising questions about consistency.  
- Free throw percentage declined to 65.9% in 2024–25, with a rigid and mechanical shooting form.  
- Lacks shot creation in isolation, relying on straight-line drives and no advanced dribbling or mid-range game.  
- Prone to turnovers under pressure (2.6 per game) when forced to make multiple dribbles or reads.  
- Defensive efforts are effective but can lead to breakdowns due to over-helping or gambling; age (24) and late emergence may raise long-term upside concerns.
weaknesses.687:
- Has significant ceiling and star potential but may spend most of his career overseas due to concerns about upside and physical development.  
- Inconsistent outside shooting (29% from 3) allows opponents to sag off him, limiting offensive effectiveness.  
- Struggles with shot selection, routinely passing up open shots, and is prone to turnovers, especially in leadership and decision-making.  
- Requires improvement in upper body strength, defensive transition from 2-3 to man-to-man, and defensive discipline—particularly in high-pressure NBA settings.  
- Character concerns arise from a shoplifting incident during his college season, which may impact team evaluations and credibility.
weaknesses.688:
- Has a solid but not exceptional game, lacking elite explosiveness or quickness despite being athletic  
- Struggles with free-throw shooting (66% last season) and has stiff, inflexible shooting mechanics leading to poor shot quality when missed  
- Inconsistent ball-handling, especially with his left hand, and prone to turnovers (3 per game)  
- Limited rim attack, finishing through contact, and lacks aggression or quickness when driving to the basket  
- Solid defensively but lacks strength, length, and quickness, making him vulnerable off the dribble and when defending low-post plays
weaknesses.689:
- Struggles to create his own shot in half court, often prioritizing rim contact over better short pullups.  
- Lacks range on his jump shot, unable to stretch the defense effectively.  
- Shooting motion is inconsistent, limiting his ability to extend his shooting range.  
- Physically undersized for the next level, making it harder to score easily compared to college.  
- Needs significant improvement as a perimeter ball-handler and must transition from a big forward to a small forward role.
weaknesses.690:
- Porter needs to improve his release speed, especially against longer defenders, and reduce his heavy load-up and long dip in catch-and-shoot motions.  
- He lacks advanced dribble moves and consistency in creating offense off the dribble, relying heavily on athleticism and outjumping opponents.  
- Porter is ineffective in half-court offense, often relying on catch-and-shoot shots and failing to create space or make decisions with the ball.  
- Defensively, he is inconsistent, lacking lateral quickness, poor rotation habits, and insufficient lower body strength when defending the post.  
- His health, particularly related to disc surgery, remains a major concern, and his offensive versatility and scoring options are limited without significant development.
weaknesses.691:
- Lacks strength and physicality, with only 2.8 FTs on 3.4 attempts and a 28.0% FTA/FGA ratio, limiting rim attack and contact finish.  
- At 210 lbs, remains thin for his position and age; could benefit from adding 10 lbs to improve finishing and physicality.  
- Primarily a catch-and-shoot player, with 96.4% of his 3-pointers coming off assists, lacking isolation and drive ability.  
- Played alongside Jalen Brunson, which enhanced his development but limited his growth as an isolation scorer.  
- Has solid foot speed and leaping ability, but struggles with contact, rebounding in traffic, and finishing after contact.
weaknesses.692:
- Lacks explosiveness, quickness, and overall athleticism for the next level  
- Has weak upper body strength and avoids contact during gameplay  
- Relies on finesse, excelling in mid-range shooting but limited on defense and rebounding  
- Averaged 2.4 blocked shots per game, but effectiveness against higher competition is questionable  
- Struggled with 3-point shooting (25% in senior year) and underperformed in NCAA tournament play
weaknesses.693:
- Has average athletic tools and lacks explosiveness, quickness, and agility  
- Struggles to get consistent shots against athletic, long-armed forwards down low  
- Not a strong face-up player or ball-handler; relies on picking action and limited space awareness  
- Developed a face-up game, but its effectiveness in the NBA remains uncertain due to lack of mid-range proficiency in college  
- Limited shot-blocking ability and minimal transition threat; susceptible to quicker defenders on the bounce
weaknesses.694:
- Lacks athleticism and explosiveness, struggling to guard quicker, more athletic players.  
- Poor lateral quickness and slow recovery from screens, making it difficult to keep up with NBA pace.  
- Limited body control, especially when hitting shots after contact.  
- No significant shot-blocking ability and needs improvement in post play.  
- Strong team awareness and understanding of team basketball, but minimal room for development limits NBA potential.
weaknesses.695:
- Bridges is a poor ball handler with limited dribbling ability, primarily functioning as a one or two dribble player rather than a playmaker.  
- He is undersized for both small forward and power forward positions, measuring 6-6 (listed as 6-7) with a 6-9 wingspan, lacking ideal length for defensive disruption.  
- His jumper lacks lift and consistency, with a low release and poor shooting form, limiting his ability to score against taller defenders.  
- He lacks a reliable pull-up jumper and mid-range game, relying mostly on catch-and-shoot opportunities and drives to the rim.  
- Bridges does not show signs of becoming an offensive focal point or consistent shot creator, with limited development in perimeter skills and ball handling at the college level.
weaknesses.696:
- McBride is a hard worker with solid fundamentals but lacks elite athleticism and explosive ability off the dribble.  
- Struggles with isolation scoring, poor burst, and limited ability to blow by defenders or finish at the rim.  
- Shoots with low efficiency (43% 2P%) and lacks shot creation, particularly over taller defenders due to poor jumper lift.  
- Needs improvement in decision-making, transition game, and consistency, especially in taking over and facilitating plays.  
- Has potential as a combo guard; gains strength through core and upper body development to improve physicality and finish.
weaknesses.697:
- Averaged 6.6 points per game this season, the best of his four-year college career  
- Low free-throw volume (2.2 attempts per game) and below-average shooting percentage (63%)  
- Inconsistent jump shot, primarily effective only within eight feet of the basket  
- Limited post movement and struggles against larger defenders  
- Offers value mainly through rebounding; lacks reliable offensive creation and is best suited as an energetic bench role player
weaknesses.698:
- Limited high-level basketball experience; considered an unknown prospect  
- Has potential but lacks proven performance beyond strong 2013 NCAA Tournament run  
- Athletic profile is more akin to Kevin Love than Blake Griffin—less explosive, more versatile  
- Work ethic and conditioning have been questioned, though he hustles hard on the court  
- Faces concerns about consistency, mechanics (especially jumper), foul-prone play, and ability to sustain high-level performance over a full season
weaknesses.699:
- Lacks awareness and is not consistently involved in the offense.  
- Raw in skillset with limited post-game or half-court offensive options.  
- Relies on hustle and physical advantage rather than technique or basketball IQ.  
- Struggles with contested shots, lacks go-to moves, and has a poor free-throw percentage (54.3%).  
- Needs improvement in mobility, passing, screen-setting, and overall basketball fundamentals.
weaknesses.700:
- Will turn 22 in August, limiting perceived upside compared to other draft prospects  
- Struggles with ball security, averaging 3.1 assists and 4.0 turnovers per game  
- Makes careless passes and can be overly unselfish during critical moments  
- Has proven scoring ability (29 points against Miami) but lacks consistency in high-impact games  
- Inconsistent performance in big games, including a poor shooting performance (2-16) in NCAA tournament vs. LSU
weaknesses.701:
- Skill level sits between 4 and 3, with frequent basket scoring due to length and athleticism.  
- Struggles to create easy shots in half court, particularly from the perimeter.  
- Lacks a reliable pull-up off the dribble.  
- Shoots only 44% from the field, a low percentage given his size and athleticism.  
- Primary focus should be improving shooting to maximize effectiveness at the 3 position.
weaknesses.702:
- Bamba needs to add strength, aiming for at least 240 pounds (possibly 250) in a year or two to better handle the paint and improve his rim protection.  
- His offensive awareness, especially when back to the basket, is underdeveloped; he struggles with spacing, cutting, and adjusting to double teams or guard pressure.  
- He relies heavily on catching and finishing for points, with minimal involvement in offensive sets and frequent scoring in single digits.  
- Despite his length, he lacks explosiveness and leaping ability, which limits his impact as a rim protector and stretch big man.  
- His post game is in early development, lacking muscle memory on jump hooks and poor progress when handling the ball or facing double teams.
weaknesses.703:
- Inconsistent scoring threat with stagnant three-point shooting (24-of-75 in Pro A, similar to last season in Pro B).  
- Free throw shooting declined sharply from 78.3% to 51.1%.  
- Projected scoring efficiency around 60% without significant improvement.  
- Occasionally over-dribbles in half-court sets, disrupting ball movement.  
- Strong defensively but limited in post defense and lateral quickness, struggling against quicker guards.
weaknesses.704:
- Needs significant improvement in ball handling, decision making, and shot creation, especially in pick-and-roll situations  
- Below-average pull-up shooter with inconsistent performance in above-the-break three-pointers and streaky spot-up shooting  
- Struggles to finish at the rim, particularly in traffic, with poor left-hand finishing and limited creativity when attacking the basket  
- Poor assist-to-turnover ratio; average passer with limited ability to create for teammates or facilitate the game  
- Defensive issues including tunnel vision, lack of low stance, occasional lapses in focus, and inability to guard bigger wings effectively
weaknesses.705:
- Morris has a slight 175-pound frame that limits his ability to compete with physical NBA backcourts.  
- He will likely give up athleticism at the next level, despite having strong basketball IQ.  
- His main challenge in the NBA will be improving his scoring or finding a viable niche.  
- Developing into a 40%+ three-point shooter would significantly enhance his effectiveness and efficiency.  
- As he turns 23 before his first NBA game, he must quickly define his role or risk losing roster spot opportunities.
weaknesses.706:
- Montrezl is undersized for a power forward at 6'8" with a solid standing reach but limited post game and poor free throw shooting (51%).  
- He lacks a developed offensive game beyond around-the-rim plays and has mechanical ball-handling and rough footwork.  
- His wingspan helps with defense and perimeter defense, but he is not a strong defensive rebounder (2.2 DRPG).  
- He has an emerging jumper but it is not a reliable or standout offensive asset; 3-point shooting remains a risky option.  
- Success at the NBA level will depend on embracing his strengths around the rim and improving his offensive versatility.
weaknesses.707:
- May struggle defensively in the pros due to lack of speed and athleticism, making it hard to defend the 4 or protect the rim effectively.  
- Likely to be neutralized into a stretch big role at the next level rather than a traditional interior presence.  
- Average rebounder for a 6'11" player, with limited impact on the boards.  
- Limited versatility beyond being a big who can shoot and score inside.  
- Struggles from the free-throw line despite being a 40% three-point shooter (below 70% FT%).
weaknesses.708:
- Lacks athleticism, explosion, and first-step ability, making it difficult to create his own shots or penetrate defenses consistently.  
- Struggles to create offensive looks off the dribble and may rely heavily on team offense and screen-based opportunities.  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from three-point range (ranging from 26.3% to 33.8%), despite good mechanics on pull-ups.  
- Limited offensive creativity and playmaking ability; averages only 1–1.8 assists per game, with minimal impact as a floor spacer or initiator.  
- Slight frame and lack of strength limit his rebounding and overall physical presence on the court.
weaknesses.709:
- Still a raw prospect unable to handle big minutes or compete physically with NBA centers  
- Lacks strength and fluidity, with limited post moves and poor body usage to score over longer centers  
- Inefficient shooter with sub-50% field goal percentage in college; struggles from the perimeter  
- Poor offensive initiation and turnovers when sets deviate from plan; lacks consistency in offensive sets  
- Limited shot-blocking production; defensive reliability remains a major concern as he must roam outside the paint
weaknesses.710:
- Lacks strength and frame, making it difficult to establish position or protect the ball in traffic  
- Unpolished offensive game with limited scoring tools; relies heavily on length and athleticism  
- Poor shooting efficiency, especially from mid-range and three-point range (low 3PT% and inconsistent results)  
- Struggles with free throw shooting (below 60%) and has a tendency to foul when contesting shots  
- Green prospect with room to grow in post play, shooting, and defensive versatility; requires patience and development
weaknesses.711:
- Lacks fluidity and mobility, with stiff legs and a noticeable limp likely due to hip issues related to growth.  
- Lower body strength is a significant concern, as he can be easily pushed off the block by more physical NCAA players.  
- Performance drops sharply against larger, stronger opponents: averages 6.4 points, 6 rebounds, and 2 blocks per game vs. major conference teams, compared to 14.75 points, 7.1 rebounds, and 3.1 blocks vs. smaller mid-major schools.  
- Has not excelled against players his own size or larger in terms of bulk, despite holding his own in key games.  
- Potential for improvement through continued weight training and flexibility development, especially as he matures.
weaknesses.712:
- Hyland’s small stature and low weight raise concerns about his suitability for point guard duties and ability to handle physicality around the basket.  
- Despite being a good defender, he struggles against bigger players due to a significant strength disadvantage.  
- His shooting percentage dropped 6% from freshman season, and he averaged more turnovers than assists, indicating poor decision-making.  
- He is best suited as an off-ball cutter for three-point looks but often waits for open opportunities and lacks consistent offensive creation.  
- Limited leaping ability and athleticism, combined with an easy schedule and low floor shooting efficiency, may lower his NBA projection.
weaknesses.714:
- Struggled with consistency and shot selection as a freshman, shooting only 28.6% from three-point range.  
- Lacks developed offensive instincts, limited ability to create offense off the dribble or as a shooter.  
- Relies heavily on physical advantages and struggles to break down defenses or create opportunities independently.  
- Scores primarily from three-pointers or strong finishes at the rim; lacks consistent mid-range scoring.  
- Needs improvement in ballhandling, passing, turnover control, and offensive versatility, particularly in pick-and-roll situations.
weaknesses.715:
- Below-average athleticism by NBA standards, with poor leaping ability  
- Concerns with foot speed, especially lateral movement  
- Questionable separation ability and lack of defensive strength  
- Needs to improve strength to finish plays without being knocked off balance  
- Inconsistent outside shooting with a flat release; must develop consistency and shot mechanics
weaknesses.716:
- Queta is a traditional center with limited three-point shooting and offensively limited outside threat.  
- His offensive strengths—size, post moves, and rebounding—are becoming less valuable due to the rise of smaller, more versatile players.  
- He lacks the speed and ability to switch defensively, limiting his effectiveness against guards and multiple positions.  
- Teams may rely on him in pick-and-roll situations, where his lack of guard coverage could create easy scoring opportunities.  
- Limited competition in the Mountain West Conference may benefit Queta, but he will need more finesse and post moves to succeed against similarly sized centers.
weaknesses.717:
- Lacks explosiveness in first steps and leaping ability  
- Struggles against smaller, quicker defenders who get into his legs  
- Thin build with insufficient muscle, leading to poor performance against contact  
- Ineffective finisher around the basket; relies on finesse and has low conversion rate  
- Unaggressive playing style, poor ball strength, and tendency to pass up good shots and throw passes in the air
weaknesses.719:
- Nedovic is a combo guard rather than a traditional point guard, lacking elite speed and quickness to effectively play the PG position at the NBA level.  
- He has strong athleticism, explosiveness, and body control but lacks consistent shot-making, especially with pull-up jumpers off the dribble.  
- His ball handling and playmaking skills need significant improvement to develop into a reliable point guard.  
- On defense, his average lateral speed makes it difficult to match up against NBA point guards.  
- With proper coaching and consistent work, especially on shooting and playmaking, Nedovic has potential to become a solid NBA player.
weaknesses.720:
- Offensive game is rudimentary: relies on putbacks, dunks, and teammates creating for him; lacks scoring versatility and post moves.  
- Poor shooting fundamentals: inconsistent form, lacks clean wrist release, struggles from close range, and shoots only 53% from the free throw line.  
- Weak post game: limited post instincts, poor footwork, and lacks effective post moves despite athleticism and quick feet.  
- Physical limitations: thin legs, narrow shoulders, and lack of strength raise concerns about durability and ability to handle the center position in the NBA.  
- Strong defensively and athletically: elite quickness and reflexes, solid defensive presence, and length, making him a top-level defensive player and a likely one-and-done prospect.
weaknesses.721:
- Has solid foot speed but lacks overall athleticism.  
- Appears well-suited for the college level as a point guard.  
- Uncertain if his game will translate to the NBA due to facing faster and stronger opponents.  
- Is very thin and needs to bulk up to better utilize his size.  
- Defensively struggles to stay in front of his opponent in one-on-one situations.
weaknesses.722:
- Johnson is 6’2”, which is short for a true shooting guard and limits his effectiveness guarding bigger wings.  
- He lacks standout offensive skills, with limited creativity as a ball-handler and a tendency to dribble into poor court positions.  
- His jump shot is decent but inconsistent, often settling too frequently and lacking reliability.  
- Despite being a high leaper, he struggled to finish around the rim in college due to insufficient height.  
- He will need a compatible team environment that accepts his size limitations and allows him to run the offense sporadically.
weaknesses.723:
- Lacks offensive skill and consistency, with robotic movements and difficulty adjusting when initial plays are disrupted.  
- Below-average feel for the game; struggles with positioning and decision-making on both offense and defense.  
- Limited offensive potential despite some improvement in shot accuracy; relies heavily on simple catch-and-shoot plays.  
- Rebounding is solid at 7.8 per game but lacks standout efficiency due to poor technique and lack of aggression.  
- Poor defensive awareness, struggles in one-on-one situations, double teams, and traffic; needs improvement in post movement, jump hook, and ball handling.
weaknesses.724:
- Lacks physicality and muscle, struggles defensively as a defender and offensively as a finisher (39.6% FG inside the arc)  
- Poor shot selection, frequently takes low-percentage mid-range shots instead of attacking the rim  
- Inconsistent 3-point shooting (33.8% 3FG%) and struggles in contested shots, often changing release with a hand in his face  
- Limited playmaking ability (1.7 apg), rarely initiates plays and only passes when forced  
- Below-average rebounding (1.6 rpg) and limited impact on the game beyond scoring, with poor basketball IQ and decision-making
weaknesses.725:
- Has wide shoulders but a slender frame with limited overall strength  
- Lacks physicality, explosive first step, and struggles to score in traffic  
- Struggles with playing through contact and getting to the free throw line frequently  
- Is not an elite athlete and averages 2.9 turnovers per game; plays upright and rigid with limited fluidity  
- Needs to improve as a vocal leader, midrange game, and offensive aggression while increasing strength and finishing ability
weaknesses.726:
- Mannion shows good basketball IQ and playmaking ability but struggles with shot selection, often taking difficult shots or playing hero ball instead of making smart passes.  
- His shooting is inconsistent from distance, with poor efficiency and a low three-point percentage (32% in freshman season), and he lacks consistency as a set shooter.  
- He favors low-percentage floaters and runners, struggles to finish at the rim, especially against taller defenders, and has limited effectiveness in finishing.  
- Physically, he lacks elite athleticism, speed, and size (6'2.5" wingspan, 8'1.5" reach), limiting his ability to defend or create in high-pressure situations.  
- He is not a plus defender at the NBA level and may struggle to defend most shooting guards, with limited explosiveness and a need for strength development.
weaknesses.727:
- Has a thin frame and needs to gain weight to avoid being bullied in the paint and improve physical presence.  
- Added strength will enhance his ability to fight in the post, secure position, and rebound more effectively.  
- Lacks a clear offensive identity; not a consistent scorer or skilled offensively.  
- Struggled as a three-point shooter, hitting only 28% of his threes, which limits potential as a stretch big.  
- Inconsistent jump shooting and occasionally passive offensive play; should focus on attacking more frequently.
weaknesses.728:
- Lacks run/jump athleticism, with below-average foot speed and overall quickness.  
- Poor lateral speed makes it difficult to contain NBA point guards.  
- Struggles to gain separation and finish on the break due to lack of explosiveness.  
- Relies on body shielding rather than athleticism to create offensive opportunities.  
- Limited upside at age 23, compounded by lack of quality competition in the WCC.
weaknesses.729:
- Lacks foot speed and overall quickness, limiting his ability to create shot opportunities or get by athletic defenders.  
- Struggles against bigger, stronger, and quicker opponents, particularly top-tier guards.  
- Ineffective on defensive end due to poor lateral speed and lack of physicality.  
- Easily pushed under the basket on drives and has been shut down in key games (e.g., 0-2 FG vs. Duke).  
- Limited run and jump athleticism, with questions about potential strength and conditioning gains.
weaknesses.730:
- Quick but not explosive; lacks elite speed for the next level  
- Poor decision-making, often rushes plays and commits bad turnovers  
- Struggles with assist/turnover ratio and has difficulty adjusting when playing with a ball-dominant guard  
- Inconsistent pull-up shooting; cannot create shots from standstill against elite defenses  
- Lateral quickness is average, defense lacks focus, and closeout defense is overly aggressive with pump fakes
weaknesses.731:
- Lacks speed and leaping ability, making it difficult to stay ahead of NBA athletes at the center position.  
- Struggles as a post player and needs to improve strength and develop back-to-the-basket moves.  
- Defense is weak due to poor lateral speed and lack of strength.  
- Length is a positive asset, but explosiveness and foot speed remain significant limitations.  
- Despite being young, his upside is constrained by lack of quickness and athleticism.
weaknesses.732:
- Thin frame and high center of gravity limit strength and defensive effectiveness  
- Lacks elite first step, burst, and lateral quickness, making it difficult to drive to the basket  
- Inconsistent shooting mechanics, especially under pressure, with a tendency to change release  
- Struggles with ball handling, particularly with his left hand, and has poor finishing, especially with his left hand  
- Defensive issues including slow pace, poor positioning, and lack of maturity, leading to lapses in focus and negative body language
weaknesses.733:
- Lanky and skinny build requires weight room work to meet NBA physical standards.  
- Lacks explosiveness and aggressiveness, differing from typical Balkan players.  
- Struggles with post-up game and lacks a solid back-to-the-basket ability.  
- Poor timing and positioning in pick-and-roll situations; ineffective screens for his size.  
- Below-average free throw shooting (less than 60%) due to slow release despite good shot mechanics.
weaknesses.734:
- Has average agility and leaping ability, with only moderate speed and floor running efficiency.  
- Lacks motivation and concentration at times, which negatively impacts offensive effort and activity.  
- Athleticism is rated as just okay for NBA standards; lacks strength and body toughness, especially for post play.  
- Primarily a perimeter scorer with limited post moves; most scoring comes from outside shots or fast breaks.  
- Effective in half-court offense and set shooting, but struggles in one-on-one fastbreak situations and against powerful opponents.
weaknesses.736:
- Has a good but not great first step, making it difficult to create shots against quicker, more athletic NBA opponents.  
- Needs to bulk up to match the physicality and pace of the NBA, especially under contact.  
- Inconsistent mid-range shooting and lacks a reliable floater; not a threat in spot-up 3-pointers.  
- Most effective with the ball in his hands; struggles off the ball and against drop coverages in pick-and-rolls.  
- Defensive challenges include average lateral quickness, difficulty against smaller/quick guards, and problems with off-ball defense and post coverage.
weaknesses.737:
- Lacks athleticism, with poor foot speed and limited explosiveness  
- Struggles with floor movement, shot timing, and defensive positioning, especially against more athletic opponents  
- Plays primarily below the rim and is not a strong shot-blocker or defensive contributor  
- Fails to keep up in pick-and-roll defense and often shows up late on defensive hedges  
- Has adequate ball-handling for his size but cannot exploit it due to lack of foot speed
weaknesses.738:
- Still developing as a consistent spot-up shooter, more comfortable creating off the dribble than shooting off the catch.  
- Needs to improve rhythm and footwork on catch-and-shoot opportunities to become a reliable floor spacer.  
- Can be rushed, often driving into traffic without a clear plan, leading to forced shots or tough angles.  
- Makes questionable decisions under pressure, resulting in avoidable turnovers.  
- Needs to tighten his handle and improve counters to consistently create separation against elite defenders.
weaknesses.739:
- Still developing as a scorer in half-court with more effective finishing than shot creation  
- Inconsistent jumper, especially from long range, with slow shot preparation and stiff mechanics  
- Free throw percentage in the low 60s, limiting reliability as a perimeter shooter  
- Post offense is underdeveloped; lacks consistent three-point shooting (currently ~25%)  
- Limited playmaking vision, poor defensive awareness, and tendency to be overaggressive
weaknesses.740:
- Needs to improve base strength, toughness, and frame to handle contact at higher competition levels.  
- Overreliant on length defensively; vulnerable to slashers targeting his chest/body, leading to fouls (2.5 FPG).  
- Limited lateral mobility due to high center of gravity; requires better hip sinking and foot sliding.  
- Struggles with quickness in space and physicality against NBA centers, making him a "tweener" with mixed advantages.  
- Limited offensive production—minimal ball handling, post scoring, and passing (0.8 APG); efficient shooting still developing (28.3% 3FG, 65% FT).
weaknesses.741:
- Penda’s perimeter shooting is his biggest skill, but he struggled this season with a 29.2% three-pointers on modest volume.  
- His release is slow and low, making shots vulnerable to contests at the NBA level.  
- Lacks fluidity in shooting mechanics and has limited off-the-dribble scoring ability.  
- Post footwork is rudimentary and can become sloppy under pressure; doesn't consistently create his own shot.  
- Defensively, he struggles against smaller, quicker guards and has lagging footwork in isolation coverage.
weaknesses.742:
- Lacks confidence and often thinks instead of reacting instinctively, affecting decision-making on the court.  
- Relies on length rather than athleticism; added weight may slow him down, especially as he grows into the 4-spot.  
- Needs improvement in low post footwork and court awareness, both offensively and defensively.  
- Frequently gets into foul trouble and struggles with defensive footwork, indicating a need for better defensive fundamentals.  
- Has strong offensive tools and range but lacks a killer mentality and consistent midrange/distance shooting, limiting his scoring potential.
weaknesses.743:
- Lacks explosiveness and athleticism, making it difficult to create separation from defenders and limiting offensive upside.  
- Struggles in one-on-one situations and may have difficulty guarding NBA-level point guards defensively.  
- Needs to improve his outside shooting and offensive threat level despite a solid 39% three-point percentage last season.  
- Physically frail with limited size, restricting his ability to play as a full-time 2-guard in the NBA.  
- Risk of being overlooked or "lost in the shuffle" at Duke due to his conservative playing style and reliance on teammates.
weaknesses.744:
- Inconsistent shot-making, with only 27% three-point shooting and a 50% true shooting percentage  
- Poor jump shot mechanics, especially under pressure; often rushes the release and fails to square his base  
- Lacks rim strength and touch, struggling to finish through traffic and absorbing contact at 185 lbs  
- Not yet a reliable primary scorer; prefers facilitating, making him predictable when passing lanes are taken away  
- Struggles with decision-making, poor isolation and late-clock scoring, and defensive lapses due to overaggression and poor positioning
weaknesses.745:
- Size concerns limit versatility and effectiveness against taller opponents  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from beyond the arc and with hands in face  
- Struggles with shooting mechanics, range, and release consistency; low catch-and-shoot percentage  
- Limited passing ability and court vision, especially when moving; often looks for his own shot  
- Lacks dribbling moves, speed changes, and defensive adaptability; high turnover rate and poor reaction to pressure
weaknesses.746:
- Solid all-around point guard with no standout strengths in any specific area  
- Weighed less than 175 lbs at the NBA Draft Combine but has good wiry strength  
- Inconsistent shooting and relies heavily on ball-handling; plays better with the ball  
- Depended on to score frequently as a mid-major prospect, lacking proven point guard versatility  
- Could be a second-round backup point guard in the NBA with improved efficiency and team chemistry
weaknesses.747:
- Lacks foot quickness, making it difficult to defend against quicker perimeter players, especially at the 4-position.  
- Has a stiff upper body and shoulders, with potential for improvement in flexibility and core strength.  
- Average rebounder (7.6 rpg this season, 5.8 rpg as a freshman) due to size advantage but limited by lack of rebounding fundamentals.  
- Limited shot-blocking ability and minimal threat in passing lanes, resulting in an unspectacular ceiling on defense.  
- Relies on right-handed jump hooks as a primary move, lacks offensive versatility, and struggles with shot creation; also needs improvement in free-throw shooting (70% this season).
weaknesses.748:
- Limited creativity and ball-handling, with mixed results against set defenses when cut off.  
- Strong on-ball defense but fundamentally upright and undisciplined, making him vulnerable to back cuts and screens.  
- Underwhelming defensive playmaking stats (low spg and bpg), despite physical tools suggesting greater potential.  
- Minimal playmaking ability (1.6/1.6 A/TO), with poor feel for the game and basketball IQ below NBA standards.  
- Mediocre shooting efficiency and low free-throw usage (2.4 FT per game, 70% career) raising questions about consistency and touch.
weaknesses.749:
- Lacks a defined offensive role and struggles with creating shots and ball handling  
- Poor shot mechanics, including a slow release, telegraphed shots, minimal arc, and lack of rotation  
- Inconsistent intensity and effort, with uneven performance and limited offensive awareness  
- Raw post skills and unproven rebounding, averaging only 2.6 and 5.4 rebounds per game  
- Recovering from a torn ACL, which may impact draft stock and overall readiness
weaknesses.750:
- Jaramaz needs to improve his outside shooting, currently at 28.4% from three-point range.  
- He must develop better decision-making in pick-and-roll situations.  
- His jump shot has a low release point and requires a higher release with improved shooting form.  
- Defensively, he tends to apply excessive pressure at times.  
- His age, being draft-eligible and older than other point guard options, is a disadvantage.
weaknesses.752:
- Struggles defensively as a perimeter player, lacking lateral quickness, intensity, and ability to fight through screens  
- Poor shot selection, especially from mid-to-long range, with a streaky and often contested shooting tendency  
- Over-dribbles and makes questionable offensive decisions, showing a tendency to do too much on offense  
- Limited defensive impact despite size, with poor recovery from screens and low steal rate for his speed  
- Needs to improve as a facilitator, with an assist-to-turnover ratio just over 1 and insufficient playmaking for a true point guard
weaknesses.753:
- Low blocks and steals in college, indicating limited defensive impact beyond basic stats  
- Limited reliability from three-point range; 3-point shooting is a recent development  
- Accurate from mid-range but needs improvement in shot mechanics and release timing  
- Poor passing and playmaking ability with a high turnover rate  
- Underwhelming rebounding for a physically athletic post player; slow and methodical movement
weaknesses.754:
- Relatively old for a one-year college player (21 years), lacking polished footwork in the post.  
- Tends to settle for three-point shots and struggles to create offensive opportunities with purpose or crisp moves.  
- Poor free throw mechanics and inconsistent execution lead to a 70% shooting percentage from the line.  
- Prone to silly mistakes on defense, which can result in foul trouble and disrupt team dynamics.  
- Limited ability to create shots in half court and struggles against more athletic, larger opponents.
weaknesses.755:
- Has a problematic character with a history of defiance, clashes, and disagreements, including getting his youth coach fired  
- Failed to lead the most talented Israeli youth team to a national title and never achieved notable results with the junior national team  
- Exhibits arrogance and ego, often avoids physicality and "dirty work" despite being tall and highly coordinated at his size  
- Relies too heavily on outside shooting and penetration, lacking aggression and foot speed; struggles with board battles  
- Needs significant senior-level game time and real playing minutes, unlike the limited opportunities he received at Maccabi
weaknesses.756:
- Reliant on physical gifts rather than skill, leading to inconsistent offensive performance  
- Frequently misses first shots and depends on second-chance opportunities  
- Struggles with fundamentals, often releasing hook shots too low  
- Lacks developed outside shooting, limiting effectiveness as a stretch four  
- Poor ball-handling and lack of offensive creativity restrict his ability to create scoring chances
weaknesses.758:
- Undersized for a center with limited wingspan and standing reach  
- Poor shooting ability, especially from outside the paint; lacks three-point shooting  
- Relies heavily on post play and within-the-paint scoring; limited mid-range shot accuracy  
- Struggles with post moves and free throw conversion; only 1.5 made free throws career, 2.5 per game as a senior  
- Lacks strength and physicality, making it difficult to compete against NBA-level bigs in the paint
weaknesses.759:
- Lacks explosive power and lateral quickness, relying on makeup speed and length to compensate.  
- Inconsistent jump shooting with poor mechanics, low 3-point percentage (below 20% as a freshman), and sporadic shot selection.  
- Struggles to create his own shots, lacks dribble creativity, and is a poor ball-handler on the perimeter.  
- Has a high center of gravity, limiting lateral movement and making him vulnerable to quicker wings off the bounce.  
- Strong defender in open space but needs significant development in strength, shooting, ball-handling, and shot creation to reach his potential.
weaknesses.760:
- Inconsistent performance with streaky shooting and frequent misses, especially from the perimeter  
- Struggles with finishing through contact and relies heavily on floaters due to fear of contact  
- Poor ball handling, particularly with his left hand, and lacks elite athletic ability  
- Decision-making issues, including poor assist-to-turnover ratio and overreliance on pump fakes  
- Ineffective against physical defenders; lacks toughness and advanced post-up skills at the senior level
weaknesses.761:
- Has OK wingspan (reported 6-9, possibly 6-11) and a solid but not elite first step  
- Foot speed is decent but not exceptional; performance is inconsistent game-to-game  
- Needs to improve physicality on both ends of the floor and consistency in shooting  
- Ball handling and decision-making require development, especially in perimeter and pick-and-roll situations  
- Lacks strength and defensive focus, particularly against physical defenders and power forwards, with mental lapses and poor help-defense execution
weaknesses.762:
- Lacks lateral quickness, making it difficult to defend quick perimeter players at the next level.  
- Provides limited rim protection, averaging less than one block per game.  
- Struggles with turnovers, having four or more in 11 of 39 Duke games; improved slightly in EYBL with 3.2 per game.  
- Free throw shooting is below ideal, with a career mark of 72.9%; consistency needs improvement.  
- Outside shooting is streaky and has shown low percentages in key tournaments (25% and 20% 3PT in EYBL and NBPA Top 100, respectively), though improved as a shooter in high school.
weaknesses.763:
- Limited experience at a high level  
- Late bloomer in development  
- Not widely recognized coming into the draft  
- Limited playing time (14 minutes per game) and low shot volume (3 shots per game)  
- Poor assist-to-turnover ratio, indicating need for more assertive ball-handling and shooting
weaknesses.764:
- Playing in a mid-major may raise doubts about the legitimacy of his stats due to lower competition levels.  
- Turned 22 in January, so his long-term upside is somewhat uncertain despite a solid skill set and game feel.  
- His offensive game is unorthodox, with predictable post moves (often going over his left shoulder) and a need to develop go-to moves and improve footwork.  
- Requires additional upper body strength, though he plays through contact well at 226.6 lbs; also needs to adjust shooting form (right elbow out, leaning into shots).  
- Not an elite leaper or a strong rim protector or shot blocker (2.2 blocks per game), but could improve with better technique and aggression in attacking the rim.
weaknesses.765:
- Lacks lateral quickness to effectively defend shooting guards.  
- Does not have the size to play small forward.  
- Not a threatening scorer in isolation situations.  
- Limited ballhandling ability.  
- Limited college production raises concerns about impact in the professional league.
weaknesses.766:
- Struggled with shot selection and efficiency, shooting only 34% from the field and failing to convert on many contested shots despite being a high-reputation recruit.  
- Lacked explosiveness and athleticism, with one of the lowest vertical jump measurements in Combine history (26"), and struggled against dribble penetration and stretch 4s.  
- Poor free-throw performance across key events (0.7 per game at NBPA Top 100, 1 per game in 2019), indicating inconsistency in clutch situations.  
- Limited offensive versatility, relying heavily on perimeter shooting and lacking the ability to create or finish at the rim effectively.  
- Durability issues, including a nagging ankle injury and poor effort on the court, contributed to his declining performance and reduced availability throughout the season.
weaknesses.767:
- Lacks natural playmaking ability compared to taller players (6'1+).  
- Struggles to read defensive rotations in pick-and-roll situations.  
- Has difficulty finishing over bigger help defenders.  
- Light frame limits his ability to attack stronger guards and defend effectively.  
- Shooting efficiency from the free-throw line declined in his last two seasons (64% and 70%).
weaknesses.768:
- McCaw is one of the lighter players in the draft at 180 lbs, struggling with balance and physicality, especially off screens.  
- Needs to gain weight and strength to improve his mid-range game and finishing around the basket with contact.  
- Lacks a natural position and is not a strong post defender, limiting his effectiveness in that role.  
- Can be careless with the ball and undisciplined, often gambling for steals and lacking consistency in scoring.  
- Should become more assertive and vocal to improve leadership, despite preferring to lead by example.
weaknesses.769:
- Lacks size and strength, limiting his ability to play as a true power forward  
- More effective at scoring than running the team effectively  
- Relies heavily on outside shots and has inconsistent shooting (3PT%:.338)  
- Takes too many contested and wild shots, indicating poor shot selection  
- Struggles with decision-making and avoids contact under the basket, limiting finish under pressure
weaknesses.770:
- Lacks a consistent outside shooting ability, with a low release point and poor consistency beyond 15 feet  
- Struggles with defensive rebounding against taller opponents and has limited defensive awareness  
- Ineffective against athletic perimeter players due to footwork and positioning issues  
- Needs to improve court awareness, patience, and help defense, especially with multiple defenders on him  
- Undersized at power forward in the NBA; must become more versatile offensively to succeed at the next level
weaknesses.771:
- Questionable desire and instinct to be a high-level scorer; may thrive as a standout role player rather than a primary scorer.  
- Limited statistical output, averaging only 9.2 points as a freshman with poor three-point shooting (32%) and inconsistent playing time.  
- Needs to improve aggression, footwork, post moves, and back-to-the-basket skills to maximize his talent and physicality.  
- Struggles with shot mechanics, especially from deep; has a slow release and could benefit from a more streamlined form.  
- Lacks ball-handling ability, playmaking, and rebounding consistency; shows untapped potential but requires development in leadership and urgency.
weaknesses.772:
- Struggles with creating his own shot, relying heavily on spot-up shooting, low-post positioning, and transition plays.  
- Lacks a strong dribble and handle, limiting his ability to generate scoring opportunities independently.  
- Tends to rush long three-point shots and lacks patience, often settling for the long ball instead of waiting for better opportunities.  
- Shows signs of sloppiness with the ball and occasional lack of focus, particularly in decision-making and court awareness.  
- Has defensive lapses when playing on the ball, though these can be improved with proper coaching and higher-level competition.
weaknesses.774:
- Plays primarily at power forward with limited playing time and potential as a "tweener" at the next level  
- Slow shot release, with inconsistent release when opponents close out  
- Average ball handling for a wing; high dribble makes it easy for opponents to strip the ball  
- Ineffective in pick-and-roll situations and struggles to create scoring opportunities independently  
- Poor lateral quickness, defensive aggression leading to fouls, and inconsistency in shooting performance
weaknesses.775:
- Has average passing skills, poor two-footed leap, and limited athleticism and explosiveness.  
- Defensively struggles to keep opponents away from the rim, with average shot-blocking and limited intimidation.  
- Poor outside shooting (only effective at 4m with a low, easily blocked release).  
- Prone to turnovers (2.5 per game) and mental errors, such as poor positioning and turning his back to the ball during critical moments.  
- Needs significant improvement in conditioning, body balance, and defensive footwork, though shows potential as a shot-blocker at a higher level.
weaknesses.776:
- Struggles to draw fouls and get to the free-throw line despite a high free-throw percentage.  
- Limited foot speed affects defensive performance at the next level.  
- Inconsistent and at times reckless shot selection, though it may improve with reduced usage.  
- Ceiling in early career is a quality minute role player.  
- Physical profile (6’2”, 195 lbs) and speed could make transition to the next level challenging, especially against faster point guards.
weaknesses.777:
- Below average in length and height for his position  
- Lack of foot speed significantly impacts defensive performance, especially against faster opponents  
- At 23, he is a super senior with limited room for growth and must produce immediately  
- Despite being a strong shooter and scoring instinct, averaged below 10 ppg over his career, with only one season above 12 ppg  
- Free throw percentage dropped to 75% as a senior, which is unexpectedly low given his overall shooting efficiency
weaknesses.778:
- Inconsistent performance with no reliable go-to skill; struggles to create easy shots and often disappears during games.  
- Poor defensive awareness and effort, evidenced by low blocks and inability to defend effectively despite physical tools.  
- Lacks strength, toughness, and willingness to battle, making him easily pushed off balance and vulnerable under pressure.  
- Inconsistent shooting, especially from three-point range, with a promising but unreliable jumpshot and poor finishing under contact.  
- Underdeveloped basketball IQ, poor passing awareness, and tendency to coast rather than play aggressively or make smart decisions.
weaknesses.779:
- Has raw skill and energy but lacks refined technique and feel for the game  
- Thin frame limits physicality, leading to balance issues in high-contact situations  
- Relies heavily on crashing the basket with minimal post moves or versatility  
- Needs to improve fundamentals, positioning, decision-making, and controlled game phases  
- Shot consistency, especially from the free throw line (below 70%), requires significant development
weaknesses.780:
- Low shooting percentages over his last two college seasons  
- Struggles from the three-point range with significant room for improvement  
- Improved decision-making but still struggles with turnovers  
- Inefficient in getting to the free-throw line despite being an efficient shooter  
- Inconsistent performance, lack of scoring improvement despite speed and athleticism  
- Vulnerable to larger point guards when posted up  
- Emotionally reactive, prone to self-doubt when performance falters
weaknesses.781:
- Lacks readiness for NBA-level competition and requires significant development time.  
- Needs to add strength to compete with NBA forwards, particularly to fight contact and attack the rim.  
- Poor offensive efficiency with low shooting percentages (32.2% FG, 22.6% 3P, 68.8% FT) and struggles in spot-up shooting.  
- Struggles with rhythm, composure, and transition play despite having run/jump athleticism.  
- Limited playing time at UCLA reduced exposure and development, leading to a small sample size and potential draft night cost.
weaknesses.784:
- Lacks size at 5’10", making him a defensive liability and requiring twice as much effort to get open.  
- Small size makes him a target for post-ups from bigger point guards.  
- Shows less defensive tenacity and passion compared to players like Nate Robinson.  
- Shooting efficiency declined from junior to senior year (FG: 46.0% to 42.7%; 3P: 40.8% to 35.9%).  
- More effective in half-court offense than transition; over-penetrates and needs to improve his tear drop.
weaknesses.785:
- Poor defender due to lack of effort, awareness, speed, and poor recovery; often in the wrong position and steps away from play.  
- Vulnerable in one-on-one situations, frequently outmatched by quicker opponents on cuts and drives to the basket.  
- Inconsistent hustle and undisciplined play, with poor offensive contribution and an average assist rate (0.8 per game in D-League).  
- Past off-court issues, including speeding, driving without a license, marijuana possession, and a gun found in his car, leading to NCAA investigation and dismissal from UNC.  
- Outlook: Could develop as a bench scorer and 3-point specialist, but limited by defensive deficiencies and average athleticism; likely a borderline first-round pick falling to the second round.
weaknesses.786:
- Washington is undersized and lacks elite athleticism for the modern power forward position, limiting his effectiveness in NBA-level play.  
- He lacks a consistent perimeter game, struggles with mid-range and three-point shooting, and is not a reliable shooter from beyond the arc.  
- His post game is limited, with little ability to score in the low post beyond a jump hook and poor technique when operating in traffic.  
- He faces challenges defensively, including difficulty reading double teams, frequent fouls, poor free-throw shooting, and vulnerability to shot-blocking due to lack of height and strength.  
- Needs significant improvement in ball handling under pressure, conditioning, strength, and defensive positioning to succeed at the NBA level.
weaknesses.787:
- Struggles with ball handling and dribble creativity, lacking techniques when his path is cut off and limited in creating offense off the dribble.  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from three-point range (averaging only 1 attempt per game, with poor efficiency in key tournaments), and a need for more consistent jump shot development.  
- Poor free throw performance (55% as a freshman), often leaning forward and appearing off-balanced, indicating room for improvement in shot execution.  
- Mediocre to below-average playmaking ability (1:2.7 assist-to-turnover ratio), with tendency to be overly aggressive or loose in decision-making and passing.  
- Older for his class (20 years old as a freshman), which may limit his development window and reduce opportunities for early NBA-level playing time.
weaknesses.788:
- Struggles with creating his own offense, lacking quickness, ball-handling, and separation, especially when going left.  
- Inconsistent scoring performance; shows poor efficiency in some games (e.g., 8.3% shooting) and explosive bursts in others (e.g., 69.6% shooting).  
- Limited finishing ability near the rim, shooting only 41% inside the arc as a junior and 46% for his career.  
- Relies heavily on outside shots and contested three-pointers, with poor free-throw shooting (40% at adidas Nations, 66.7% in Gauntlet).  
- Has strong offensive stats in major showcases (e.g., 29.5 PPG, 8.6 RPG, 4.9 APG as a senior) but lacks a standout skill or elite ability that separates him at the NBA level.
weaknesses.789:
- Lacks ideal size and bulk for a power forward at the next level  
- No perimeter skills to justify a potential shift to small forward  
- Underwhelming rebounder, especially defensively; limited potential at the next level  
- Has a slow, awkward jump shot release  
- Overaggressive on both ends of the court, leading to frequent foul trouble and lack of reliable scoring moves
weaknesses.790:
- Lost speed and explosiveness after tearing his ACL in high school, impacting his ability to create open looks.  
- Struggles on breaks and in open floor situations, limiting his scoring opportunities.  
- Low jump shot release results in poor elevation, making three-point shots easy to defend.  
- Inconsistent shooting, including a 2-for-18 performance in last 12 games (34% overall), raising doubts about his shooting reliability.  
- Lacks physicality, struggles on the glass, and shows defensive lapses, especially against faster, stronger opponents.
weaknesses.791:
- Limited perimeter skills; more comfortable in the mid-post area where he can attack from the triple threat  
- Poor ball handling with no reliable secondary move to maintain drives when cut off  
- Offensively limited in lateral movement and change of direction  
- Inconsistent and selective shooter from beyond the college three-point line; needs to extend range and improve consistency for NBA level  
- Lacks lateral quickness as a one-on-one defender despite being an impressive athlete
weaknesses.793:
- 24 years old, limiting his potential due to age  
- Limited leaping ability, with a max vertical of 27 inches  
- Struggles to create offense in the paint due to lack of verticality  
- Inadequate explosiveness and quickness affect floor movement and defense  
- Developing post game, but still primarily effective as a face-up player
weaknesses.794:
- Lacks elite explosiveness and leaping ability  
- Needs improvement in upper body strength to compete at the NBA level  
- Inconsistent shooter, with less than 30% success from three-point range  
- Struggles to create and execute jumpers in isolation situations  
- Limited defensive lateral speed, making it difficult to guard quicker opponents
weaknesses.795:
- Has a solid 6’10.75 wingspan but lacks elite length for a big man.  
- Conditioning needs significant improvement.  
- Struggles with his jump shot, shooting only 27% from behind the arc despite a 51% field goal percentage.  
- Limited ability to dominate weaker defenders in the paint at the next level compared to college performance.  
- Free throw percentage improved from nearly 70% to 76% over two seasons, indicating shooting touch, but needs better shot selection and body control, especially when moving.
weaknesses.796:
- Undersized for center; better suited as a power forward; struggles to guard taller, stronger post players.  
- Inconsistent offensive game; lacks a go-to move and reliable secondary moves; needs to improve shot selection and decision-making.  
- Poor outside shooting; flat release, limited range, and inconsistent mid-range jumper; needs better shot form and range.  
- Weak ball-handling; tends to bring the ball down after rebounds; lacks effective defensive footwork and sealing.  
- Defensive limitations, especially in man-to-man post defense; prone to fouls from head/shot fakes; questions about motor and energy.
weaknesses.797:
- Forced shot selection, often settling for off-balanced or contested shots instead of making extra passes  
- Limited post-up ability despite size advantage and poor use of offensive positioning  
- Left hand needs significant improvement; noticeable decline when switching to off-hand  
- Weak on-ball defense with poor length and a low steal rate  
- Struggles with finishing near the basket, poor free-throw shooting (69.4%), and inconsistent decision-making and passing
weaknesses.798:
- Struggles with creating his own shot and lacks a go-to move in the post or off the dribble.  
- Off-ball awareness is inconsistent, often relying on guards to create for him.  
- Assist-to-turnover ratio is poor (1.3 to 1.5), indicating issues with decision-making under pressure.  
- Needs improvement in defensive discipline, including avoiding reach-ins and late contests.  
- Limited lateral quickness and defensive footwork raise concerns about containing perimeter players in the NBA.
weaknesses.799:
- Lacks optimal height and leaping ability for athletic performance  
- Inconsistent perimeter shooting, especially from the midrange using pull-up jumpers  
- Needs improvement in 3-point shooting in catch-and-shoot situations  
- Poor playmaking and decision-making, often over-dribbling and seeking highlight plays  
- Defensive intensity is offset by lack of maturity, leading to early foul trouble against experienced opponents
weaknesses.800:
- Needs to improve shooting ability, particularly from beyond the 3-point line (only 32% last season) and on all jump shots.  
- Has an awkward shooting motion and slow release, limiting efficiency and consistency.  
- Lacks great length and physical tools, which may hinder performance at the NBA level.  
- Tends to take inefficient pull-up jumpers early in the shot clock and struggles as a spot-up shooter.  
- Shot only 72% from the free throw line and must develop greater free throw consistency.
weaknesses.801:
- Lacks strength and shooting consistency, particularly from the field and at the rim under contact  
- Struggles with ball handling, decision-making, and creating his own shot due to thin frame and poor footwork  
- Inconsistent pull-up game, high release point, and poor 3-point shooting despite good mechanics  
- Ineffective against physical defense; lacks a post game and cannot exploit mismatches  
- Poor defensive awareness, prone to overaggression, poor foul draw, and questionable shot selection
weaknesses.802:
- Limited offensive production, with only 26% three-point shooting and 64% free throw accuracy this season  
- Post-game ability remains restricted and inconsistent  
- Scouts cite lack of consistency and defensive discipline as major concerns  
- Only one season as a starter and no NCAA Tournament experience with his team  
- Needs to gain strength and bulk to compete against NBA-level big men
weaknesses.803:
- Lacks ideal size and physique for the next level  
- Often out of position or takes high-risk plays that lead to turnovers  
- Functions best as a glue guy/connector rather than a top offensive option  
- Struggles to create scoring opportunities and lacks move-making ability  
- Limited versatility and best suited for a specific, defined role
weaknesses.804:
- Lacks strong 1-on-1 skills and ball handling, limiting his ability to drive and create shots.  
- Relies heavily on facilitators to set up plays; has virtually no shot-creation ability.  
- Underperforms offensively with poor free throw attempts (2.1 per game) and lack of aggressive rim-running.  
- Needs to develop a better floater/tear drop game and improve overall offensive versatility.  
- Shows potential defensively for his size and athleticism but lacks intensity, competitiveness, and physicality on defense.
weaknesses.805:
- Jump shot is a major weakness, described as slow and methodical due to shooting from the hip.  
- Shot only 27% from downtown last season, dropping to 26% this year.  
- Acts as a tweener, lacking a point guard's vision and court awareness and the size of a typical shooting guard.  
- May struggle to execute the shot effectively against NBA-level competition.  
- Can be careless with the ball off the dribble.
weaknesses.806:
- Struggles with consistency and decision-making, often making head scratching plays and forcing shots when faced with defensive pressure.  
- Prone to emotional frustration, especially when teammates make mistakes, and can lose composure under pressure.  
- Slow to initiate offense due to thick legs, which limits his defensive mobility and ability to rotate effectively.  
- Lacks creativity in offensive play, with limited improvisation, pump fakes, and footwork; relies too heavily on force rather than finesse.  
- Inconsistent shooting, particularly from three-point range and free throws, with poor shot selection and limited offensive technique in close situations.
weaknesses.807:
- Struggles with playing off the ball due to lack of experience and upper body strength.  
- Inconsistent shooting mechanics and poor shot selection.  
- Academic issues in high school led to sitting out his freshman season at Providence.  
- Limited experience against top-tier college competition; unknown performance against higher levels.  
- Questions about effort, energy, and ability to play a role in a team setting or adapt to NBA game speed.
weaknesses.808:
- Jump shot remains inconsistent and is still a work in progress.  
- Likely position change from center to another role due to college team playing zone defense.  
- Strong hand dominant; needs to develop right-hand skills to avoid exploitation.  
- Limited lateral quickness, making it difficult to keep up with faster players.  
- Rarely created his own shot; ball skills and passing need improvement.
weaknesses.809:
- Lacks explosive ability and reliable leaping, forcing him to shoot many attempts in the paint  
- Heavily relies on the pick-and-roll and struggles to create opportunities in isolation  
- Passes up open shots due to a "pass-first" mentality, leading to poor assist-to-turnover ratio  
- Outside shooting is inconsistent, with a slow footset and low release point that limits shot quality  
- Injury history, durability concerns, and potential overseas stay raise long-term uncertainty in the NBA
weaknesses.810:
- Has strong athleticism with good speed in transition and explosive ability in space, but lacks consistent explosiveness in half-court play.  
- Struggles with ball handling, particularly with his right hand; dribbling is high and loose, leading to poor control and sloppy passes.  
- Inconsistent shooter, especially from three-point range (31.2% over Nike EYBL), with a flat shot, low release, and hesitation when defenders give him space.  
- Poor free throw shooting (58.2%) and limited shot selection; tends to rely on natural ability and exhibit tunnel vision, often ignoring teammates.  
- Defensive effort is solid with good rotation, but lacks aggression, uses hands excessively, and struggles with contact due to insufficient strength.
weaknesses.811:
- Struggles with defense, particularly against dribble penetration and screen action, often falling asleep off-ball and allowing easy access to the rim  
- Lacks physical strength and frame, needing significant improvement to defend bigger perimeter players and compete in the paint  
- Poor footwork affects efficiency both as a shooter and defender; decision-making and ball-handling instincts are middling  
- Inconsistent shot selection, often settling for deep, contested shots; lacks midrange pull-ups and a floater; struggles in traffic  
- High turnover rate, telegraphs passes, and shows emotional volatility by arguing calls and gambling for steals without discipline
weaknesses.812:
- Struggles to get open looks due to lack of strength, explosiveness, and body control.  
- Requires a top-tier point guard to set up plays; ineffective at creating offense off the dribble.  
- Limited development against physical, high-level competition despite playing in the Sun Belt.  
- Relies heavily on offensive floaters and can get caught ball-watching on both offense and defense.  
- Confidence leads to inconsistent shooting; needs to improve shot selection and efficiency.
weaknesses.813:
- Has a slight frame and lacks strength/physicality, limiting defensive potential, especially against bigger, more physical defenders.  
- Relies heavily on scoring, often taking tough, contested shots and settling for deep shots; needs to improve shot selection and efficiency.  
- Tends to dribble into traffic and lose the ball to players with active hands; leads team in turnovers per game and per minute among rotation players.  
- Shows ability to create space and score from jumpers, but must develop this skill to consistently keep defenders off balance at the next level.  
- Favors right-hand finishes, has inconsistent 3-point shooting (ranging from 20% to 31.3%), and lacks the tools of a true point guard despite good assist numbers.
weaknesses.814:
- Hummel lacks quickness and athleticism, limiting his performance on both ends of the floor.  
- He struggles to get around defenders and is an inefficient finisher around the rim despite his 6'8" size and position as a power forward.  
- He lacks lateral quickness to defend NBA small forwards, making it difficult to contain speedy wing players.  
- His two ACL surgeries and past back issues have significantly reduced his athleticism and made him easier to defend.  
- He doesn’t possess the size or quickness to effectively play power forward or match up against NBA-level guards.
weaknesses.816:
- Lacks length, with arms that appear short for his height  
- Not a great leaper and struggles to explode off the floor  
- Inconsistent rebounder, averaging only about 7 boards per game in senior year  
- Fails to dominate inferior big men in the WCC despite his size and advantages  
- Struggles when double-teamed, hesitates, and often doesn’t dunk when he should
weaknesses.817:
- Too reliant on the 3-point shot, making him predictable and easier to guard  
- Needs to improve ball handling and attack the basket more frequently  
- Should reduce turnovers to enhance overall game control  
- Lacks lateral speed and first-step explosiveness, limiting defensive potential  
- Plays in a weak conference with limited high-level competition, though has NBA-caliber shooting
weaknesses.818:
- Inconsistent offensive performance, especially as a scorer, with struggles to make major impacts without physical advantages  
- Poor free throw shooting (59%) and lack of consistency in jump shot mechanics and range  
- Tends to rely on athleticism rather than skill, and often posts in space instead of contesting for position  
- Shows mental lapses, lazy passes, and turnovers; prone to losing focus and energy during games  
- Raw development with potential hindered by inconsistency, lack of post feel, and dependence on team dynamics and defensive coverage
weaknesses.819:
- Woodard's 6'7" frame requires superior ball-handling, which he currently lacks, limiting his ability to facilitate or create shots.  
- He struggles to get open on his own and relies on excessive space to shoot threes due to a slow, Draymond Green-like release.  
- While his three-point shooting improved, it comes at the cost of a slow, squatting release that needs to be faster for success.  
- He must transition to a rebounding and defensive power forward role, with potential to spread the floor only if he improves his jump shot speed—making this transition difficult against taller, stronger NBA forwards.  
- His free-throw shooting remains a concern, with only 58% and 64% success rates as a freshman and sophomore, respectively.
